Document in protocol: all section references in spec should reference by heading name, not section number, to prevent drift.\n\n### OQ-002: TurnLedger in pipeline API\n- **Status**: Resolved (Blocking)\n- **Resolution**: `TurnLedger` is NOT in the pipeline API (`superclaude.cli.pipeline.models`). It exists only in `superclaude.cli.sprint.models` (line 466). Portified pipelines that need budget tracking should import from `superclaude.cli.sprint.models.TurnLedger` directly. The pipeline-spec.md reference (line ~419) correctly shows this import path. No spec change needed.\n\n### OQ-003: --dry-run behavior\n- **Status**: Resolved (Blocking)\n- **Resolution**: `--dry-run` = execute Phases 0-2 only. Emit contracts (`portify-prerequisites.yaml`, `portify-analysis.yaml`), display analysis to user. No code generation (Phase 3) or integration (Phase 4). `--skip-integration` = generate code (Phase 3) but skip `main.py` wiring (Phase 4).\n\n### OQ-004: Integration schema\n- **Status**: Resolved (Blocking)\n- **Resolution**: `portify-integration.yaml` schema:\n  ```yaml\n  schema_version: \"1.0\"\n  phase: 4\n  status: \"passed\" | \"failed\"\n  timestamp: <ISO8601>\n  main_py_patched: true | false\n  command_registered: true | false\n  test_file_generated: true | false\n  smoke_test_passed: true | false\n  ```\n\n### OQ-005: batch_dynamic\n- **Status**: Resolved (Non-blocking)\n- **Resolution**: Set `batch_dynamic: false` always. Dynamic fan-out is unsupported. Remove the field from the contract schema; static batch sizes only.\n\n### OQ-006: refs/analysis-protocol.md exists\n- **Status**: Resolved (Non-blocking)\n- **Resolution**: Confirmed. File exists at `src/superclaude/skills/sc-cli-portify/refs/analysis-protocol.md` (216 lines). Contains: discovery checklist, step decomposition algorithm, classification rubric, output format template, common workflow patterns.\n\n### OQ-007: Approval gate mechanism\n- **Status**: Resolved (Blocking)\n- **Resolution**: Approval gates use TodoWrite checkpoint pattern:\n  1. Write contract artifact to disk\n  2. Mark TodoWrite task as \"awaiting review\"\n  3. Present summary to user with key decisions\n  4. User resumes by continuing (implicit approval) or provides overrides\n  5. Protocol reads contract on resume and validates completed phases\n\n### OQ-008: Default output path\n- **Status**: Resolved (Blocking)\n- **Resolution**: Default `--output` to `src/superclaude/cli/<derived_name>/` where `<derived_name>` is the kebab-case CLI name with `sc-` prefix and `-protocol` suffix stripped. This affects contract emission paths — all contracts are written to the output directory.\n\n### OQ-009: Test file location\n- **Status**: Resolved (Non-blocking)\n- **Resolution**: Structural tests go to `tests/` per project convention. File named `test_<cli_name>_structure.py`. This follows the existing project pattern where all tests reside in the `tests/` directory.\n\n### OQ-010: Step boundary algorithm\n- **Status**: Resolved (Blocking for Phase 3)\n- **Resolution**: The step boundary algorithm is already documented in `refs/analysis-protocol.md` under \"## Step Decomposition Algorithm\" → \"### Identify Step Boundaries\". The algorithm states a new Step starts when:\n  - A new artifact is produced\n  - A different agent takes over\n  - The execution mode changes (sequential → parallel)\n  - A quality gate must be evaluated\n  - The operation type changes (analysis → generation → validation)\n\n  No additional extraction needed — the algorithm is already explicitly documented.\n\n## Summary\n\n| OQ | Blocking? | Status |\n|----|-----------|--------|\n| OQ-001 | No | Resolved |\n| OQ-002 | Yes | Resolved — TurnLedger not in pipeline API |\n| OQ-003 | Yes | Resolved — dry-run = Phases 0-2 only |\n| OQ-004 | Yes | Resolved — schema defined |\n| OQ-005 | No | Resolved — batch_dynamic removed |\n| OQ-006 | No | Resolved — file confirmed |\n| OQ-007 | Yes | Resolved — TodoWrite checkpoint pattern |\n| OQ-008 | Yes | Resolved — default src/superclaude/cli/<name>/ |\n| OQ-009 | No | Resolved — tests/ directory |\n| OQ-010 | Yes | Resolved — already in analysis-protocol.md |\n\n**All 10 OQs resolved. The work produces a 5-phase portification pipeline that transforms SuperClaude skill/command/agent workflows into programmatic CLI pipelines with validated Python code generation.\n\n**Critical path**: Ref file remediation → Command/Protocol split → Contract schema definition → Phase 0-1 implementation → Phase 2 design → Phase 3-4 code generation and integration → Golden fixture validation.\n\n**Key architectural decisions**:\n- Contract-driven phase boundaries (YAML with versioned schemas) — phases are independently resumable\n- Live API snapshot with hash verification prevents drift-induced generation errors\n- Atomic generation with AST validation ensures no partial or broken output\n- 7 supported patterns only — explicit boundary prevents aspirational scope creep\n- Determinism validated against enumerated artifacts (`source_step_registry`, `step_mapping`, `module_plan`)\n\n**Open questions**: All 10 OQs must be resolved before Phase 2 implementation begins. OQ-002 (TurnLedger), OQ-003 (dry-run behavior), OQ-004 (integration schema), OQ-007 (approval gate mechanism), and OQ-008 (default output path) are blocking for design work.\n\n**Estimated delivery**: 14–19 working sessions (approximately 26–35 working days, assuming 1.5–2 sessions per day with review latency).\n\n---\n\n## Phase 1: Foundation and Prerequisite Remediation\n\n**Duration**: 2–3 sessions (~3–5 days)\n**Goal**: Eliminate known drift, establish the split architecture, resolve blocking open questions, define contract schema versioning policy\n\n### Milestone 1.1: Ref File API Alignment\n\nAddresses RISK-002 (ref file staleness) — the highest-priority prerequisite. No downstream work proceeds until refs match the live API.\n\n1. Read live `models.py` and `gates.py` to capture current API signatures\n2. Update `refs/pipeline-spec.md`:\n   - Fix tier casing: `tier=\"strict\"` → `enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\"`\n   - Align `GateCriteria` field names to match live dataclass\n   - Correct `SemanticCheck` contract: `Callable[[str], bool]` signature, not `tuple[bool, str]`\n3. Update `refs/code-templates.md`:\n   - Align all import paths to `superclaude.cli.pipeline.models` / `.gates`\n   - Fix gate template to use actual `GateCriteria` constructor fields\n   - Verify all 12 file templates reference correct base classes\n4. **Validation**: Diff ref files against live API — zero field name mismatches. Add stale-ref detector script that compares ref field names against live API signatures.\n\n**Requirements**: FR-048, NFR-003, NFR-018\n**Risks mitigated**: RISK-002\n\n### Milestone 1.2: Command/Protocol Split\n\nRuns in parallel with Milestone 1.1.\n\n1. Create `src/superclaude/commands/cli-portify.md` (thin command shim):\n   - Parse arguments: `--workflow`, `--name`, `--output`, `--dry-run`, `--skip-integration`\n   - Input validation with 6 error codes (MISSING_WORKFLOW, INVALID_PATH, AMBIGUOUS_PATH, OUTPUT_NOT_WRITABLE, NAME_COLLISION, DERIVATION_FAILED)\n   - Delegate to `Skill sc:cli-portify-protocol`\n3. Remove field or make constant. | No |\n| OQ-006 | Verify `refs/analysis-protocol.md` exists (confirmed). | No |\n| OQ-007 | Approval gates use TodoWrite checkpoint pattern: write contract → mark task \"awaiting review\" → user resumes. | **Yes** |\n| OQ-008 | Default `--output` to `src/superclaude/cli/<derived_name>/`. Blocking OQs (002, 003, 004, 007, 008, 010) have implementation decisions recorded in `decisions.yaml`.\n\n### Phase 1 Exit Criteria\n\n- No legacy structural conflicts remain\n- Protocol and command discoverable from both `src/` and `.claude/`\n- Ref files verified against live API with zero mismatches\n- All 10 OQs resolved and documented\n- `make verify-sync` passes for new protocol directory\n\n---\n\n## Phase 2: Protocol Core — Contract Infrastructure, Phase 0, and Phase 1\n\n**Duration**: 3–4 sessions (~5–8 days)\n**Goal**: Implement contract infrastructure, prerequisite scanning (Phase 0), and workflow analysis (Phase 1)\n**Dependencies**: Phase 1 complete, all blocking OQs resolved\n\n### Milestone 2.1: Contract Infrastructure and Schema Versioning\n\nContract schemas are defined as a prerequisite sub-task, then validated through real Phase 0 execution.\n\n1. Define contract schema versioning policy:\n   - Schema version field in contract header (e.g., `schema_version: \"1.0\"`)\n   - Backward-compatibility rules: additive fields allowed, field removal requires major version bump\n   - Version validation on contract read — reject incompatible versions with actionable error\n2. Define YAML contract common header schema:\n   ```yaml\n   schema_version: \"1.0\"\n   phase: <int>\n   status: \"passed\" | \"failed\" | \"skipped\"\n   timestamp: <ISO8601>\n   resume_checkpoint: <string>\n   validation_status: {blocking_passed: <int>, blocking_failed: <int>, advisory: [...]}\n   ```\n3. Define per-phase contract schemas (`portify-prerequisites.yaml`, `portify-analysis.yaml`, `portify-spec.yaml`, `portify-codegen.yaml`, `portify-integration.yaml`, return contract)\n4. Implement contract validation logic — next phase reads and validates incoming contract before proceeding\n5. Implement return contract structure (FR-043): status, failure_phase, failure_type, generated_files, counts, api_snapshot_hash, resume_command, warnings, phase_contracts\n6. Implement resume protocol (FR-052): read latest contract, validate completed phases, resume from failure point\n7. Validate resume semantics with a synthetic failure scenario before Phase 0 runs — proves contract mechanics work independently\n8. Define null-field policy: unreached fields explicitly set to `null`\n\n**Requirements**: FR-007, FR-008, FR-009, FR-043, FR-052, NFR-009, NFR-015, NFR-016\n\n### Milestone 2.2: Phase 0 — Prerequisites\n\n1. Validation\n\nGolden fixtures (SC-014):\n\n1. **Simple sequential skill**: Basic skill portification, all phases pass\n2. **Batched audit skill**: Parallel step groups, trailing gates, complex DAG\n3. **Adversarial multi-agent skill**: Multi-domain, high step count, N:1 mappings\n4. **Intentionally unsupported skill**: Contains dynamic code gen pattern — correctly aborts in Phase 0\n\nEach fixture validates:\n- Determinism (SC-002): repeated runs produce identical `source_step_registry`, `step_mapping`, and `module_plan`\n- All blocking checks pass or halt with correct failure state (SC-001)\n- deliberately wrong field names — blocks before Phase 1\n6. **API-drift fixture**: Modified API signatures between snapshot and generation — blocks at conformance check\n7. **Name collision fixture**: Pre-existing non-portified code at output path — aborts with correct error\n8. **Non-portified collision fixture**: Pre-existing human-written code at output path — never overwrites\n\n### Milestone 5.2: MCP Degradation Testing\n\nRuns in parallel with Milestone 5.1.\n\n1. Final `make lint` — no ruff violations in new/modified files\n\n### Phase 5 Exit Criteria\n\n- All 4 golden fixture tests pass\n- All 4 negative-path fixtures produce correct failure behavior\n- MCP degradation produces advisory warnings, not failures\n- Resume from each phase boundary works correctly\n- `make verify-sync` passes\n- `make test` passes\n- Old `sc-cli-portify/` removed\n\n---\n\n## Risk Assessment\n\n### High Severity\n\n| Risk | Phase Mitigated | Strategy |\n|------|----------------|----------|\n| RISK-001: Live API drift | Phase 1 (M1.1), Phase 4 (M4.1), Phase 5 (M5.1) | Snapshot with hash verification at every phase boundary; API-drift negative fixture validates detection |\n| RISK-002: Ref file staleness | **Phase 1 (M1.1)** — hard prerequisite | Update refs before any other work; stale-ref detector blocks generation; stale-ref negative fixture validates detection |\n| RISK-003: Unsupported pattern | Phase 2 (M2.2), Phase 5 (M5.1) | Early scan in Phase 0 before analysis investment; unsupported-skill golden fixture validates abort |\n\n### Medium Severity\n\n| Risk | Phase Mitigated | Strategy |\n|------|----------------|----------|\n| RISK-004: Step conservation violation | Phase 2 (M2.3), Phase 3 (M3.1) | Divergence detection and conservation equation enforcement at both phases |\n| RISK-005: Circular imports | Phase 4 (M4.1) | Strict generation order and import graph acyclicity check |\n| RISK-006: MCP unavailability | Phase 5 (M5.2) | Graceful degradation with advisory warnings; no hard MCP dependency outside local validation |\n| RISK-007: Low confidence classification | Phase 2 (M2.3) | User review with evidence and override capability at confidence < 0.7 |\n| RISK-008: Name collision | Phase 2 (M2.2), Phase 4 (M4.2), Phase 5 (M5.1) | Collision check at Phase 0 entry and Phase 4 integration; collision negative fixture validates abort |\n| RISK-009: Resume state corruption | Phase 5 (M5.3) | Re-validate contracts on resume; compare filesystem state and snapshot hash |\n\n### Low Severity\n\n| Risk | Phase Mitigated | Strategy |\n|------|----------------|----------|\n| RISK-010: Human review bottleneck | By design | `--dry-run` for preview (Phases 0-2 only); approval gates are intentional safety |\n| RISK-011: Template coverage gap | Phase 3 (M3.3) | Explicit coverage matrix with abort on gap |\n| RISK-012: Non-portified collision | Phase 4 (M4.3), Phase 5 (M5.1) | `portify-summary.md` marker detection; non-portified collision negative fixture validates behavior |\n\n**Highest-risk item requiring immediate attention**: RISK-002 (ref file staleness). The current `refs/pipeline-spec.md` and `refs/code-templates.md` have confirmed API drift from the live pipeline. This must be resolved first in Phase 1, Milestone 1.1.\n\n---\n\n## Resource Requirements and Dependencies\n\n### Internal Dependencies\n\n| Dependency | Status | Required By |\n|-----------|--------|-------------|\n| `superclaude.cli.pipeline.models` | Exists | Phase 2 (API snapshot) |\n| `superclaude.cli.pipeline.gates` | Exists | Phase 2 (API snapshot) |\n| `superclaude.cli.main` (Click app) | Exists | Phase 4 (integration) |\n| `tasklist.md` command/protocol pattern | Exists | Phase 1 (reference architecture) |\n| `refs/analysis-protocol.md` | Exists | Phase 2 (Phase 1 analysis) |\n\n### External Dependencies\n\n| Dependency | Required? | Fallback |\n|-----------|-----------|----------|\n| Python `ast` module | Hard | Standard library, always available |\n| `concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor` | Hard | Standard library, always available |\n| MCP: Auggie (codebase-retrieval) | Soft | Native file read tools |\n| MCP: Serena | Soft | Native grep/glob tools |\n| MCP: Sequential | Soft | Native Claude reasoning |\n| MCP: Context7 | Soft | WebSearch fallback |\n\n### Tooling\n\n- `uv` for all Python operations\n- `make verify-sync` for sync validation\n- `make test` / `uv run pytest` for test execution\n- `make lint` for code quality\n\n### Non-Code Prerequisites\n\n1. import graph | Phase 4 (M4.1) | Cross-file validation check |\n| SC-005: GateCriteria field match | Phase 4 (M4.1) | Per-file validation against snapshot |\n| SC-006: SemanticCheck signature correct | (M4.2) | Smoke test |\n| SC-009: Click command accessible | Phase 4 (M4.2) | Smoke test |\n| SC-010: Return contract always emitted | Phase 5 (M5.1) | All fixture runs including failures |\n| SC-011: |\n\n### Acceptance Gate\n\nDo not declare completion until all pass:\n\n1. **Golden fixtures**: all 4 positive scenarios produce correct output\n2. **Negative fixtures**: all 4 failure scenarios produce correct abort/error behavior\n3. **Determinism**: repeated runs produce identical enumerated artifacts\n4. **Safety**: collision and unsupported-path enforcement validated\n5. **Integration**: command visible and functional in CLI\n6. **Contracts**: emitted on both success and failure paths\n7. **Resume**: works correctly from every phase boundary\n8. **Sync**: `make verify-sync` includes new protocol structure\n9. **Tests**: `make test` passes with no regressions\n\n---\n\n## Timeline Summary\n\n| Phase | Milestones | Sessions | Days (approx.) | Cumulative Sessions | Cumulative Days `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/skills/sc-cli-portify/SKILL.md`\n```md\n---\nname: sc-cli-portify\ndescription: \"Port inference-based SuperClaude workflows (commands + skills + agents) into programmatic CLI pipelines with sprint-style supervised execution. Use this skill when you need to convert any multi-step workflow into a reliable, repeatable CLI runner that uses Python to control flow and Claude to fill in content. Triggers on: portify, CLI pipeline, programmatic workflow, convert skill to CLI, workflow runner, deterministic pipeline, move to code, make repeatable, ensure consistency.\"\nallowed-tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Edit, Write, Bash, TodoWrite, Task\n---\n\n> **DEPRECATED**: This skill has been replaced by `sc-cli-portify-protocol/`.\n> The protocol skill contains the full behavioral protocol with updated API-aligned refs.\n> This directory is scheduled for removal after Phase 5 validation.\n> Do NOT use this skill for new work — use `Skill sc:cli-portify-protocol` instead.\n\n# sc:cli-portify — Workflow-to-CLI Pipeline Compiler\n\nConvert inference-based SuperClaude workflows into programmatic CLI pipelines that ensure 100% completion and consistency between runs.\n\n## Purpose\n\nSuperClaude workflows implemented as commands + skills + agents + refs rely entirely on Claude's reasoning for orchestration. This works well for flexible, one-off tasks but breaks down for repeatable multi-step pipelines where you need:\n\n- **Deterministic control flow** — Python decides what runs when, not inference\n- **Formal artifact validation** — Gates check output quality programmatically\n- **Resume/retry** — Failed steps resume without re-running everything\n- **Live monitoring** — TUI shows progress, stall detection, diagnostics\n- **Budget economics** — Turn ledger tracks consumption across subprocesses\n- **Consistent behavior** — Same inputs produce structurally identical outputs every run\n\nThe existing `pipeline/` and `sprint/` modules already solve these problems. This skill bridges the gap by analyzing a workflow and generating a new CLI module that uses these proven patterns.\n\n## What Gets Generated\n\nA CLI subcommand package under `src/superclaude/cli/<name>/`:\n\n| File | Purpose | When Generated |\n|------|---------|----------------|\n| `models.py` | Domain types extending `PipelineConfig`, `Step`, `StepResult` | Always |\n| `gates.py` | `GateCriteria` + semantic check functions returning `tuple[bool, str]` | Always |\n| `prompts.py` | LLM contract builders per Claude-assisted step | Always |\n| `config.py` | CLI arg resolution, file discovery, config construction | Always |\n| `executor.py` | Synchronous supervisor loop with `ThreadPoolExecutor` for parallelism | Always |\n| `monitor.py` | NDJSON incremental output parser with domain signals | Always |\n| `process.py` | `ClaudeProcess` subclass with domain-specific prompt building | Always |\n| `tui.py` | Rich live dashboard with gate state machine | Always |\n| `logging_.py` | Dual JSONL + Markdown execution logging | Always |\n| `diagnostics.py` | `DiagnosticCollector`, `FailureClassifier`, `ReportGenerator` | Always |\n| `commands.py` | Click CLI group and subcommands | Always |\n| `__init__.py` | Package exports | Always |\n| `inventory.py` | Pure-programmatic file discovery/classification | If workflow has discovery steps |\n| `filtering.py` | Pure-programmatic inter-step data filtering | If workflow has filtering between steps |\n\nPlus integration patches for `main.py`.\n\n## Required Input\n\n```text\n/sc:cli-portify --workflow <skill-name-or-path> [--name <cli-name>] [--output <dir>]\n```\n\n| Argument | Required | Description |\n|----------|----------|-------------|\n| `--workflow` | Yes | Skill directory path or `sc-*` name to portify |\n| `--name` | No | CLI subcommand name (default: derived from workflow) |\n| `--output` | No | Output directory (default: `src/superclaude/cli/<name>/`) |\n| `--dry-run` | No | Show decomposition plan without generating code |\n| `--skip-integration` | No | Generate module only, skip main.py wiring |\n\n**STOP** if `--workflow` is not provided or the path doesn't resolve to a valid skill.\n\n## Behavioral Flow\n\nFour phases, each producing artifacts that feed the next. Present output to user for review after Phases 1 and 2 before continuing.\n\n### Phase 1: Workflow Analysis\n... [content continues exactly as in the protocol copy below]\n```\n\nNote: this file’s substantive body matches the protocol version except for the old frontmatter/deprecation wrapper.\n\n### `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/skills/sc-cli-portify/refs/analysis-protocol.md`\n```md\n# Workflow Analysis Protocol\n\nLoaded during Phase 1 of portification. Guides the systematic decomposition of an inference-based workflow into a pipeline specification.\n\n## Discovery Checklist\n\nWhen analyzing a target workflow, locate and read these components in order:\n\n### 1. Find the Command\n\nLook in `src/superclaude/commands/` for the matching command file. Extract:\n- Frontmatter: category, complexity, mcp-servers, personas\n- Behavioral Flow section: the high-level step sequence\n- Arguments and options\n- Boundaries (Will Do / Will Not Do)\n\n### 2. Find the Skill\n\nLook in `src/superclaude/skills/` for the matching skill directory. Read:\n- `SKILL.md`: The full protocol with step-by-step behavioral flow\n- `refs/`: All reference files (algorithms, templates, scoring protocols)\n- `rules/`: Validation rules and classification taxonomies\n- `templates/`: Output format templates (these become gate validation targets)\n- `scripts/`: Shell scripts for preprocessing (these become pure-programmatic steps)\n- Note which refs are loaded at which phase/wave\n\n### 3. Find the Agents\n\nSearch for agents referenced in the skill:\n- Look in `src/superclaude/agents/` for `.md` files\n- Note each agent's: triggers, tools, responsibilities, boundaries\n- Identify delegation patterns: parallel vs sequential, orchestrator vs worker\n\n### 4. Map the Data Flow\n\nTrace how data moves through the workflow:\n- What inputs does each step consume?\n- What artifacts does each step produce?\n- Which artifacts feed into downstream steps?\n- What is the final output?\n\n## Step Decomposition Algorithm\n\nFor each discrete operation in the workflow:\n\n### Identify Step Boundaries\n\nA new Step starts when:\n- A new artifact is produced\n- A different agent takes over\n- The execution mode changes (sequential → parallel)\n- A quality gate must be evaluated\n- The operation type changes (analysis → generation → validation)\n\n### Classify Each Step\n\nRate each step on the **programmatic spectrum**:\n\n| Classification | Characteristics | Implementation |\n|---------------|-----------------|----------------|\n| **Pure Programmatic** | Deterministic, formula-based, structural | Python function, no Claude |\n| **Programmatic + Validation** | Structural check with semantic judgment | Python primary, Claude for edge cases |\n| **Claude-Assisted** | Content generation within structured format | Claude subprocess with gate validation |\n| **Pure Inference** | Creative, subjective, context-dependent | Claude subprocess with light gate |\n\n### Map to Pipeline Primitives\n\n| Workflow Concept | Pipeline Primitive |\n|-----------------|-------------------|\n| Sequential step | `Step` in step list |\n| Parallel operations | `list[Step]` parallel group |\n| Quality gate | `GateCriteria` on the Step |\n| Agent delegation | Prompt in `prompts.py` |\n| Scoring formula | Python function in `gates.py` or separate module |\n| Status decision | `_determine_status()` in `executor.py` |\n| Input validation | `config.py` validation logic |\n| Output format | Frontmatter requirements in gate + prompt |\n| Retry on failure | `retry_limit` on Step |\n| Skip on success | Resume logic in executor |\n\n## Analysis Output Format\n\nPhase 1 produces `portify-analysis.md` with this structure:\n\n```markdown\n---\nsource_skill: <skill-name>\nsource_command: <command-name>\nstep_count: <N>\nparallel_groups: <N>\ngate_count: <N>\nagent_count: <N>\ncomplexity: simple|moderate|high\n---\n\n# Portification Analysis: {workflow-name}\n\n## Source Components\n\n| Component | Path | Lines | Purpose |\n|-----------|------|-------|---------|\n| Command | ... | ... | ... |\n| Skill | ... | ... | ... |\n| Agent: ... | ... | ... | ... |\n| Ref: ... | ... | ... | ... |\n\n## Step Graph\n\n### Step 1: {name}\n- **Type**: pure-programmatic | claude-assisted | hybrid\n- **Inputs**: [list of input artifacts]\n- **Output**: artifact-name.md\n- **Gate**: exempt | light | standard | strict\n- **Agent**: none | agent-name\n- **Parallel**: yes (with step N) | no\n- **Timeout**: Ns\n- **Retry**: N attempts\n- **Notes**: any special considerations\n\n### Step 2: {name}\n...\n\n## Parallel Groups\n\n| Group | Steps | Rationale |\n|-------|-------|-----------|\n| 1 | step-2, step-3 | Independent variant generation |\n\n## Gates Summary\n\n| Step | Tier | Frontmatter | Min Lines | Semantic Checks |\n|------|------|-------------|-----------|-----------------|\n| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |\n\n## Agent Delegation Map\n\n| Agent | Used In Steps | Parallel | Contract |\n|-------|--------------|----------|----------|\n| ... | ... | ... | ... |\n\n## Data Flow Diagram\n\n```\n[input] → step-1 → [artifact-1]\n                         ↓\n                    ┌─step-2─┐\n                    │         │ (parallel)\n                    └─step-3─┘\n                         ↓\n               [artifact-2, artifact-3]\n                         ↓\n                    step-4 → [final-output]\n```\n\n## Classification Summary\n\n| Category | Count | Steps |\n|----------|-------|-------|\n| Pure Programmatic | N | ... |\n| Claude-Assisted | N | ... |\n| Hybrid | N | ... |\n| Pure Inference | N | ... |\n\n## Recommendations\n\n- Things that should definitely be programmatic but are currently inference\n- Steps that could be split for better gate coverage\n- Opportunities for parallelization\n- Potential failure modes and how to handle them\n```\n\n## Common Workflow Patterns\n\n### Pattern: Multi-Agent Debate\nFound in: adversarial, spec-panel\n\nSteps:\n1. Parse inputs and validate (programmatic)\n2. Generate variants in parallel (Claude, parallel group)\n3. Diff analysis (Claude, single step with strict gate)\n4. Debate rounds (Claude, sequential with convergence gate)\n5. Score and select (hybrid — formulas are programmatic, rubrics are Claude)\n6. Generate plan (Claude, standard gate)\n7. Execute merge (Claude, strict gate with post-validation)\n\n### Pattern: Multi-Pass Audit\nFound in: cleanup-audit\n\nSteps:\n1. Discover files (programmatic)\n2. Surface scan with batching (Claude, batched parallel)\n3. Deep analysis per file (Claude, batched parallel)\n4. Cross-cutting comparison (Claude, single with strict gate)\n5. Consolidate findings (Claude, standard gate)\n6. Validate claims (Claude, sampling with standard gate)\n\n### Pattern: Extract-Transform-Validate\nFound in: roadmap, tasklist\n\nSteps:\n1. Extract requirements (Claude, strict gate on frontmatter)\n2. Generate artifacts (Claude, parallel for multiple agents)\n3. Compare/analyze (Claude, standard gate)\n4. Synthesize final (Claude, strict gate)\n5. Validate structure (programmatic)\n\n### Pattern: Iterative Refinement\nFound in: implement, improve\n\nSteps:\n1. Analyze current state (Claude, standard gate)\n2. Generate plan (Claude, standard gate)\n3. Execute changes (Claude, strict gate)\n4. Validate changes (programmatic + Claude)\n5. Iterate if quality threshold not met (retry loop)\n```\n\n### `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/skills/sc-cli-portify/refs/pipeline-spec.md`\nThis file is long. I read it successfully in chunks. Full content captured below as one combined document:\n\n```md\n# Pipeline Specification Reference\n\nLoaded during Phase 2 of portification. This ref provides the detailed specification patterns for converting a workflow analysis into concrete code designs.\n\n## Step Graph Design\n\n### Step Definition Pattern\n\nEvery workflow operation that produces an artifact maps to a `Step`:\n\n```python\nStep(\n    id=\"step-id\",              # kebab-case, unique within pipeline\n    prompt=build_X_prompt(),   # Function from prompts.py\n    output_file=\"artifact.md\", # Relative to work_dir\n    gate=STEP_GATE,            # From gates.py\n    gate_mode=GateMode.BLOCKING,  # BLOCKING (default) or TRAILING\n    timeout_seconds=600,       # Per-step timeout\n    inputs=[\"dep1.md\"],        # Files this step reads\n    retry_limit=1,             # How many retries on gate failure\n    model=None,                # None = use default from config\n)\n```\n\n### Gate Mode Selection\n\nChoose `GateMode` based on step criticality:\n- `BLOCKING` (default): Pipeline halts if gate fails. Use for steps whose output is consumed by downstream steps.\n- `TRAILING`: Gate evaluates asynchronously; pipeline continues. Use for quality checks that don't affect downstream data flow.\n\nRule of thumb from `resolve_gate_mode()`:\n- Release scope → always BLOCKING\n- Milestone scope → configurable\n- Task scope → TRAILING when `grace_period > 0`\n\n### Parallel Group Pattern\n\nSteps that can run concurrently are wrapped in a list:\n\n```python\nsteps = [\n    single_step_1,\n    [parallel_step_a, parallel_step_b],  # Run concurrently\n    single_step_2,                        # Waits for parallel group\n]\n```\n\nThe generic `execute_pipeline()` handles thread coordination. If using sprint-style custom executor, implement your own parallel dispatch.\n\n### Dependency Rules\n\n- Steps consume outputs of prior steps via `inputs` list\n- A step can only reference outputs from steps that precede it in the graph\n- Parallel steps within a group must not depend on each other\n- Resume logic skips steps whose output already passes their gate\n\n## Model Design Patterns\n\n### Config Model\n\nAlways extend `PipelineConfig`:\n\n```python\n@dataclass\nclass MyConfig(PipelineConfig):\n    \"\"\"Pipeline-specific configuration.\"\"\"\n    # Required: identify the input\n    source_path: Path\n\n    # Optional: control behavior\n    depth: str = \"standard\"       # quick|standard|deep\n\n    # Computed paths\n    @property\n    def results_dir(self) -> Path:\n        return self.work_dir / \"results\"\n\n    @property\n    def output_file(self, step_id: str) -> Path:\n        return self.work_dir / f\"{step_id}-output.md\"\n```\n\n### Status Enum\n\nSprint-style pipelines need richer status than generic `StepStatus`:\n\n```python\nclass StepPhaseStatus(Enum):\n    PENDING = \"pending\"\n    RUNNING = \"running\"\n    PASS = \"pass\"\n    PASS_NO_SIGNAL = \"pass_no_signal\"    # Completed but no structured report\n    PASS_NO_REPORT = \"pass_no_report\"    # Output exists but no result file\n    INCOMPLETE = \"incomplete\"             # Budget exhausted\n    HALT = \"halt\"                         # Step recommends stopping\n    TIMEOUT = \"timeout\"                   # Hard timeout exceeded\n    ERROR = \"error\"                       # Process crash\n    SKIPPED = \"skipped\"                   # Skipped by config\n```\n\n### Result Model\n\nExtend `StepResult` with domain telemetry:\n\n```python\n@dataclass\nclass MyStepResult(StepResult):\n    exit_code: int | None = None\n    started_at: float | None = None\n    finished_at: float | None = None\n    output_bytes: int = 0\n    error_bytes: int = 0\n    # Domain-specific fields:\n    artifacts_produced: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n    gate_details: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n```\n\n### Monitor State\n\nDomain-specific signals extracted from NDJSON output:\n\n```python\n@dataclass\nclass MyMonitorState:\n    output_bytes: int = 0\n    last_growth_time: float = 0.0\n    events_received: int = 0\n    lines_total: int = 0\n    growth_rate_bps: float = 0.0\n    stall_seconds: float = 0.0\n    # Domain-specific signals:\n    last_step_id: str | None = None\n    current_artifact: str | None = None\n    files_changed: set = field(default_factory=set)\n```\n\n## Gate Design Patterns\n\n### Gate Criteria Tiers\n\n```python\n# Exempt: always passes (for optional/informational steps)\nEXEMPT_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[],\n    min_lines=0,\n    enforcement_tier=\"EXEMPT\",\n)\n\n# Light: file exists and non-empty\nLIGHT_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[],\n    min_lines=0,\n    enforcement_tier=\"LIGHT\",\n)\n\n# Standard: structural validation\nSTANDARD_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"title\", \"status\"],\n    min_lines=50,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STANDARD\",\n)\n\n# Strict: structural + semantic validation\nSTRICT_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"title\", \"status\", \"convergence_score\"],\n    min_lines=100,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n    semantic_checks=[\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"no_heading_gaps\",\n            check_fn=_no_heading_gaps,\n            failure_message=\"Heading hierarchy has gaps (e.g. H1→H3 without H2)\",\n        ),\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"refs_resolve\",\n            check_fn=_cross_refs_resolve,\n            failure_message=\"Cross-references do not resolve to valid targets\",\n        ),\n    ],\n)\n```\n\n### Semantic Check Functions\n\nSemantic checks are pure functions matching the `Callable[[str], bool]` signature — they take file content and return a boolean:\n\n```python\ndef _no_heading_gaps(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Verify heading hierarchy has no gaps (H1->H3 without H2).\"\"\"\n    # ... implementation ...\n    return True  # or False on failure\n\ndef _has_required_sections(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Verify all required sections are present.\"\"\"\n    required = {\"## Summary\", \"## Findings\", \"## Recommendations\"}\n    present = {line.strip() for line in content.splitlines() if line.startswith(\"## \")}\n    missing = required - present\n    return len(missing) == 0\n```\n\n### Frontmatter Validation\n\nThe shared `gate_passed()` function handles YAML frontmatter checks. Steps that produce markdown with frontmatter get automatic validation:\n\n```yaml\n---\ntitle: Diff Analysis\nstatus: complete\ndiff_points: 12\nconvergence_ready: true\n---\n```\n\n## Prompt Design Patterns\n\n### Prompt Builder Structure\n\nEach step's prompt is a function that takes inputs and returns a complete prompt string:\n\n```python\ndef build_analysis_prompt(source_path: Path, config: MyConfig) -> str:\n    return f\"\"\"Read the source file and produce a structured analysis.\n\nSource: {source_path}\nDepth: {config.depth}\n\n## Required Output Format\n\nWrite your analysis to the output file with this structure:\n\n```yaml\n---\ntitle: Analysis Report\nstatus: complete\nfinding_count: <integer>\n---\n```\n\n## Sections Required\n\n### Summary\nBrief overview of findings.\n\n### Findings\nNumbered list of findings with evidence.\n\n### Recommendations\nActionable next steps.\n\n## Machine-Readable Markers\n\nAt the end of your output, include:\n```\nEXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\n```\nor\n```\nEXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\n```\n\"\"\"\n```\n\n### Key Prompt Rules\n\n1. **Specify output file format** — Tell Claude exactly what frontmatter fields to include\n2. **Require machine-readable markers** — `EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE|HALT` for status determination\n3. **Embed input content when small** — Under ~50KB, embed directly in prompt; otherwise use `--file` args\n4. **Be explicit about sections** — Name every required section; don't leave structure to inference\n5. **Include depth/focus constraints** — If the step has configurable depth, encode it in the prompt\n\n### Input Embedding Pattern\n\n```python\ndef _embed_inputs(prompt: str, inputs: list[Path], config: MyConfig) -> tuple[str, list[str]]:\n    \"\"\"Embed small input files directly into prompt, return file args for large ones.\"\"\"\n    file_args = []\n    for input_path in inputs:\n        full_path = config.work_dir / input_path\n        if full_path.stat().st_size < 50_000:\n            content = full_path.read_text()\n            prompt += f\"\\n\\n---\\n## Input: {input_path}\\n\\n{content}\"\n        else:\n            file_args.append(str(full_path))\n    return prompt, file_args\n```\n\n## Executor Design Patterns\n\n### Sprint-Style Supervisor Loop\n\nThe sprint executor pattern provides supervised execution with monitoring. IMPORTANT: Sprint uses synchronous execution with `threading` for parallelism, NOT `async/await`. Use `time.sleep()` polling loops and `concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor` for batch dispatch.\n\n```python\ndef execute_pipeline(config: MyConfig) -> MyResult:\n    # 1. Pre-flight checks\n    verify_claude_binary()\n\n    # 2. Setup infrastructure\n    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n    logger = MyLogger(config)\n    tui = MyTUI(config)\n    monitor = OutputMonitor()\n    result = MyResult(config=config)\n    ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=config.max_turns)\n\n    # 3. Start TUI\n    tui.start()\n\n    try:\n        for step in config.steps:\n            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                break\n\n            # 4. Reset monitor for this step\n            monitor.reset(config.output_file(step))\n            monitor.start()\n\n            # 5. Launch Claude subprocess\n            process = ClaudeProcess(config, step)\n            process.start()\n\n            # 6. Supervision loop\n            while process.is_running():\n                if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                    process.stop()\n                    break\n\n                state = monitor.get_state()\n\n                # Stall detection\n                if state.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout:\n                    process.stop()\n                    break\n\n                # TUI update\n                tui.update(step, state)\n                time.sleep(0.5)\n\n            # 7. Classify result\n            monitor.stop()\n            status = determine_status(process.exit_code, step, config)\n\n            # 8. Record and decide\n            step_result = build_result(step, status, monitor.state)\n            result.add(step_result)\n            logger.write_step_result(step_result)\n\n            if status not in (PASS, PASS_NO_SIGNAL):\n                # Collect diagnostics and halt\n                diagnostics = collect_diagnostics(step_result, monitor.state)\n                write_diagnostic_report(diagnostics, config)\n                result.outcome = HALTED\n                break\n\n    finally:\n        tui.stop()\n        monitor.stop()\n        signal_handler.restore()\n\n    return result\n```\n\n### Status Classification\n\nMap every possible exit condition to a deterministic status:\n\n```python\ndef determine_status(exit_code, step, config):\n    # 1. Hard failures\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return TIMEOUT\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        return ERROR\n\n    # 2. Check for structured result file\n    result_path = config.result_file(step)\n    if result_path.exists():\n        content = result_path.read_text()\n        if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in content:\n            return HALT\n        if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in content:\n            return PASS\n        return PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n\n    # 3. Check for output without result\n    output_path = config.output_file(step)\n    if output_path.exists():\n        if detect_error_max_turns(output_path):\n            return INCOMPLETE\n        return PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n    # 4. No output at all\n    return ERROR\n```\n\n## Integration Patterns\n\n### main.py Registration\n\n```python\n# In src/superclaude/cli/main.py\nfrom superclaude.cli.my_pipeline import my_pipeline_group\napp.add_command(my_pipeline_group)\n```\n\n### Click Command Group\n\n```python\n@click.group(\"my-pipeline\")\ndef my_pipeline_group():\n    \"\"\"My pipeline description.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n@my_pipeline_group.command(\"run\")\n@click.argument(\"source\")\n@click.option(\"--depth\", default=\"standard\", type=click.Choice([\"quick\", \"standard\", \"deep\"]))\n@click.option(\"--dry-run\", is_flag=True)\n@click.option(\"--max-turns\", default=25, type=int)\n@click.option(\"--model\", default=None)\n@click.option(\"--debug\", is_flag=True)\ndef run(source, depth, dry_run, max_turns, model, debug):\n    \"\"\"Execute the pipeline.\"\"\"\n    config = load_config(source, depth=depth, dry_run=dry_run, ...)\n    result = execute_pipeline(config)\n    sys.exit(0 if result.success else 1)\n```\n\n## Turn Budget Economics (from unified-audit-gating)\n\nFor pipelines with multiple Claude subprocesses, use `TurnLedger` to manage budget:\n\n```python\nfrom superclaude.cli.sprint.models import TurnLedger\n\n@dataclass\nclass MyConfig(PipelineConfig):\n    max_turns: int = 200\n    min_launch_allocation: int = 10\n    min_remediation_budget: int = 5\n\n# In executor:\nledger = TurnLedger(\n    initial_budget=config.max_turns,\n    minimum_allocation=config.min_launch_allocation,\n    minimum_remediation_budget=config.min_remediation_budget,\n)\n\n# Before launching a step:\nif not ledger.can_launch():\n    result.outcome = HALTED\n    result.halt_reason = \"Budget exhausted\"\n    break\n\n# After step completes, count actual turns used:\nactual_turns = count_turns_from_output(output_path)\nledger.debit(actual_turns)\n\n# If step used fewer turns than allocated, credit back:\nif actual_turns < allocated:\n    ledger.credit(allocated - actual_turns)\n```\n\n## Subprocess Isolation Pattern\n\nFor pipelines spawning child Claude sessions, use the 4-layer isolation model:\n\n```python\ndef setup_isolation(work_dir: Path) -> dict[str, str]:\n    \"\"\"Create isolated environment for child Claude subprocess.\"\"\"\n    env = os.environ.copy()\n\n    # Layer 1: Scoped work directory\n    env[\"CLAUDE_WORK_DIR\"] = str(work_dir)\n\n    # Layer 2: Git ceiling (prevent escaping work_dir)\n    env[\"GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES\"] = str(work_dir.parent)\n\n    # Layer 3: Isolated plugin directory\n    plugin_dir = work_dir / \".claude-plugins\"\n    plugin_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n    env[\"CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DIR\"] = str(plugin_dir)\n\n    # Layer 4: Isolated settings\n    settings_dir = work_dir / \".claude-settings\"\n    settings_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n    env[\"CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR\"] = str(settings_dir)\n\n    return env\n```\n\n## Context Injection Pattern\n\nPrior step results feed into subsequent prompts as compressed context:\n\n```python\ndef build_step_prompt_with_context(step, prior_results: list[StepResult]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Build prompt with prior step context injected.\"\"\"\n    base_prompt = step.prompt\n\n    if not prior_results:\n        return base_prompt\n\n    context_block = \"## Prior Step Context\\n\\n\"\n    for result in prior_results[-3:]:  # Keep recent, compress older\n        context_block += result.to_context_summary(verbose=(result == prior_results[-1]))\n        context_block += \"\\n\\n\"\n\n    return f\"{base_prompt}\\n\\n{context_block}\"\n```\n\n## Resume-First Failure Output Pattern\n\nOn halt, generate actionable resume information:\n\n```python\n@dataclass\nclass MyResult:\n    # ... other fields ...\n\n    def resume_command(self) -> str | None:\n        \"\"\"Generate CLI command to resume from failure point.\"\"\"\n        if not self.halt_step:\n            return None\n        remaining = [r.step.id for r in self.step_results\n                     if r.status == MyStatus.PENDING]\n        return (\n            f\"superclaude {CLI_NAME} run \"\n            f\"--resume --start {self.halt_step} \"\n            f\"--max-turns {self.suggested_resume_budget}\"\n        )\n\n    @property\n    def suggested_resume_budget(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Estimate turns needed to complete remaining work.\"\"\"\n        remaining_steps = sum(1 for r in self.step_results\n                              if r.status in (MyStatus.PENDING, MyStatus.INCOMPLETE))\n        return remaining_steps * 25  # Default allocation per step\n```\n\n## Pure-Programmatic Step Execution\n\nNot all steps need Claude. Pure-programmatic steps run as direct Python function calls:\n\n```python\ndef execute_step(step, config):\n    \"\"\"Route step to appropriate executor.\"\"\"\n    if step.prompt == \"\":\n        # Pure programmatic — run directly\n        return run_programmatic_step(step, config)\n    else:\n        # Claude-assisted — launch subprocess\n        return run_claude_step(step, config)\n\ndef run_programmatic_step(step, config) -> StepResult:\n    \"\"\"Execute a pure-programmatic step without Claude.\"\"\"\n    started = time.time()\n    try:\n        # Call the step's implementation function\n        PROGRAMMATIC_RUNNERS[step.id](config)\n        return StepResult(step=step, status=StepStatus.PASS,\n                         started_at=started, finished_at=time.time())\n    except Exception as e:\n        return StepResult(step=step, status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n                         gate_failure_reason=str(e),\n                         started_at=started, finished_at=time.time())\n```\n\n## Gate Display State Machine (for TUI)\n\nModel gate states as explicit transitions for richer UX:\n\n```python\nclass GateDisplayState(Enum):\n    NONE = \"none\"\n    CHECKING = \"checking\"\n    PASS = \"pass\"\n    FAIL_DEFERRED = \"fail_deferred\"\n    REMEDIATING = \"remediating\"\n    REMEDIATED = \"remediated\"\n    HALT = \"halt\"\n\n    @property\n    def icon(self) -> str:\n        return {\n            \"none\": \" \", \"checking\": \"...\",\n            \"pass\": \"ok\", \"fail_deferred\": \"!\",\n            \"remediating\": \"~\", \"remediated\": \"ok\",\n            \"halt\": \"X\",\n        }[self.value]\n```\n```\n\n### `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/skills/sc-cli-portify/refs/code-templates.md`\nCombined from chunked reads:\n\n```md\n# Code Templates Reference\n\nLoaded during Phase 3 of portification. Contains the actual file templates used to generate each module in the CLI pipeline package.\n\nThese templates are parameterized. Placeholders use `{PARAM}` syntax. The skill fills these based on the pipeline specification from Phase 2.\n\n## File Generation Order\n\nGenerate in this order to respect dependencies:\n\n1. `models.py` — No internal deps\n2. `gates.py` — Imports from models\n3. `prompts.py` — Imports from models\n4. `config.py` — Imports from models\n5. `monitor.py` — Imports from models\n6. `process.py` — Imports from models, config; extends pipeline.process\n7. `executor.py` — Imports from everything above\n8. `tui.py` — Imports from models\n9. `logging_.py` — Imports from models\n10. `diagnostics.py` — Imports from models\n11. `commands.py` — Imports from config, executor\n12. `__init__.py` — Re-exports\n\n## Template: `__init__.py`\n\n```python\n\"\"\"CLI subcommand package for {PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline.\n\nPortified from: {SOURCE_SKILL}\nGenerated by: sc-cli-portify\n\nThis module provides the `superclaude {CLI_NAME}` command group.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom .commands import {GROUP_NAME}_group\n\n__all__ = [\"{GROUP_NAME}_group\"]\n```\n\n## Template: `models.py`\n\n```python\n\"\"\"Domain models for {PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline.\n\nExtends shared pipeline base types with {PIPELINE_NAME}-specific\nstatus tracking, configuration, and result telemetry.\n\nSource workflow: {SOURCE_SKILL}\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport time\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom superclaude.cli.pipeline.models import (\n    GateCriteria,\n    GateMode,\n    PipelineConfig,\n    Step,\n    StepResult,\n    StepStatus,\n)\n\n\n# --- Enums ---\n\nclass {PREFIX}Status(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Step-level status for {PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline.\n\n    Extends generic StepStatus with domain-specific states.\n    \"\"\"\n    PENDING = \"pending\"\n    RUNNING = \"running\"\n    PASS = \"pass\"\n    PASS_NO_SIGNAL = \"pass_no_signal\"\n    PASS_NO_REPORT = \"pass_no_report\"\n    INCOMPLETE = \"incomplete\"\n    HALT = \"halt\"\n    TIMEOUT = \"timeout\"\n    ERROR = \"error\"\n    SKIPPED = \"skipped\"\n\n\nclass {PREFIX}Outcome(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Aggregate pipeline outcome.\"\"\"\n    SUCCESS = \"success\"\n    HALTED = \"halted\"\n    INTERRUPTED = \"interrupted\"\n    ERROR = \"error\"\n\n\n{DOMAIN_ENUMS}\n\n\n# --- Configuration ---\n\n@dataclass\nclass {PREFIX}Config(PipelineConfig):\n    \"\"\"{PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline configuration.\n\n    Extends PipelineConfig with {PIPELINE_NAME}-specific settings.\n    \"\"\"\n    {CONFIG_FIELDS}\n\n    def __post_init__(self):\n        \"\"\"Ensure work_dir is set from pipeline-specific paths.\"\"\"\n        if not self.work_dir:\n            self.work_dir = self.{PRIMARY_PATH_FIELD}\n        self.work_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    {CONFIG_PROPERTIES}\n\n\n# --- Step Specialization ---\n\n@dataclass\nclass {PREFIX}Step(Step):\n    \"\"\"{PIPELINE_NAME}-specific step with domain metadata.\"\"\"\n    {STEP_FIELDS}\n\n\n# --- Result Types ---\n\n@dataclass\nclass {PREFIX}StepResult(StepResult):\n    \"\"\"Result for a single {PIPELINE_NAME} step.\n\n    Extends StepResult with execution telemetry.\n    \"\"\"\n    status: {PREFIX}Status = {PREFIX}Status.PENDING\n    exit_code: int | None = None\n    started_at: float | None = None\n    finished_at: float | None = None\n    output_bytes: int = 0\n    error_bytes: int = 0\n    {RESULT_FIELDS}\n\n    @property\n    def duration_seconds(self) -> float:\n        if self.started_at and self.finished_at:\n            return self.finished_at - self.started_at\n        return 0.0\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass {PREFIX}Result:\n    \"\"\"Aggregate result for the entire {PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline.\"\"\"\n    config: {PREFIX}Config\n    step_results: list[{PREFIX}StepResult] = field(default_factory=list)\n    outcome: {PREFIX}Outcome = {PREFIX}Outcome.SUCCESS\n    started_at: float = field(default_factory=time.time)\n    finished_at: float | None = None\n    halt_step: str | None = None\n\n    @property\n    def duration_seconds(self) -> float:\n        end = self.finished_at or time.time()\n        return end - self.started_at\n\n    @property\n    def steps_passed(self) -> int:\n        return sum(1 for r in self.step_results\n                   if r.status in ({PREFIX}Status.PASS, {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_SIGNAL))\n\n    @property\n    def steps_failed(self) -> int:\n        return sum(1 for r in self.step_results\n                   if r.status in ({PREFIX}Status.HALT, {PREFIX}Status.ERROR, {PREFIX}Status.TIMEOUT))\n\n    def resume_command(self) -> str | None:\n        \"\"\"Generate CLI command to resume from the failed step.\"\"\"\n        if self.halt_step:\n            return f\"superclaude {CLI_NAME} run --resume --start {self.halt_step}\"\n        return None\n\n\n# --- Monitor State ---\n\n@dataclass\nclass {PREFIX}MonitorState:\n    \"\"\"Live execution state fed by the output monitor.\"\"\"\n    output_bytes: int = 0\n    output_bytes_prev: int = 0\n    last_growth_time: float = 0.0\n    last_event_time: float = 0.0\n    step_started_at: float = 0.0\n    events_received: int = 0\n    lines_total: int = 0\n    growth_rate_bps: float = 0.0\n    stall_seconds: float = 0.0\n    {MONITOR_FIELDS}\n\n    @property\n    def stall_status(self) -> str:\n        if self.events_received == 0:\n            elapsed = time.time() - self.step_started_at if self.step_started_at else 0\n            return \"waiting...\" if elapsed < 30 else \"STALLED\"\n        if self.stall_seconds > 120:\n            return \"STALLED\"\n        if self.stall_seconds > 30:\n            return \"thinking...\"\n        return \"active\"\n\n    @property\n    def output_size_display(self) -> str:\n        if self.output_bytes < 1024:\n            return f\"{self.output_bytes}B\"\n        return f\"{self.output_bytes / 1024:.1f}KB\"\n```\n\n## Template: `gates.py`\n\n```python\n\"\"\"Gate criteria and semantic checks for {PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline.\n\nEach step's output must pass its gate before the pipeline continues.\nGates enforce structural and semantic quality programmatically,\nremoving this responsibility from inference.\n\nSource workflow: {SOURCE_SKILL}\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport re\nimport yaml\n\nfrom superclaude.cli.pipeline.models import GateCriteria, SemanticCheck\n\n\n# --- Semantic Check Functions ---\n# Pure functions: Callable[[str], bool]  (content: str) -> bool\n\n{SEMANTIC_CHECKS}\n\n\n# --- Gate Definitions ---\n\n{GATE_DEFINITIONS}\n\n# Collected for iteration\nALL_GATES: dict[str, GateCriteria] = {{\n    {GATE_DICT_ENTRIES}\n}}\n```\n\n## Template: `prompts.py`\n\n```python\n\"\"\"Prompt builders for {PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline.\n\nEach function constructs the LLM contract for one pipeline step.\nThe prompt specifies:\n- What context/inputs to read\n- What output format is required\n- What machine-readable markers must appear\n- What quality expectations apply\n\nSource workflow: {SOURCE_SKILL}\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom .models import {PREFIX}Config\n\n{DEPTH_INSTRUCTIONS}\n\n{PROMPT_BUILDERS}\n```\n\n## Template: `config.py`\n\n```python\n\"\"\"Configuration loading and validation for {PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline.\n\nHandles CLI argument resolution, file discovery, and config construction.\n\nSource workflow: {SOURCE_SKILL}\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom .models import {PREFIX}Config\n\n{CONFIG_CONSTANTS}\n\n\ndef load_{CLI_NAME}_config(\n    {CONFIG_PARAMS}\n) -> {PREFIX}Config:\n    \"\"\"Construct and validate pipeline configuration from CLI arguments.\"\"\"\n    {CONFIG_BODY}\n    return config\n\n\n{CONFIG_HELPERS}\n```\n\n## Template: `executor.py`\n\n```python\n\"\"\"Sprint-style supervised executor for {PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline.\n\nControls the execution loop with:\n- Per-step live monitoring\n- TUI refresh at ~2Hz\n- Stall detection and watchdog\n- Signal-aware graceful shutdown\n- Deterministic status classification\n- Failure diagnostics\n\nIMPORTANT: Use synchronous execution with threading, NOT async/await.\nSprint uses time.sleep() polling loops and concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor\nfor parallel batch dispatch. Do not use asyncio.\n\nSource workflow: {SOURCE_SKILL}\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport shutil\nimport signal\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom superclaude.cli.pipeline.gates import gate_passed\n\nfrom .config import load_{CLI_NAME}_config\nfrom .diagnostics import DiagnosticCollector, FailureClassifier, ReportGenerator\nfrom .logging_ import {PREFIX}Logger\nfrom .models import (\n    {PREFIX}Config,\n    {PREFIX}MonitorState,\n    {PREFIX}Outcome,\n    {PREFIX}Result,\n    {PREFIX}Status,\n    {PREFIX}StepResult,\n)\nfrom .monitor import OutputMonitor, detect_error_max_turns\nfrom .process import {PREFIX}Process, SignalHandler\nfrom .tui import {PREFIX}TUI\n\n\ndef execute_{CLI_NAME}(config: {PREFIX}Config) -> {PREFIX}Result:\n    \"\"\"Execute the {PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline with supervised monitoring.\"\"\"\n\n    # Pre-flight\n    if not shutil.which(\"claude\"):\n        print(\"Error: 'claude' binary not found in PATH\", file=sys.stderr)\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Setup infrastructure\n    handler = SignalHandler()\n    logger = {PREFIX}Logger(config)\n    tui = {PREFIX}TUI(config)\n    monitor = OutputMonitor()\n    result = {PREFIX}Result(config=config)\n\n    logger.write_header()\n    tui.start()\n\n    try:\n        for step in _build_steps(config):\n            if handler.shutdown_requested:\n                result.outcome = {PREFIX}Outcome.INTERRUPTED\n                break\n\n            # Reset monitor for this step's output\n            output_path = config.work_dir / step.output_file\n            monitor.reset(output_path)\n            monitor.start()\n\n            # Launch subprocess\n            process = {PREFIX}Process(config, step)\n            logger.write_step_start(step)\n            deadline = time.monotonic() + step.timeout_seconds\n            process.start()\n\n            # Supervision loop\n            while process.is_running():\n                if handler.shutdown_requested:\n                    process.stop()\n                    break\n\n                if time.monotonic() > deadline:\n                    process.stop()\n                    break\n\n                state = monitor.get_state()\n\n                # Stall watchdog\n                if (config.stall_timeout\n                        and state.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout\n                        and state.events_received > 0):\n                    if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n                        process.stop()\n                        break\n\n                try:\n                    tui.update(step, state)\n                except Exception:\n                    pass\n\n                time.sleep(0.5)\n\n            # Classify result\n            exit_code = process.wait()\n            monitor.stop()\n            status = _determine_status(exit_code, step, config)\n\n            step_result = {PREFIX}StepResult(\n                step=step,\n                status=status,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                started_at=process.started_at,\n                finished_at=time.time(),\n                output_bytes=monitor.state.output_bytes,\n            )\n            result.step_results.append(step_result)\n            logger.write_step_result(step_result)\n\n            # Gate check for passing steps\n            if status == {PREFIX}Status.PASS and step.gate:\n                output_content = (config.work_dir / step.output_file).read_text()\n                passed, reason = gate_passed(output_content, step.gate)\n                if not passed:\n                    step_result.status = {PREFIX}Status.HALT\n                    step_result.gate_failure_reason = reason\n                    status = {PREFIX}Status.HALT\n\n            # Handle failures\n            if status not in ({PREFIX}Status.PASS, {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_SIGNAL, {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_REPORT):\n                collector = DiagnosticCollector()\n                bundle = collector.collect(step_result, monitor.state, config)\n                classifier = FailureClassifier()\n                category = classifier.classify(bundle)\n                reporter = ReportGenerator()\n                reporter.write_report(bundle, category, config)\n\n                result.outcome = {PREFIX}Outcome.HALTED\n                result.halt_step = step.id\n                break\n\n        # Finalize\n        if result.outcome == {PREFIX}Outcome.SUCCESS:\n            all_passed = all(\n                r.status in ({PREFIX}Status.PASS, {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_SIGNAL, {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_REPORT, {PREFIX}Status.SKIPPED)\n                for r in result.step_results\n            )\n            if not all_passed:\n                result.outcome = {PREFIX}Outcome.ERROR\n\n        result.finished_at = time.time()\n        logger.write_summary(result)\n\n    finally:\n        tui.stop()\n        monitor.stop()\n        handler.restore()\n\n    return result\n\n\n{BUILD_STEPS_FUNCTION}\n\n\ndef _determine_status(\n    exit_code: int | None,\n    step,\n    config: {PREFIX}Config,\n) -> {PREFIX}Status:\n    \"\"\"Classify step outcome from exit code and output artifacts.\"\"\"\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return {PREFIX}Status.TIMEOUT\n    if exit_code and exit_code != 0:\n        return {PREFIX}Status.ERROR\n\n    result_path = config.work_dir / f\"{step.id}-result.md\"\n    output_path = config.work_dir / step.output_file\n\n    if result_path.exists():\n        content = result_path.read_text()\n        if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in content:\n            return {PREFIX}Status.HALT\n        if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in content:\n            return {PREFIX}Status.PASS\n        if \"status: PASS\" in content.lower():\n            return {PREFIX}Status.PASS\n        if \"status: FAIL\" in content.lower():\n            return {PREFIX}Status.HALT\n        return {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n\n    if output_path.exists():\n        if detect_error_max_turns(output_path):\n            return {PREFIX}Status.INCOMPLETE\n        return {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n    return {PREFIX}Status.ERROR\n```\n\n## Template: `monitor.py`\n\nFollow the sprint monitor pattern exactly — incremental NDJSON tail with signal extraction. See `src/superclaude/cli/sprint/monitor.py` for the implementation. load_{CLI_NAME}_config({CONFIG_ARGS})\n\n    if dry_run:\n        _print_dry_run(config)\n        return\n\n    result = execute_{CLI_NAME}(config)\n    sys.exit(0 if result.outcome.value == \"success\" else 1)\n\n\ndef _print_dry_run(config):\n    \"\"\"Display execution plan without running.\"\"\"\n    from rich.console import Console\n    from rich.table import Table\n\n    console = Console()\n    table = Table(title=f\"{PIPELINE_NAME} Pipeline Plan\")\n    table.add_column(\"Step\", style=\"cyan\")\n    table.add_column(\"Output\", style=\"green\")\n    table.add_column(\"Gate\", style=\"yellow\")\n    table.add_column(\"Timeout\", style=\"dim\")\n\n    {DRY_RUN_ROWS}\n\n    console.print(table)\n```\n\n## Integration Template: `main.py` Patch\n\nAdd — port inference-based SuperClaude workflows into programmatic CLI pipelines with sprint-style supervised execution.\"\ncategory: development\ncomplexity: high\nallowed-tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Edit, Write, Bash, TodoWrite, Task\nmcp-servers: [sequential, serena, context7, auggie-mcp]\npersonas: [architect, analyzer, backend]\nargument-hint: \"--workflow <skill-name-or-path> [--name <cli-name>] [--output <dir>] [--dry-run] [--skip-integration]\"\n---\n\n# sc:cli-portify — Workflow-to-CLI Pipeline Compiler\n\nConvert inference-based SuperClaude workflows into programmatic CLI pipelines that ensure 100% completion and consistency between runs.\n\n## Purpose\n\nSuperClaude workflows implemented as commands + skills + agents + refs rely entirely on Claude's reasoning for orchestration. This works well for flexible, one-off tasks but breaks down for repeatable multi-step pipelines where you need:\n\n- **Deterministic control flow** — Python decides what runs when, not inference\n- **Formal artifact validation** — Gates check output quality programmatically\n- **Resume/retry** — Failed steps resume without re-running everything\n- **Live monitoring** — TUI shows progress, stall detection, diagnostics\n- **Budget economics** — Turn ledger tracks consumption across subprocesses\n- **Consistent behavior** — Same inputs produce structurally identical outputs every run\n\nThe existing `pipeline/` and `sprint/` modules already solve these problems. This skill bridges the gap by analyzing a workflow and generating a new CLI module that uses these proven patterns.\n\n## What Gets Generated\n\nA CLI subcommand package under `src/superclaude/cli/<name>/`:\n\n| File | Purpose | When Generated |\n|------|---------|----------------|\n| `models.py` | Domain types extending `PipelineConfig`, `Step`, `StepResult` | Always |\n| `gates.py` | `GateCriteria` + semantic check functions returning `tuple[bool, str]` | Always |\n| `prompts.py` | LLM contract builders per Claude-assisted step | Always |\n| `config.py` | CLI arg resolution, file discovery, config construction | Always |\n| `executor.py` | Synchronous supervisor loop with `ThreadPoolExecutor` for parallelism | Always |\n| `monitor.py` | NDJSON incremental output parser with domain signals | Always |\n| `process.py` | `ClaudeProcess` subclass with domain-specific prompt building | Always |\n| `tui.py` | Rich live dashboard with gate state machine | Always |\n| `logging_.py` | Dual JSONL + Markdown execution logging | Always |\n| `diagnostics.py` | `DiagnosticCollector`, `FailureClassifier`, `ReportGenerator` | Always |\n| `commands.py` | Click CLI group and subcommands | Always |\n| `__init__.py` | Package exports | Always |\n| `inventory.py` | Pure-programmatic file discovery/classification | If workflow has discovery steps |\n| `filtering.py` | Pure-programmatic inter-step data filtering | If workflow has filtering between steps |\n\nPlus integration patches for `main.py`.\n\n## Required Input\n\n```text\n/sc:cli-portify --workflow <skill-name-or-path> [--name <cli-name>] [--output <dir>]\n```\n\n| Argument | Required | Description |\n|----------|----------|-------------|\n| `--workflow` | Yes | Skill directory path or `sc-*` name to portify |\n| `--name` | No | CLI subcommand name (default: derived from workflow) |\n| `--output` | No | Output directory (default: `src/superclaude/cli/<name>/`) |\n| `--dry-run` | No | Show decomposition plan without generating code |\n| `--skip-integration` | No | Generate module only, skip main.py wiring |\n\n**STOP** if `--workflow` is not provided or the path doesn't resolve to a valid skill.\n\n## Behavioral Flow\n\nFour phases, each producing artifacts that feed the next. Present output to user for review after Phases 1 and 2 before continuing.\n\n### Phase 1: Workflow Analysis\n### Phase 2: Pipeline Specification\n### Phase 3: Code Generation\n### Phase 4: Integration\n\n## Decision Framework: What Becomes Programmatic\n...\n## Code Generation Principles\n...\n## Boundaries\n...\n## Related Commands\n...\n```\n\nThis file’s body is effectively the same workflow/compiler protocol already shown in the deprecated `sc-cli-portify` copy, but with promoted frontmatter and without the deprecation notice.\n\n### `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/skills/sc-cli-portify-protocol/decisions.yaml`\n```yaml\n# Blocking OQ Implementation Decisions\n# Generated: 2026-03-08\n# Task: T01.09\n# Roadmap Items: R-014, R-015\n\nschema_version: \"1.0\"\n\ndecisions:\n  OQ-002:\n    title: \"TurnLedger in pipeline API\"\n    blocking: true\n    resolution: \"TurnLedger is NOT in superclaude.cli.pipeline.models.
