{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01SpA2q4S9ee6boHks8Zk6yY","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→---\n     2→name: sc-cli-portify-protocol\n     3→description: \"Full behavioral protocol for sc:cli-portify — port inference-based SuperClaude workflows into programmatic CLI pipelines with sprint-style supervised execution.\"\n     4→category: development\n     5→complexity: high\n     6→allowed-tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Edit, Write, Bash, TodoWrite, Task\n     7→mcp-servers: [sequential, serena, context7, auggie-mcp]\n     8→personas: [architect, analyzer, backend]\n     9→argument-hint: \"--workflow <skill-name-or-path> [--name <cli-name>] [--output <dir>] [--dry-run] [--skip-integration]\"\n    10→---\n    11→\n    12→# sc:cli-portify — Workflow-to-CLI Pipeline Compiler\n    13→\n    14→Convert inference-based SuperClaude workflows into programmatic CLI pipelines that ensure 100% completion and consistency between runs.\n    15→\n    16→## Purpose\n    17→\n    18→SuperClaude workflows implemented as commands + skills + agents + refs rely entirely on Claude's reasoning for orchestration. This works well for flexible, one-off tasks but breaks down for repeatable multi-step pipelines where you need:\n    19→\n    20→- **Deterministic control flow** — Python decides what runs when, not inference\n    21→- **Formal artifact validation** — Gates check output quality programmatically\n    22→- **Resume/retry** — Failed steps resume without re-running everything\n    23→- **Live monitoring** — TUI shows progress, stall detection, diagnostics\n    24→- **Budget economics** — Turn ledger tracks consumption across subprocesses\n    25→- **Consistent behavior** — Same inputs produce structurally identical outputs every run\n    26→\n    27→The existing `pipeline/` and `sprint/` modules already solve these problems. This skill bridges the gap by analyzing a workflow and generating a new CLI module that uses these proven patterns.\n    28→\n    29→## What Gets Generated\n    30→\n    31→A CLI subcommand package under `src/superclaude/cli/<name>/`:\n    32→\n    33→| File | Purpose | When Generated |\n    34→|------|---------|----------------|\n    35→| `models.py` | Domain types extending `PipelineConfig`, `Step`, `StepResult` | Always |\n    36→| `gates.py` | `GateCriteria` + semantic check functions returning `tuple[bool, str]` | Always |\n    37→| `prompts.py` | LLM contract builders per Claude-assisted step | Always |\n    38→| `config.py` | CLI arg resolution, file discovery, config construction | Always |\n    39→| `executor.py` | Synchronous supervisor loop with `ThreadPoolExecutor` for parallelism | Always |\n    40→| `monitor.py` | NDJSON incremental output parser with domain signals | Always |\n    41→| `process.py` | `ClaudeProcess` subclass with domain-specific prompt building | Always |\n    42→| `tui.py` | Rich live dashboard with gate state machine | Always |\n    43→| `logging_.py` | Dual JSONL + Markdown execution logging | Always |\n    44→| `diagnostics.py` | `DiagnosticCollector`, `FailureClassifier`, `ReportGenerator` | Always |\n    45→| `commands.py` | Click CLI group and subcommands | Always |\n    46→| `__init__.py` | Package exports | Always |\n    47→| `inventory.py` | Pure-programmatic file discovery/classification | If workflow has discovery steps |\n    48→| `filtering.py` | Pure-programmatic inter-step data filtering | If workflow has filtering between steps |\n    49→\n    50→Plus integration patches for `main.py`.\n    51→\n    52→## Required Input\n    53→\n    54→```\n    55→/sc:cli-portify --workflow <skill-name-or-path> [--name <cli-name>] [--output <dir>]\n    56→```\n    57→\n    58→| Argument | Required | Description |\n    59→|----------|----------|-------------|\n    60→| `--workflow` | Yes | Skill directory path or `sc-*` name to portify |\n    61→| `--name` | No | CLI subcommand name (default: derived from workflow) |\n    62→| `--output` | No | Output directory (default: `src/superclaude/cli/<name>/`) |\n    63→| `--dry-run` | No | Show decomposition plan without generating code |\n    64→| `--skip-integration` | No | Generate module only, skip main.py wiring |\n    65→\n    66→**STOP** if `--workflow` is not provided or the path doesn't resolve to a valid skill.\n    67→\n    68→## Behavioral Flow\n    69→\n    70→Four phases, each producing artifacts that feed the next. Present output to user for review after Phases 1 and 2 before continuing.\n    71→\n    72→### Phase 1: Workflow Analysis\n    73→\n    74→Load `refs/analysis-protocol.md` before this phase. It contains the discovery checklist, step decomposition algorithm, classification rubric, and output format template.\n    75→\n    76→**Goal**: Decompose the target workflow into a structured pipeline specification.\n    77→\n    78→1. **Discover components** — Find the command `.md`, skill `SKILL.md`, all `refs/` and `rules/`, all `templates/`, all `scripts/`, and all referenced agents. Build a component inventory table with paths, line counts, and purposes.\n    79→\n    80→2. **Map the protocol** — Extract the step-by-step behavioral flow. Pay attention to:\n    81→   - Wave/phase boundaries in the skill\n    82→   - Agent delegation patterns (which agent, parallel vs sequential)\n    83→   - Conditional execution paths (flags that skip steps)\n    84→   - Implicit inter-step data flow (what one step produces that another consumes)\n    85→\n    86→3. **Identify steps** — A new Step boundary exists when:\n    87→   - A new artifact is produced\n    88→   - A different agent takes over\n    89→   - Execution mode changes (sequential → parallel)\n    90→   - A quality gate must be evaluated\n    91→   - Operation type changes (analysis → generation → validation)\n    92→\n    93→4. **Classify each step** on the programmatic spectrum:\n    94→   - **Pure programmatic**: Runs as a Python function, no Claude subprocess. Examples: file inventory via `git ls-files`, config construction, file filtering between passes, scoring formulas, structural validation, batch assignment. If the logic is deterministic and the input/output formats are well-defined, it should be programmatic.\n    95→   - **Claude-assisted**: Runs as a Claude subprocess with a prompt contract and gate. Examples: content analysis, writing reports, rubric-based assessment, creative synthesis, debate.\n    96→   - **Hybrid**: Programmatic setup → Claude subprocess → programmatic validation. Examples: steps that need Claude for content but have strict structural validation on the output.\n    97→\n    98→5. **Map dependencies and parallel groups** — Draw the data flow: which steps produce artifacts consumed by downstream steps? Which steps are independent and can run concurrently? For batched workflows, identify where batch parallelism applies.\n    99→\n   100→6. **Extract gates** — For each step output, identify what validation the original workflow expects. Map these to gate tiers: EXEMPT (informational), LIGHT (exists + non-empty), STANDARD (structural checks), STRICT (structural + semantic).\n   101→\n   102→7. **Assign gate modes** — `GateMode.BLOCKING` for steps whose output feeds downstream steps. `GateMode.TRAILING` for quality-only checks that don't affect data flow.\n   103→\n   104→**Output**: `portify-analysis.md` — Complete decomposition following the template in `refs/analysis-protocol.md`. Must include: component inventory, step graph with classifications, parallel groups, gates summary, data flow diagram, and recommendations.\n   105→\n   106→**Present to user for review before Phase 2.**\n   107→\n   108→### Phase 2: Pipeline Specification\n   109→\n   110→Load `refs/pipeline-spec.md` before this phase. It contains Step definition patterns, model design patterns, gate patterns, executor patterns, and the new unified-audit-gating patterns (TurnLedger, gate modes, subprocess isolation, context injection, resume semantics).\n   111→\n   112→**Goal**: Convert the analysis into concrete, code-ready specifications.\n   113→\n   114→1. **Design the Step graph** — Map each workflow step to `Step` objects. Batched parallel steps are `list[Step]` groups. Dynamic step counts (e.g., batch count determined at runtime) require a `build_steps()` function.\n   115→\n   116→2. **Define models** — Design domain-specific dataclasses:\n   117→   - Config extending `PipelineConfig` with workflow-specific fields (source paths, pass selection, batch sizes, stall/timeout policies)\n   118→   - Status enum with domain-specific states (e.g., `VALIDATION_FAIL`, `CRITICAL_FAIL` for audit workflows)\n   119→   - Result extending `StepResult` with execution telemetry\n   120→   - Aggregate result with `resume_command()` and `suggested_resume_budget`\n   121→   - Monitor state with domain-specific NDJSON signals\n   122→   - `TurnLedger` integration for budget tracking\n   123→\n   124→3. **Design prompts** — For each Claude-assisted step, write the prompt builder. Each prompt must specify:\n   125→   - What input files/context to embed (inline for <50KB, `--file` args for larger)\n   126→   - Required output sections and frontmatter fields\n   127→   - Machine-readable markers: `EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE|HALT`\n   128→   - Any structural requirements the gate will check\n   129→\n   130→   If prompts collectively exceed ~300 lines, put them in a separate `portify-prompts.md`.\n   131→\n   132→4. **Design gates** — For each output artifact, define:\n   133→   - `GateCriteria` with tier, required frontmatter, min lines, semantic checks\n   134→   - `GateMode` (BLOCKING or TRAILING)\n   135→   - All semantic check functions returning `tuple[bool, str]`\n   136→\n   137→5. **Implement pure-programmatic steps** — Write actual runnable Python code for steps classified as \"pure programmatic\". These run as direct function calls in the executor, bypassing `ClaudeProcess`. Include: file discovery, domain classification, batch assignment, inter-pass filtering, config construction.\n   138→\n   139→6. **Design the executor** — Sprint-style synchronous supervisor:\n   140→   - `ThreadPoolExecutor` for batch parallelism (NOT async/await)\n   141→   - `time.sleep()` polling loops for monitoring\n   142→   - Per-step/batch monitoring with stall detection\n   143→   - `TurnLedger` budget guards before each launch\n   144→   - Runner-authored truth: reports from observed data, not Claude self-reporting\n   145→   - Subprocess isolation (4-layer model) for child Claude sessions\n   146→   - Context injection: prior results compressed into subsequent prompts\n   147→   - Signal-aware shutdown with `SignalHandler`\n   148→\n   149→7. **Plan integration** — Click command group, main.py import, file generation order.\n   150→\n   151→**Output**: `portify-spec.md` (+ optional `portify-prompts.md`) — Complete code spec with models, gates, executor design, pure-programmatic implementations, and integration plan.\n   152→\n   153→**Present to user for approval before Phase 3.**\n   154→\n   155→### Phase 3: Code Generation\n   156→\n   157→Load `refs/code-templates.md` before this phase. It contains parameterized file templates for every module.\n   158→\n   159→Generate files in dependency order (models → gates → prompts → config → monitor → process → executor → tui → logging → diagnostics → commands → __init__). Each file imports from `superclaude.cli.pipeline` for shared base types and follows sprint/roadmap naming conventions.\n   160→\n   161→### Phase 4: Integration\n   162→\n   163→1. Patch `main.py` — Add import and `app.add_command()`\n   164→2. Verify imports — Quick check for circular dependencies\n   165→3. Generate structural test — Validates step graph, gate definitions, and model consistency\n   166→4. Write `portify-summary.md` — File inventory, CLI usage, step graph, known limitations\n   167→\n   168→## Decision Framework: What Becomes Programmatic\n   169→\n   170→The value of portification is moving orchestration out of inference. Here are concrete examples from tested workflows:\n   171→\n   172→| Operation | Classification | Why |\n   173→|-----------|---------------|-----|\n   174→| Enumerate repo files | Pure programmatic | `git ls-files` + pattern matching is deterministic |\n   175→| Classify file domains | Pure programmatic | Extension/path pattern rules, no judgment needed |\n   176→| Assign files to batches | Pure programmatic | Math: divide files by batch_size, assign to agents |\n   177→| Filter Pass 1 results for Pass 2 | Pure programmatic | Parse markdown, extract classifications, filter list |\n   178→| Analyze file for issues | Claude-assisted | Requires reading code, understanding context |\n   179→| Score variant quality | Hybrid | Formulas are programmatic, rubric evaluation is Claude |\n   180→| Validate structural integrity | Pure programmatic | Regex checks, section presence, field counts |\n   181→| Spot-check verification | Claude-assisted | Re-reads files, re-evaluates claims independently |\n   182→| Merge batch reports | Claude-assisted | Requires synthesis, deduplication, pattern extraction |\n   183→| Compute convergence score | Pure programmatic | `agreed / total` — arithmetic |\n   184→| Generate executive summary | Claude-assisted | Requires narrative synthesis |\n   185→\n   186→### Rule of Thumb\n   187→If you can write a Python function for it with clear input→output types and no ambiguity, make it programmatic. If it requires reading natural language, making judgments, or synthesizing information, use Claude with a gate to verify the output.\n   188→\n   189→## Code Generation Principles\n   190→\n   191→These align with the unified-audit-gating architecture in `pipeline/`.\n   192→\n   193→### Critical Constraints\n   194→1. **Synchronous execution** — Sprint uses `threading` + `time.sleep()` polling. Use `concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor` for batch parallelism. Do NOT use async/await.\n   195→2. **Gate function signatures** — All semantic checks return `tuple[bool, str]`. The reason string feeds diagnostics and retry decisions.\n   196→3. **Runner-authored truth** — Reports derive from runner-observed data (exit codes, artifacts, gates, monitor signals), not Claude's self-reported status.\n   197→4. **Deterministic flow control** — Python makes all decisions about what runs next. Claude never decides \"what's next\".\n   198→\n   199→### Shared Pipeline Patterns\n   200→5. **Reuse `pipeline/` primitives** — `PipelineConfig`, `Step`, `StepResult`, `GateMode`, `GateCriteria`, `SemanticCheck`, `gate_passed()`, `ClaudeProcess`\n   201→6. **Gate modes** — `BLOCKING` (default) stops pipeline on failure. `TRAILING` defers evaluation. Use trailing only for quality checks that don't affect downstream data flow.\n   202→7. **Turn budget** — `TurnLedger` with debit/credit/guard for multi-subprocess pipelines. Pre-launch guards prevent launching when budget too low.\n   203→8. **Subprocess isolation** — 4-layer model: scoped work dir, git ceiling, isolated plugin dir, isolated settings dir.\n   204→9. **Context injection** — Prior results serialized into subsequent prompts. Compress older, preserve recent.\n   205→10. **Resume-first failures** — On halt: exact resume command, remaining work, diagnostic path, budget suggestion.\n   206→11. **Diagnostic chain** — Runner-side failure analysis that doesn't consume turn budget.\n   207→\n   208→### Output Guidance\n   209→- `portify-analysis.md`: Under 400 lines. Step graph, gates, data flow.\n   210→- `portify-spec.md`: Split prompts to separate file if spec exceeds 800 lines.\n   211→- Pure-programmatic steps: Full implementation code, not descriptions.\n   212→\n   213→## Boundaries\n   214→\n   215→### Will Do\n   216→- Analyze any SuperClaude skill/command/agent workflow\n   217→- Decompose into structured pipeline specification\n   218→- Generate pipeline\n   224→- Modify original skill/command/agent files\n   225→- Create new skills or agents\n   226→- Make architectural decisions about the workflow's logic\n   227→- Generate LLM content 235→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2a0dcd71-e2d6-492b-be01-9ec2d4de516f","uuid":"e0ea68a9-604c-4d68-9b57-5d1f2109a66f","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/skills/sc-cli-portify-protocol/SKILL.md","content":"---\nname: sc-cli-portify-protocol\ndescription: \"Full behavioral protocol for sc:cli-portify — port inference-based SuperClaude workflows into programmatic CLI pipelines with sprint-style supervised execution.\"\ncategory: development\ncomplexity: high\nallowed-tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Edit, Write, Bash, TodoWrite, Task\nmcp-servers: [sequential, serena, context7, auggie-mcp]\npersonas: [architect, analyzer, backend]\nargument-hint: \"--workflow <skill-name-or-path> [--name <cli-name>] [--output <dir>] [--dry-run] [--skip-integration]\"\n---\n\n# sc:cli-portify — Workflow-to-CLI Pipeline Compiler\n\nConvert inference-based SuperClaude workflows into programmatic CLI pipelines that ensure 100% completion and consistency between runs.\n\n## Purpose\n\nSuperClaude workflows implemented as commands + skills + agents + refs rely entirely on Claude's reasoning for orchestration. This works well for flexible, one-off tasks but breaks down for repeatable multi-step pipelines where you need:\n\n- **Deterministic control flow** — Python decides what runs when, not inference\n- **Formal artifact validation** — Gates check output quality programmatically\n- **Resume/retry** — Failed steps resume without re-running everything\n- **Live monitoring** — TUI shows progress, stall detection, diagnostics\n- **Budget economics** — Turn ledger tracks consumption across subprocesses\n- **Consistent behavior** — Same inputs produce structurally identical outputs every run\n\nThe existing `pipeline/` and `sprint/` modules already solve these problems. This skill bridges the gap by analyzing a workflow and generating a new CLI module that uses these proven patterns.\n\n## What Gets Generated\n\nA CLI subcommand package under `src/superclaude/cli/<name>/`:\n\n| File | Purpose | When Generated |\n|------|---------|----------------|\n| `models.py` | Domain types extending `PipelineConfig`, `Step`, `StepResult` | Always |\n| `gates.py` | `GateCriteria` + semantic check functions returning `tuple[bool, str]` | Always |\n| `prompts.py` | LLM contract builders per Claude-assisted step | Always |\n| `config.py` | CLI arg resolution, file discovery, config construction | Always |\n| `executor.py` | Synchronous supervisor loop with `ThreadPoolExecutor` for parallelism | Always |\n| `monitor.py` | NDJSON incremental output parser with domain signals | Always |\n| `process.py` | `ClaudeProcess` subclass with domain-specific prompt building | Always |\n| `tui.py` | Rich live dashboard with gate state machine | Always |\n| `logging_.py` | Dual JSONL + Markdown execution logging | Always |\n| `diagnostics.py` | `DiagnosticCollector`, `FailureClassifier`, `ReportGenerator` | Always |\n| `commands.py` | Click CLI group and subcommands | Always |\n| `__init__.py` | Package exports | Always |\n| `inventory.py` | Pure-programmatic file discovery/classification | If workflow has discovery steps |\n| `filtering.py` | Pure-programmatic inter-step data filtering | If workflow has filtering between steps |\n\nPlus integration patches for `main.py`.\n\n## Required Input\n\n```\n/sc:cli-portify --workflow <skill-name-or-path> [--name <cli-name>] [--output <dir>]\n```\n\n| Argument | Required | Description |\n|----------|----------|-------------|\n| `--workflow` | Yes | Skill directory path or `sc-*` name to portify |\n| `--name` | No | CLI subcommand name (default: derived from workflow) |\n| `--output` | No | Output directory (default: `src/superclaude/cli/<name>/`) |\n| `--dry-run` | No | Show decomposition plan without generating code |\n| `--skip-integration` | No | Generate module only, skip main.py wiring |\n\n**STOP** if `--workflow` is not provided or the path doesn't resolve to a valid skill.\n\n## Behavioral Flow\n\nFour phases, each producing artifacts that feed the next. Present output to user for review after Phases 1 and 2 before continuing.\n\n### Phase 1: Workflow Analysis\n\nLoad `refs/analysis-protocol.md` before this phase. It contains the discovery checklist, step decomposition algorithm, classification rubric, and output format template.\n\n**Goal**: Decompose the target workflow into a structured pipeline specification.\n\n1. **Discover components** — Find the command `.md`, skill `SKILL.md`, all `refs/` and `rules/`, all `templates/`, all `scripts/`, and all referenced agents. Build a component inventory table with paths, line counts, and purposes.\n\n2. **Map the protocol** — Extract the step-by-step behavioral flow. Pay attention to:\n   - Wave/phase boundaries in the skill\n   - Agent delegation patterns (which agent, parallel vs sequential)\n   - Conditional execution paths (flags that skip steps)\n   - Implicit inter-step data flow (what one step produces that another consumes)\n\n3. **Identify steps** — A new Step boundary exists when:\n   - A new artifact is produced\n   - A different agent takes over\n   - Execution mode changes (sequential → parallel)\n   - A quality gate must be evaluated\n   - Operation type changes (analysis → generation → validation)\n\n4. **Classify each step** on the programmatic spectrum:\n   - **Pure programmatic**: Runs as a Python function, no Claude subprocess. Examples: file inventory via `git ls-files`, config construction, file filtering between passes, scoring formulas, structural validation, batch assignment. If the logic is deterministic and the input/output formats are well-defined, it should be programmatic.\n   - **Claude-assisted**: Runs as a Claude subprocess with a prompt contract and gate. Examples: content analysis, writing reports, rubric-based assessment, creative synthesis, debate.\n   - **Hybrid**: Programmatic setup → Claude subprocess → programmatic validation. Examples: steps that need Claude for content but have strict structural validation on the output.\n\n5. **Map dependencies and parallel groups** — Draw the data flow: which steps produce artifacts consumed by downstream steps? Which steps are independent and can run concurrently? For batched workflows, identify where batch parallelism applies.\n\n6. **Extract gates** — For each step output, identify what validation the original workflow expects. Map these to gate tiers: EXEMPT (informational), LIGHT (exists + non-empty), STANDARD (structural checks), STRICT (structural + semantic).\n\n7. **Assign gate modes** — `GateMode.BLOCKING` for steps whose output feeds downstream steps. `GateMode.TRAILING` for quality-only checks that don't affect data flow.\n\n**Output**: `portify-analysis.md` — Complete decomposition following the template in `refs/analysis-protocol.md`. Must include: component inventory, step graph with classifications, parallel groups, gates summary, data flow diagram, and recommendations.\n\n**Present to user for review before Phase 2.**\n\n### Phase 2: Pipeline Specification\n\nLoad `refs/pipeline-spec.md` before this phase. It contains Step definition patterns, model design patterns, gate patterns, executor patterns, and the new unified-audit-gating patterns (TurnLedger, gate modes, subprocess isolation, context injection, resume semantics).\n\n**Goal**: Convert the analysis into concrete, code-ready specifications.\n\n1. **Design the Step graph** — Map each workflow step to `Step` objects. Batched parallel steps are `list[Step]` groups. Dynamic step counts (e.g., batch count determined at runtime) require a `build_steps()` function.\n\n2. **Define models** — Design domain-specific dataclasses:\n   - Config extending `PipelineConfig` with workflow-specific fields (source paths, pass selection, batch sizes, stall/timeout policies)\n   - Status enum with domain-specific states (e.g., `VALIDATION_FAIL`, `CRITICAL_FAIL` for audit workflows)\n   - Result extending `StepResult` with execution telemetry\n   - Aggregate result with `resume_command()` and `suggested_resume_budget`\n   - Monitor state with domain-specific NDJSON signals\n   - `TurnLedger` integration for budget tracking\n\n3. **Design prompts** — For each Claude-assisted step, write the prompt builder. Each prompt must specify:\n   - What input files/context to embed (inline for <50KB, `--file` args for larger)\n   - Required output sections and frontmatter fields\n   - Machine-readable markers: `EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE|HALT`\n   - Any structural requirements the gate will check\n\n   If prompts collectively exceed ~300 lines, put them in a separate `portify-prompts.md`.\n\n4. **Design gates** — For each output artifact, define:\n   - `GateCriteria` with tier, required frontmatter, min lines, semantic checks\n   - `GateMode` (BLOCKING or TRAILING)\n   - All semantic check functions returning `tuple[bool, str]`\n\n5. **Implement pure-programmatic steps** — Write actual runnable Python code for steps classified as \"pure programmatic\". These run as direct function calls in the executor, bypassing `ClaudeProcess`. Include: file discovery, domain classification, batch assignment, inter-pass filtering, config construction.\n\n6. **Design the executor** — Sprint-style synchronous supervisor:\n   - `ThreadPoolExecutor` for batch parallelism (NOT async/await)\n   - `time.sleep()` polling loops for monitoring\n   - Per-step/batch monitoring with stall detection\n   - `TurnLedger` budget guards before each launch\n   - Runner-authored truth: reports from observed data, not Claude self-reporting\n   - Subprocess isolation (4-layer model) for child Claude sessions\n   - Context injection: prior results compressed into subsequent prompts\n   - Signal-aware shutdown with `SignalHandler`\n\n7. **Plan integration** — Click command group, main.py import, file generation order.\n\n**Output**: `portify-spec.md` (+ optional `portify-prompts.md`) — Complete code spec with models, gates, executor design, pure-programmatic implementations, and integration plan.\n\n**Present to user for approval before Phase 3.**\n\n### Phase 3: Code Generation\n\nLoad `refs/code-templates.md` before this phase. It contains parameterized file templates for every module.\n\nGenerate files in dependency order (models → gates → prompts → config → monitor → process → executor → tui → logging → diagnostics → commands → __init__). Each file imports from `superclaude.cli.pipeline` for shared base types and follows sprint/roadmap naming conventions.\n\n### Phase 4: Integration\n\n1. Patch `main.py` — Add import and `app.add_command()`\n2. Verify imports — Quick check for circular dependencies\n3. Generate structural test — Validates step graph, gate definitions, and model consistency\n4. Write `portify-summary.md` — File inventory, CLI usage, step graph, known limitations\n\n## Decision Framework: What Becomes Programmatic\n\nThe value of portification is moving orchestration out of inference. Here are concrete examples from tested workflows:\n\n| Operation | Classification | Why |\n|-----------|---------------|-----|\n| Enumerate repo files | Pure programmatic | `git ls-files` + pattern matching is deterministic |\n| Classify file domains | Pure programmatic | Extension/path pattern rules, no judgment needed |\n| Assign files to batches | Pure programmatic | Math: divide files by batch_size, assign to agents |\n| Filter Pass 1 results for Pass 2 | Pure programmatic | Parse markdown, extract classifications, filter list |\n| Analyze file for issues | Claude-assisted | Requires reading code, understanding context |\n| Score variant quality | Hybrid | Formulas are programmatic, rubric evaluation is Claude |\n| Validate structural integrity | Pure programmatic | Regex checks, section presence, field counts |\n| Spot-check verification | Claude-assisted | Re-reads files, re-evaluates claims independently |\n| Merge batch reports | Claude-assisted | Requires synthesis, deduplication, pattern extraction |\n| Compute convergence score | Pure programmatic | `agreed / total` — arithmetic |\n| Generate executive summary | Claude-assisted | Requires narrative synthesis |\n\n### Rule of Thumb\nIf you can write a Python function for it with clear input→output types and no ambiguity, make it programmatic. If it requires reading natural language, making judgments, or synthesizing information, use Claude with a gate to verify the output.\n\n## Code Generation Principles\n\nThese align with the unified-audit-gating architecture in `pipeline/`.\n\n### Critical Constraints\n1. **Synchronous execution** — Sprint uses `threading` + `time.sleep()` polling. Use `concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor` for batch parallelism. Do NOT use async/await.\n2. **Gate function signatures** — All semantic checks return `tuple[bool, str]`. The reason string feeds diagnostics and retry decisions.\n3. **Runner-authored truth** — Reports derive from runner-observed data (exit codes, artifacts, gates, monitor signals), not Claude's self-reported status.\n4. **Deterministic flow control** — Python makes all decisions about what runs next. Claude never decides \"what's next\".\n\n### Shared Pipeline Patterns\n5. **Reuse `pipeline/` primitives** — `PipelineConfig`, `Step`, `StepResult`, `GateMode`, `GateCriteria`, `SemanticCheck`, `gate_passed()`, `ClaudeProcess`\n6. **Gate modes** — `BLOCKING` (default) stops pipeline on failure. `TRAILING` defers evaluation. Use trailing only for quality checks that don't affect downstream data flow.\n7. **Turn budget** — `TurnLedger` with debit/credit/guard for multi-subprocess pipelines. Pre-launch guards prevent launching when budget too low.\n8. **Subprocess isolation** — 4-layer model: scoped work dir, git ceiling, isolated plugin dir, isolated settings dir.\n9. **Context injection** — Prior results serialized into subsequent prompts. Compress older, preserve recent.\n10. **Resume-first failures** — On halt: exact resume command, remaining work, diagnostic path, budget suggestion.\n11. **Diagnostic chain** — Runner-side failure analysis that doesn't consume turn budget.\n\n### Output Guidance\n- `portify-analysis.md`: Under 400 lines. Step graph, gates, data flow.\n- `portify-spec.md`: Split prompts to separate file if spec exceeds 800 lines.\n- Pure-programmatic steps: Full implementation code, not descriptions.\n\n## Boundaries\n\n### Will Do\n- Analyze any SuperClaude skill/command/agent workflow\n- Decompose into structured pipeline specification\n- Generate complete CLI subcommand module\n- Wire module into CLI infrastructure\n- Generate structural tests for pipeline\n\n### Will Not Do\n- Execute the generated pipeline\n- Modify original skill/command/agent files\n- Create new skills or agents\n- Make architectural decisions about the workflow's logic\n- Generate LLM content quality tests (only structural)\n\n## Related Commands\n\n- `/sc:implement` — General feature implementation\n- `/sc:design` — System architecture design\n- `/sc:analyze` — Code analysis for understanding workflows\n- `/sc:build` — Build and compile after generating\n","numLines":235,"startLine":1,"totalLines":235}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QxvZCJeSg4SwLmf3L5ddfc","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Pipeline Specification Reference\n     2→\n     3→Loaded during Phase 2 of portification. This ref provides the detailed specification patterns for converting a workflow analysis into concrete code designs.\n     4→\n     5→## Step Graph Design\n     6→\n     7→### Step Definition Pattern\n     8→\n     9→Every workflow operation that produces an artifact maps to a `Step`:\n    10→\n    11→```python\n    12→Step(\n    13→    id=\"step-id\",              # kebab-case, unique within pipeline\n    14→    prompt=build_X_prompt(),   # Function from prompts.py\n    15→    output_file=\"artifact.md\", # Relative to work_dir\n    16→    gate=STEP_GATE,            # From gates.py\n    17→    gate_mode=GateMode.BLOCKING,  # BLOCKING (default) or TRAILING\n    18→    timeout_seconds=600,       # Per-step timeout\n    19→    inputs=[\"dep1.md\"],        # Files this step reads\n    20→    retry_limit=1,             # How many retries on gate failure\n    21→    model=None,                # None = use default from config\n    22→)\n    23→```\n    24→\n    25→### Gate Mode Selection\n    26→\n    27→Choose `GateMode` based on step criticality:\n    28→- `BLOCKING` (default): Pipeline halts if gate fails. Use for steps whose output is consumed by downstream steps.\n    29→- `TRAILING`: Gate evaluates asynchronously; pipeline continues. Use for quality checks that don't affect downstream data flow.\n    30→\n    31→Rule of thumb from `resolve_gate_mode()`:\n    32→- Release scope → always BLOCKING\n    33→- Milestone scope → configurable\n    34→- Task scope → TRAILING when `grace_period > 0`\n    35→\n    36→### Parallel Group Pattern\n    37→\n    38→Steps that can run concurrently are wrapped in a list:\n    39→\n    40→```python\n    41→steps = [\n    42→    single_step_1,\n    43→    [parallel_step_a, parallel_step_b],  # Run concurrently\n    44→    single_step_2,                        # Waits for parallel group\n    45→]\n    46→```\n    47→\n    48→The generic `execute_pipeline()` handles thread coordination. If using sprint-style custom executor, implement your own parallel dispatch.\n    49→\n    50→### Dependency Rules\n    51→\n    52→- Steps consume outputs of prior steps via `inputs` list\n    53→- A step can only reference outputs from steps that precede it in the graph\n    54→- Parallel steps within a group must not depend on each other\n    55→- Resume logic skips steps whose output already passes their gate\n    56→\n    57→## Model Design Patterns\n    58→\n    59→### Config Model\n    60→\n    61→Always extend `PipelineConfig`:\n    62→\n    63→```python\n    64→@dataclass\n    65→class MyConfig(PipelineConfig):\n    66→    \"\"\"Pipeline-specific configuration.\"\"\"\n    67→    # Required: identify the input\n    68→    source_path: Path\n    69→\n    70→    # Optional: control behavior\n    71→    depth: str = \"standard\"       # quick|standard|deep\n    72→\n    73→    # Computed paths\n    74→    @property\n    75→    def results_dir(self) -> Path:\n    76→        return self.work_dir / \"results\"\n    77→\n    78→    @property\n    79→    def output_file(self, step_id: str) -> Path:\n    80→        return self.work_dir / f\"{step_id}-output.md\"\n    81→```\n    82→\n    83→### Status Enum\n    84→\n    85→Sprint-style pipelines need richer status than generic `StepStatus`:\n    86→\n    87→```python\n    88→class StepPhaseStatus(Enum):\n    89→    PENDING = \"pending\"\n    90→    RUNNING = \"running\"\n    91→    PASS = \"pass\"\n    92→    PASS_NO_SIGNAL = \"pass_no_signal\"    # Completed but no structured report\n    93→    PASS_NO_REPORT = \"pass_no_report\"    # Output exists but no result file\n    94→    INCOMPLETE = \"incomplete\"             # Budget exhausted\n    95→    HALT = \"halt\"                         # Step recommends stopping\n    96→    TIMEOUT = \"timeout\"                   # Hard timeout exceeded\n    97→    ERROR = \"error\"                       # Process crash\n    98→    SKIPPED = \"skipped\"                   # Skipped by config\n    99→```\n   100→\n   101→### Result Model\n   102→\n   103→Extend `StepResult` with domain telemetry:\n   104→\n   105→```python\n   106→@dataclass\n   107→class MyStepResult(StepResult):\n   108→    exit_code: int | None = None\n   109→    started_at: float | None = None\n   110→    finished_at: float | None = None\n   111→    output_bytes: int = 0\n   112→    error_bytes: int = 0\n   113→    # Domain-specific fields:\n   114→    artifacts_produced: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n   115→    gate_details: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n   116→```\n   117→\n   118→### Monitor State\n   119→\n   120→Domain-specific signals extracted from NDJSON output:\n   121→\n   122→```python\n   123→@dataclass\n   124→class MyMonitorState:\n   125→    output_bytes: int = 0\n   126→    last_growth_time: float = 0.0\n   127→    events_received: int = 0\n   128→    lines_total: int = 0\n   129→    growth_rate_bps: float = 0.0\n   130→    stall_seconds: float = 0.0\n   131→    # Domain-specific signals:\n   132→    last_step_id: str | None = None\n   133→    current_artifact: str | None = None\n   134→    files_changed: set = field(default_factory=set)\n   135→```\n   136→\n   137→## Gate Design Patterns\n   138→\n   139→### Gate Criteria Tiers\n   140→\n   141→```python\n   142→# Exempt: always passes (for optional/informational steps)\n   143→EXEMPT_GATE = GateCriteria(\n   144→    required_frontmatter_fields=[],\n   145→    min_lines=0,\n   146→    enforcement_tier=\"EXEMPT\",\n   147→)\n   148→\n   149→# Light: file exists and non-empty\n   150→LIGHT_GATE = GateCriteria(\n   151→    required_frontmatter_fields=[],\n   152→    min_lines=0,\n   153→    enforcement_tier=\"LIGHT\",\n   154→)\n   155→\n   156→# Standard: structural validation\n   157→STANDARD_GATE = GateCriteria(\n   158→    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"title\", \"status\"],\n   159→    min_lines=50,\n   160→    enforcement_tier=\"STANDARD\",\n   161→)\n   162→\n   163→# Strict: structural + semantic validation\n   164→STRICT_GATE = GateCriteria(\n   165→    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"title\", \"status\", \"convergence_score\"],\n   166→    min_lines=100,\n   167→    enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n   168→    semantic_checks=[\n   169→        SemanticCheck(\n   170→            name=\"no_heading_gaps\",\n   171→            check_fn=_no_heading_gaps,\n   172→            failure_message=\"Heading hierarchy has gaps (e.g. H1→H3 without H2)\",\n   173→        ),\n   174→        SemanticCheck(\n   175→            name=\"refs_resolve\",\n   176→            check_fn=_cross_refs_resolve,\n   177→            failure_message=\"Cross-references do not resolve to valid targets\",\n   178→        ),\n   179→    ],\n   180→)\n   181→```\n   182→\n   183→### Semantic Check Functions\n   184→\n   185→Semantic checks are pure functions matching the `Callable[[str], bool]` signature — they take file content and return a boolean:\n   186→\n   187→```python\n   188→def _no_heading_gaps(content: str) -> bool:\n   189→    \"\"\"Verify heading hierarchy has no gaps (H1->H3 without H2).\"\"\"\n   190→    # ... implementation ...\n   191→    return True  # or False on failure\n   192→\n   193→def _has_required_sections(content: str) -> bool:\n   194→    \"\"\"Verify all required sections are present.\"\"\"\n   195→    required = {\"## Summary\", \"## Findings\", \"## Recommendations\"}\n   196→    present = {line.strip() for line in content.splitlines() if line.startswith(\"## \")}\n   197→    missing = required - present\n   198→    return len(missing) == 0\n   199→```\n   200→\n   201→### Frontmatter Validation\n   202→\n   203→The shared `gate_passed()` function handles YAML frontmatter checks. Steps that produce markdown with frontmatter get automatic validation:\n   204→\n   205→```yaml\n   206→---\n   207→title: Diff Analysis\n   208→status: complete\n   209→diff_points: 12\n   210→convergence_ready: true\n   211→---\n   212→```\n   213→\n   214→## Prompt Design Patterns\n   215→\n   216→### Prompt Builder Structure\n   217→\n   218→Each step's prompt is a function that takes inputs and returns a complete prompt string:\n   219→\n   220→```python\n   221→def build_analysis_prompt(source_path: Path, config: MyConfig) -> str:\n   222→    return f\"\"\"Read the source file and produce a structured analysis.\n   223→\n   224→Source: {source_path}\n   225→Depth: {config.depth}\n   226→\n   227→## Required Output Format\n   228→\n   229→Write your analysis to the output file with this structure:\n   230→\n   231→```yaml\n   232→---\n   233→title: Analysis Report\n   234→status: complete\n   235→finding_count: <integer>\n   236→---\n   237→```\n   238→\n   239→## Sections Required\n   240→\n   241→### Summary\n   242→Brief overview of findings.\n   243→\n   244→### Findings\n   245→Numbered list of findings with evidence.\n   246→\n   247→### Recommendations\n   248→Actionable next steps.\n   249→\n   250→## Machine-Readable Markers\n   251→\n   252→At the end of your output, include:\n   253→```\n   254→EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\n   255→```\n   256→or\n   257→```\n   258→EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\n   259→```\n   260→\"\"\"\n   261→```\n   262→\n   263→### Key Prompt Rules\n   264→\n   265→1. **Specify output file format** — Tell Claude exactly what frontmatter fields to include\n   266→2. **Require machine-readable markers** — `EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE|HALT` for status determination\n   267→3. **Embed input content when small** — Under ~50KB, embed directly in prompt; otherwise use `--file` args\n   268→4. **Be explicit about sections** — Name every required section; don't leave structure to inference\n   269→5. **Include depth/focus constraints** — If the step has configurable depth, encode it in the prompt\n   270→\n   271→### Input Embedding Pattern\n   272→\n   273→```python\n   274→def _embed_inputs(prompt: str, inputs: list[Path], config: MyConfig) -> tuple[str, list[str]]:\n   275→    \"\"\"Embed small input files directly into prompt, return file args for large ones.\"\"\"\n   276→    file_args = []\n   277→    for input_path in inputs:\n   278→        full_path = config.work_dir / input_path\n   279→        if full_path.stat().st_size < 50_000:\n   280→            content = full_path.read_text()\n   281→            prompt += f\"\\n\\n---\\n## Input: {input_path}\\n\\n{content}\"\n   282→        else:\n   283→            file_args.append(str(full_path))\n   284→    return prompt, file_args\n   285→```\n   286→\n   287→## Executor Design Patterns\n   288→\n   289→### Sprint-Style Supervisor Loop\n   290→\n   291→The sprint executor pattern provides supervised execution with monitoring. IMPORTANT: Sprint uses synchronous execution with `threading` for parallelism, NOT `async/await`. Use `time.sleep()` polling loops and `concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor` for batch dispatch.\n   292→\n   293→```python\n   294→def execute_pipeline(config: MyConfig) -> MyResult:\n   295→    # 1. Pre-flight checks\n   296→    verify_claude_binary()\n   297→\n   298→    # 2. Setup infrastructure\n   299→    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n   300→    logger = MyLogger(config)\n   301→    tui = MyTUI(config)\n   302→    monitor = OutputMonitor()\n   303→    result = MyResult(config=config)\n   304→    ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=config.max_turns)\n   305→\n   306→    # 3. Start TUI\n   307→    tui.start()\n   308→\n   309→    try:\n   310→        for step in config.steps:\n   311→            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   312→                break\n   313→\n   314→            # 4. Reset monitor for this step\n   315→            monitor.reset(config.output_file(step))\n   316→            monitor.start()\n   317→\n   318→            # 5. Launch Claude subprocess\n   319→            process = ClaudeProcess(config, step)\n   320→            process.start()\n   321→\n   322→            # 6. Supervision loop\n   323→            while process.is_running():\n   324→                if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   325→                    process.stop()\n   326→                    break\n   327→\n   328→                state = monitor.get_state()\n   329→\n   330→                # Stall detection\n   331→                if state.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout:\n   332→                    process.stop()\n   333→                    break\n   334→\n   335→                # TUI update\n   336→                tui.update(step, state)\n   337→                time.sleep(0.5)\n   338→\n   339→            # 7. Classify result\n   340→            monitor.stop()\n   341→            status = determine_status(process.exit_code, step, config)\n   342→\n   343→            # 8. Record and decide\n   344→            step_result = build_result(step, status, monitor.state)\n   345→            result.add(step_result)\n   346→            logger.write_step_result(step_result)\n   347→\n   348→            if status not in (PASS, PASS_NO_SIGNAL):\n   349→                # Collect diagnostics and halt\n   350→                diagnostics = collect_diagnostics(step_result, monitor.state)\n   351→                write_diagnostic_report(diagnostics, config)\n   352→                result.outcome = HALTED\n   353→                break\n   354→\n   355→    finally:\n   356→        tui.stop()\n   357→        monitor.stop()\n   358→        signal_handler.restore()\n   359→\n   360→    return result\n   361→```\n   362→\n   363→### Status Classification\n   364→\n   365→Map every possible exit condition to a deterministic status:\n   366→\n   367→```python\n   368→def determine_status(exit_code, step, config):\n   369→    # 1. Hard failures\n   370→    if exit_code == 124:\n   371→        return TIMEOUT\n   372→    if exit_code != 0:\n   373→        return ERROR\n   374→\n   375→    # 2. Check for structured result file\n   376→    result_path = config.result_file(step)\n   377→    if result_path.exists():\n   378→        content = result_path.read_text()\n   379→        if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in content:\n   380→            return HALT\n   381→        if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in content:\n   382→            return PASS\n   383→        return PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n   384→\n   385→    # 3. Check for output without result\n   386→    output_path = config.output_file(step)\n   387→    if output_path.exists():\n   388→        if detect_error_max_turns(output_path):\n   389→            return INCOMPLETE\n   390→        return PASS_NO_REPORT\n   391→\n   392→    # 4. No output at all\n   393→    return ERROR\n   394→```\n   395→\n   396→## Integration Patterns\n   397→\n   398→### main.py Registration\n   399→\n   400→```python\n   401→# In src/superclaude/cli/main.py\n   402→from superclaude.cli.my_pipeline import my_pipeline_group\n   403→app.add_command(my_pipeline_group)\n   404→```\n   405→\n   406→### Click Command Group\n   407→\n   408→```python\n   409→@click.group(\"my-pipeline\")\n   410→def my_pipeline_group():\n   411→    \"\"\"My pipeline description.\"\"\"\n   412→    pass\n   413→\n   414→@my_pipeline_group.command(\"run\")\n   415→@click.argument(\"source\")\n   416→@click.option(\"--depth\", default=\"standard\", type=click.Choice([\"quick\", \"standard\", \"deep\"]))\n   417→@click.option(\"--dry-run\", is_flag=True)\n   418→@click.option(\"--max-turns\", default=25, type=int)\n   419→@click.option(\"--model\", default=None)\n   420→@click.option(\"--debug\", is_flag=True)\n   421→def run(source, depth, dry_run, max_turns, model, debug):\n   422→    \"\"\"Execute the pipeline.\"\"\"\n   423→    config = load_config(source, depth=depth, dry_run=dry_run, ...)\n   424→    result = execute_pipeline(config)\n   425→    sys.exit(0 if result.success else 1)\n   426→```\n   427→\n   428→## Turn Budget Economics (from unified-audit-gating)\n   429→\n   430→For pipelines with multiple Claude subprocesses, use `TurnLedger` to manage budget:\n   431→\n   432→```python\n   433→from superclaude.cli.sprint.models import TurnLedger\n   434→\n   435→@dataclass\n   436→class MyConfig(PipelineConfig):\n   437→    max_turns: int = 200\n   438→    min_launch_allocation: int = 10\n   439→    min_remediation_budget: int = 5\n   440→\n   441→# In executor:\n   442→ledger = TurnLedger(\n   443→    initial_budget=config.max_turns,\n   444→    minimum_allocation=config.min_launch_allocation,\n   445→    minimum_remediation_budget=config.min_remediation_budget,\n   446→)\n   447→\n   448→# Before launching a step:\n   449→if not ledger.can_launch():\n   450→    result.outcome = HALTED\n   451→    result.halt_reason = \"Budget exhausted\"\n   452→    break\n   453→\n   454→# After step completes, count actual turns used:\n   455→actual_turns = count_turns_from_output(output_path)\n   456→ledger.debit(actual_turns)\n   457→\n   458→# If step used fewer turns than allocated, credit back:\n   459→if actual_turns < allocated:\n   460→    ledger.credit(allocated - actual_turns)\n   461→```\n   462→\n   463→## Subprocess Isolation Pattern\n   464→\n   465→For pipelines spawning child Claude sessions, use the 4-layer isolation model:\n   466→\n   467→```python\n   468→def setup_isolation(work_dir: Path) -> dict[str, str]:\n   469→    \"\"\"Create isolated environment for child Claude subprocess.\"\"\"\n   470→    env = os.environ.copy()\n   471→\n   472→    # Layer 1: Scoped work directory\n   473→    env[\"CLAUDE_WORK_DIR\"] = str(work_dir)\n   474→\n   475→    # Layer 2: Git ceiling (prevent escaping work_dir)\n   476→    env[\"GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES\"] = str(work_dir.parent)\n   477→\n   478→    # Layer 3: Isolated plugin directory\n   479→    plugin_dir = work_dir / \".claude-plugins\"\n   480→    plugin_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n   481→    env[\"CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DIR\"] = str(plugin_dir)\n   482→\n   483→    # Layer 4: Isolated settings\n   484→    settings_dir = work_dir / \".claude-settings\"\n   485→    settings_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n   486→    env[\"CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR\"] = str(settings_dir)\n   487→\n   488→    return env\n   489→```\n   490→\n   491→## Context Injection Pattern\n   492→\n   493→Prior step results feed into subsequent prompts as compressed context:\n   494→\n   495→```python\n   496→def build_step_prompt_with_context(step, prior_results: list[StepResult]) -> str:\n   497→    \"\"\"Build prompt with prior step context injected.\"\"\"\n   498→    base_prompt = step.prompt\n   499→\n   500→    if not prior_results:\n   501→        return base_prompt\n   502→\n   503→    context_block = \"## Prior Step Context\\n\\n\"\n   504→    for result in prior_results[-3:]:  # Keep recent, compress older\n   505→        context_block += result.to_context_summary(verbose=(result == prior_results[-1]))\n   506→        context_block += \"\\n\\n\"\n   507→\n   508→    return f\"{base_prompt}\\n\\n{context_block}\"\n   509→```\n   510→\n   511→## Resume-First Failure Output Pattern\n   512→\n   513→On halt, generate actionable resume information:\n   514→\n   515→```python\n   516→@dataclass\n   517→class MyResult:\n   518→    # ... other fields ...\n   519→\n   520→    def resume_command(self) -> str | None:\n   521→        \"\"\"Generate CLI command to resume from failure point.\"\"\"\n   522→        if not self.halt_step:\n   523→            return None\n   524→        remaining = [r.step.id for r in self.step_results\n   525→                     if r.status == MyStatus.PENDING]\n   526→        return (\n   527→            f\"superclaude {CLI_NAME} run \"\n   528→            f\"--resume --start {self.halt_step} \"\n   529→            f\"--max-turns {self.suggested_resume_budget}\"\n   530→        )\n   531→\n   532→    @property\n   533→    def suggested_resume_budget(self) -> int:\n   534→        \"\"\"Estimate turns needed to complete remaining work.\"\"\"\n   535→        remaining_steps = sum(1 for r in self.step_results\n   536→                              if r.status in (MyStatus.PENDING, MyStatus.INCOMPLETE))\n   537→        return remaining_steps * 25  # Default allocation per step\n   538→```\n   539→\n   540→## Pure-Programmatic Step Execution\n   541→\n   542→Not all steps need Claude. Pure-programmatic steps run as direct Python function calls:\n   543→\n   544→```python\n   545→def execute_step(step, config):\n   546→    \"\"\"Route step to appropriate executor.\"\"\"\n   547→    if step.prompt == \"\":\n   548→        # Pure programmatic — run directly\n   549→        return run_programmatic_step(step, config)\n   550→    else:\n   551→        # Claude-assisted — launch subprocess\n   552→        return run_claude_step(step, config)\n   553→\n   554→def run_programmatic_step(step, config) -> StepResult:\n   555→    \"\"\"Execute a pure-programmatic step without Claude.\"\"\"\n   556→    started = time.time()\n   557→    try:\n   558→        # Call the step's implementation function\n   559→        PROGRAMMATIC_RUNNERS[step.id](config)\n   560→        return StepResult(step=step, status=StepStatus.PASS,\n   561→                         started_at=started, finished_at=time.time())\n   562→    except Exception as e:\n   563→        return StepResult(step=step, status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n   564→                         gate_failure_reason=str(e),\n   565→                         started_at=started, finished_at=time.time())\n   566→```\n   567→\n   568→## Gate Display State Machine (for TUI)\n   569→\n   570→Model gate states as explicit transitions for richer UX:\n   571→\n   572→```python\n   573→class GateDisplayState(Enum):\n   574→    NONE = \"none\"\n   575→    CHECKING = \"checking\"\n   576→    PASS = \"pass\"\n   577→    FAIL_DEFERRED = \"fail_deferred\"\n   578→    REMEDIATING = \"remediating\"\n   579→    REMEDIATED = \"remediated\"\n   580→    HALT = \"halt\"\n   581→\n   582→    @property\n   583→    def icon(self) -> str:\n   584→        return {\n   585→            \"none\": \" \", \"checking\": \"...\",\n   586→            \"pass\": \"ok\", \"fail_deferred\": \"!\",\n   587→            \"remediating\": \"~\", \"remediated\": \"ok\",\n   588→            \"halt\": \"X\",\n   589→        }[self.value]\n   590→```\n   591→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2a0dcd71-e2d6-492b-be01-9ec2d4de516f","uuid":"a8d10b33-2e61-43ce-9089-38bfc72fbbcc","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/skills/sc-cli-portify-protocol/refs/pipeline-spec.md","content":"# Pipeline Specification Reference\n\nLoaded during Phase 2 of portification. This ref provides the detailed specification patterns for converting a workflow analysis into concrete code designs.\n\n## Step Graph Design\n\n### Step Definition Pattern\n\nEvery workflow operation that produces an artifact maps to a `Step`:\n\n```python\nStep(\n    id=\"step-id\",              # kebab-case, unique within pipeline\n    prompt=build_X_prompt(),   # Function from prompts.py\n    output_file=\"artifact.md\", # Relative to work_dir\n    gate=STEP_GATE,            # From gates.py\n    gate_mode=GateMode.BLOCKING,  # BLOCKING (default) or TRAILING\n    timeout_seconds=600,       # Per-step timeout\n    inputs=[\"dep1.md\"],        # Files this step reads\n    retry_limit=1,             # How many retries on gate failure\n    model=None,                # None = use default from config\n)\n```\n\n### Gate Mode Selection\n\nChoose `GateMode` based on step criticality:\n- `BLOCKING` (default): Pipeline halts if gate fails. Use for steps whose output is consumed by downstream steps.\n- `TRAILING`: Gate evaluates asynchronously; pipeline continues. Use for quality checks that don't affect downstream data flow.\n\nRule of thumb from `resolve_gate_mode()`:\n- Release scope → always BLOCKING\n- Milestone scope → configurable\n- Task scope → TRAILING when `grace_period > 0`\n\n### Parallel Group Pattern\n\nSteps that can run concurrently are wrapped in a list:\n\n```python\nsteps = [\n    single_step_1,\n    [parallel_step_a, parallel_step_b],  # Run concurrently\n    single_step_2,                        # Waits for parallel group\n]\n```\n\nThe generic `execute_pipeline()` handles thread coordination. If using sprint-style custom executor, implement your own parallel dispatch.\n\n### Dependency Rules\n\n- Steps consume outputs of prior steps via `inputs` list\n- A step can only reference outputs from steps that precede it in the graph\n- Parallel steps within a group must not depend on each other\n- Resume logic skips steps whose output already passes their gate\n\n## Model Design Patterns\n\n### Config Model\n\nAlways extend `PipelineConfig`:\n\n```python\n@dataclass\nclass MyConfig(PipelineConfig):\n    \"\"\"Pipeline-specific configuration.\"\"\"\n    # Required: identify the input\n    source_path: Path\n\n    # Optional: control behavior\n    depth: str = \"standard\"       # quick|standard|deep\n\n    # Computed paths\n    @property\n    def results_dir(self) -> Path:\n        return self.work_dir / \"results\"\n\n    @property\n    def output_file(self, step_id: str) -> Path:\n        return self.work_dir / f\"{step_id}-output.md\"\n```\n\n### Status Enum\n\nSprint-style pipelines need richer status than generic `StepStatus`:\n\n```python\nclass StepPhaseStatus(Enum):\n    PENDING = \"pending\"\n    RUNNING = \"running\"\n    PASS = \"pass\"\n    PASS_NO_SIGNAL = \"pass_no_signal\"    # Completed but no structured report\n    PASS_NO_REPORT = \"pass_no_report\"    # Output exists but no result file\n    INCOMPLETE = \"incomplete\"             # Budget exhausted\n    HALT = \"halt\"                         # Step recommends stopping\n    TIMEOUT = \"timeout\"                   # Hard timeout exceeded\n    ERROR = \"error\"                       # Process crash\n    SKIPPED = \"skipped\"                   # Skipped by config\n```\n\n### Result Model\n\nExtend `StepResult` with domain telemetry:\n\n```python\n@dataclass\nclass MyStepResult(StepResult):\n    exit_code: int | None = None\n    started_at: float | None = None\n    finished_at: float | None = None\n    output_bytes: int = 0\n    error_bytes: int = 0\n    # Domain-specific fields:\n    artifacts_produced: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n    gate_details: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n```\n\n### Monitor State\n\nDomain-specific signals extracted from NDJSON output:\n\n```python\n@dataclass\nclass MyMonitorState:\n    output_bytes: int = 0\n    last_growth_time: float = 0.0\n    events_received: int = 0\n    lines_total: int = 0\n    growth_rate_bps: float = 0.0\n    stall_seconds: float = 0.0\n    # Domain-specific signals:\n    last_step_id: str | None = None\n    current_artifact: str | None = None\n    files_changed: set = field(default_factory=set)\n```\n\n## Gate Design Patterns\n\n### Gate Criteria Tiers\n\n```python\n# Exempt: always passes (for optional/informational steps)\nEXEMPT_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[],\n    min_lines=0,\n    enforcement_tier=\"EXEMPT\",\n)\n\n# Light: file exists and non-empty\nLIGHT_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[],\n    min_lines=0,\n    enforcement_tier=\"LIGHT\",\n)\n\n# Standard: structural validation\nSTANDARD_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"title\", \"status\"],\n    min_lines=50,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STANDARD\",\n)\n\n# Strict: structural + semantic validation\nSTRICT_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"title\", \"status\", \"convergence_score\"],\n    min_lines=100,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n    semantic_checks=[\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"no_heading_gaps\",\n            check_fn=_no_heading_gaps,\n            failure_message=\"Heading hierarchy has gaps (e.g. H1→H3 without H2)\",\n        ),\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"refs_resolve\",\n            check_fn=_cross_refs_resolve,\n            failure_message=\"Cross-references do not resolve to valid targets\",\n        ),\n    ],\n)\n```\n\n### Semantic Check Functions\n\nSemantic checks are pure functions matching the `Callable[[str], bool]` signature — they take file content and return a boolean:\n\n```python\ndef _no_heading_gaps(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Verify heading hierarchy has no gaps (H1->H3 without H2).\"\"\"\n    # ... implementation ...\n    return True  # or False on failure\n\ndef _has_required_sections(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Verify all required sections are present.\"\"\"\n    required = {\"## Summary\", \"## Findings\", \"## Recommendations\"}\n    present = {line.strip() for line in content.splitlines() if line.startswith(\"## \")}\n    missing = required - present\n    return len(missing) == 0\n```\n\n### Frontmatter Validation\n\nThe shared `gate_passed()` function handles YAML frontmatter checks. Steps that produce markdown with frontmatter get automatic validation:\n\n```yaml\n---\ntitle: Diff Analysis\nstatus: complete\ndiff_points: 12\nconvergence_ready: true\n---\n```\n\n## Prompt Design Patterns\n\n### Prompt Builder Structure\n\nEach step's prompt is a function that takes inputs and returns a complete prompt string:\n\n```python\ndef build_analysis_prompt(source_path: Path, config: MyConfig) -> str:\n    return f\"\"\"Read the source file and produce a structured analysis.\n\nSource: {source_path}\nDepth: {config.depth}\n\n## Required Output Format\n\nWrite your analysis to the output file with this structure:\n\n```yaml\n---\ntitle: Analysis Report\nstatus: complete\nfinding_count: <integer>\n---\n```\n\n## Sections Required\n\n### Summary\nBrief overview of findings.\n\n### Findings\nNumbered list of findings with evidence.\n\n### Recommendations\nActionable next steps.\n\n## Machine-Readable Markers\n\nAt the end of your output, include:\n```\nEXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\n```\nor\n```\nEXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\n```\n\"\"\"\n```\n\n### Key Prompt Rules\n\n1. **Specify output file format** — Tell Claude exactly what frontmatter fields to include\n2. **Require machine-readable markers** — `EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE|HALT` for status determination\n3. **Embed input content when small** — Under ~50KB, embed directly in prompt; otherwise use `--file` args\n4. **Be explicit about sections** — Name every required section; don't leave structure to inference\n5. **Include depth/focus constraints** — If the step has configurable depth, encode it in the prompt\n\n### Input Embedding Pattern\n\n```python\ndef _embed_inputs(prompt: str, inputs: list[Path], config: MyConfig) -> tuple[str, list[str]]:\n    \"\"\"Embed small input files directly into prompt, return file args for large ones.\"\"\"\n    file_args = []\n    for input_path in inputs:\n        full_path = config.work_dir / input_path\n        if full_path.stat().st_size < 50_000:\n            content = full_path.read_text()\n            prompt += f\"\\n\\n---\\n## Input: {input_path}\\n\\n{content}\"\n        else:\n            file_args.append(str(full_path))\n    return prompt, file_args\n```\n\n## Executor Design Patterns\n\n### Sprint-Style Supervisor Loop\n\nThe sprint executor pattern provides supervised execution with monitoring. IMPORTANT: Sprint uses synchronous execution with `threading` for parallelism, NOT `async/await`. Use `time.sleep()` polling loops and `concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor` for batch dispatch.\n\n```python\ndef execute_pipeline(config: MyConfig) -> MyResult:\n    # 1. Pre-flight checks\n    verify_claude_binary()\n\n    # 2. Setup infrastructure\n    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n    logger = MyLogger(config)\n    tui = MyTUI(config)\n    monitor = OutputMonitor()\n    result = MyResult(config=config)\n    ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=config.max_turns)\n\n    # 3. Start TUI\n    tui.start()\n\n    try:\n        for step in config.steps:\n            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                break\n\n            # 4. Reset monitor for this step\n            monitor.reset(config.output_file(step))\n            monitor.start()\n\n            # 5. Launch Claude subprocess\n            process = ClaudeProcess(config, step)\n            process.start()\n\n            # 6. Supervision loop\n            while process.is_running():\n                if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                    process.stop()\n                    break\n\n                state = monitor.get_state()\n\n                # Stall detection\n                if state.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout:\n                    process.stop()\n                    break\n\n                # TUI update\n                tui.update(step, state)\n                time.sleep(0.5)\n\n            # 7. Classify result\n            monitor.stop()\n            status = determine_status(process.exit_code, step, config)\n\n            # 8. Record and decide\n            step_result = build_result(step, status, monitor.state)\n            result.add(step_result)\n            logger.write_step_result(step_result)\n\n            if status not in (PASS, PASS_NO_SIGNAL):\n                # Collect diagnostics and halt\n                diagnostics = collect_diagnostics(step_result, monitor.state)\n                write_diagnostic_report(diagnostics, config)\n                result.outcome = HALTED\n                break\n\n    finally:\n        tui.stop()\n        monitor.stop()\n        signal_handler.restore()\n\n    return result\n```\n\n### Status Classification\n\nMap every possible exit condition to a deterministic status:\n\n```python\ndef determine_status(exit_code, step, config):\n    # 1. Hard failures\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return TIMEOUT\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        return ERROR\n\n    # 2. Check for structured result file\n    result_path = config.result_file(step)\n    if result_path.exists():\n        content = result_path.read_text()\n        if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in content:\n            return HALT\n        if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in content:\n            return PASS\n        return PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n\n    # 3. Check for output without result\n    output_path = config.output_file(step)\n    if output_path.exists():\n        if detect_error_max_turns(output_path):\n            return INCOMPLETE\n        return PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n    # 4. No output at all\n    return ERROR\n```\n\n## Integration Patterns\n\n### main.py Registration\n\n```python\n# In src/superclaude/cli/main.py\nfrom superclaude.cli.my_pipeline import my_pipeline_group\napp.add_command(my_pipeline_group)\n```\n\n### Click Command Group\n\n```python\n@click.group(\"my-pipeline\")\ndef my_pipeline_group():\n    \"\"\"My pipeline description.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n@my_pipeline_group.command(\"run\")\n@click.argument(\"source\")\n@click.option(\"--depth\", default=\"standard\", type=click.Choice([\"quick\", \"standard\", \"deep\"]))\n@click.option(\"--dry-run\", is_flag=True)\n@click.option(\"--max-turns\", default=25, type=int)\n@click.option(\"--model\", default=None)\n@click.option(\"--debug\", is_flag=True)\ndef run(source, depth, dry_run, max_turns, model, debug):\n    \"\"\"Execute the pipeline.\"\"\"\n    config = load_config(source, depth=depth, dry_run=dry_run, ...)\n    result = execute_pipeline(config)\n    sys.exit(0 if result.success else 1)\n```\n\n## Turn Budget Economics (from unified-audit-gating)\n\nFor pipelines with multiple Claude subprocesses, use `TurnLedger` to manage budget:\n\n```python\nfrom superclaude.cli.sprint.models import TurnLedger\n\n@dataclass\nclass MyConfig(PipelineConfig):\n    max_turns: int = 200\n    min_launch_allocation: int = 10\n    min_remediation_budget: int = 5\n\n# In executor:\nledger = TurnLedger(\n    initial_budget=config.max_turns,\n    minimum_allocation=config.min_launch_allocation,\n    minimum_remediation_budget=config.min_remediation_budget,\n)\n\n# Before launching a step:\nif not ledger.can_launch():\n    result.outcome = HALTED\n    result.halt_reason = \"Budget exhausted\"\n    break\n\n# After step completes, count actual turns used:\nactual_turns = count_turns_from_output(output_path)\nledger.debit(actual_turns)\n\n# If step used fewer turns than allocated, credit back:\nif actual_turns < allocated:\n    ledger.credit(allocated - actual_turns)\n```\n\n## Subprocess Isolation Pattern\n\nFor pipelines spawning child Claude sessions, use the 4-layer isolation model:\n\n```python\ndef setup_isolation(work_dir: Path) -> dict[str, str]:\n    \"\"\"Create isolated environment for child Claude subprocess.\"\"\"\n    env = os.environ.copy()\n\n    # Layer 1: Scoped work directory\n    env[\"CLAUDE_WORK_DIR\"] = str(work_dir)\n\n    # Layer 2: Git ceiling (prevent escaping work_dir)\n    env[\"GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES\"] = str(work_dir.parent)\n\n    # Layer 3: Isolated plugin directory\n    plugin_dir = work_dir / \".claude-plugins\"\n    plugin_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n    env[\"CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DIR\"] = str(plugin_dir)\n\n    # Layer 4: Isolated settings\n    settings_dir = work_dir / \".claude-settings\"\n    settings_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n    env[\"CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR\"] = str(settings_dir)\n\n    return env\n```\n\n## Context Injection Pattern\n\nPrior step results feed into subsequent prompts as compressed context:\n\n```python\ndef build_step_prompt_with_context(step, prior_results: list[StepResult]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Build prompt with prior step context injected.\"\"\"\n    base_prompt = step.prompt\n\n    if not prior_results:\n        return base_prompt\n\n    context_block = \"## Prior Step Context\\n\\n\"\n    for result in prior_results[-3:]:  # Keep recent, compress older\n        context_block += result.to_context_summary(verbose=(result == prior_results[-1]))\n        context_block += \"\\n\\n\"\n\n    return f\"{base_prompt}\\n\\n{context_block}\"\n```\n\n## Resume-First Failure Output Pattern\n\nOn halt, generate actionable resume information:\n\n```python\n@dataclass\nclass MyResult:\n    # ... other fields ...\n\n    def resume_command(self) -> str | None:\n        \"\"\"Generate CLI command to resume from failure point.\"\"\"\n        if not self.halt_step:\n            return None\n        remaining = [r.step.id for r in self.step_results\n                     if r.status == MyStatus.PENDING]\n        return (\n            f\"superclaude {CLI_NAME} run \"\n            f\"--resume --start {self.halt_step} \"\n            f\"--max-turns {self.suggested_resume_budget}\"\n        )\n\n    @property\n    def suggested_resume_budget(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Estimate turns needed to complete remaining work.\"\"\"\n        remaining_steps = sum(1 for r in self.step_results\n                              if r.status in (MyStatus.PENDING, MyStatus.INCOMPLETE))\n        return remaining_steps * 25  # Default allocation per step\n```\n\n## Pure-Programmatic Step Execution\n\nNot all steps need Claude. Pure-programmatic steps run as direct Python function calls:\n\n```python\ndef execute_step(step, config):\n    \"\"\"Route step to appropriate executor.\"\"\"\n    if step.prompt == \"\":\n        # Pure programmatic — run directly\n        return run_programmatic_step(step, config)\n    else:\n        # Claude-assisted — launch subprocess\n        return run_claude_step(step, config)\n\ndef run_programmatic_step(step, config) -> StepResult:\n    \"\"\"Execute a pure-programmatic step without Claude.\"\"\"\n    started = time.time()\n    try:\n        # Call the step's implementation function\n        PROGRAMMATIC_RUNNERS[step.id](config)\n        return StepResult(step=step, status=StepStatus.PASS,\n                         started_at=started, finished_at=time.time())\n    except Exception as e:\n        return StepResult(step=step, status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n                         gate_failure_reason=str(e),\n                         started_at=started, finished_at=time.time())\n```\n\n## Gate Display State Machine (for TUI)\n\nModel gate states as explicit transitions for richer UX:\n\n```python\nclass GateDisplayState(Enum):\n    NONE = \"none\"\n    CHECKING = \"checking\"\n    PASS = \"pass\"\n    FAIL_DEFERRED = \"fail_deferred\"\n    REMEDIATING = \"remediating\"\n    REMEDIATED = \"remediated\"\n    HALT = \"halt\"\n\n    @property\n    def icon(self) -> str:\n        return {\n            \"none\": \" \", \"checking\": \"...\",\n            \"pass\": \"ok\", \"fail_deferred\": \"!\",\n            \"remediating\": \"~\", \"remediated\": \"ok\",\n            \"halt\": \"X\",\n        }[self.value]\n```\n","numLines":591,"startLine":1,"totalLines":591}}}
