\"refactoring-spec-cli-portify.md\"\ncomplexity_score: 0.92\nadversarial: true\n---\n\n## Executive Summary\n\nThis roadmap delivers the refactoring of `sc-cli-portify` from a monolithic skill into a command/protocol split architecture with contract-driven phase boundaries, live API conformance checking, deterministic code generation with AST verification, and comprehensive failure routing with resume semantics.\n\n**Scope**: 70 requirements (52 functional, 18 non-functional) across 7 domains at enterprise complexity (0.92). The work produces a 5-phase portification pipeline that transforms SuperClaude skill/command/agent workflows into programmatic CLI pipelines with validated Python code generation.\n\n**Critical path**: Ref file remediation → Command/Protocol split → Contract schema definition → Phase 0-1 implementation → Phase 2 design → Phase 3-4 code generation and integration → Golden fixture validation.\n\n**Key architectural decisions**:\n- Contract-driven phase boundaries (YAML with versioned schemas) — phases are independently resumable\n- Live API snapshot with hash verification prevents drift-induced generation errors\n- Atomic generation with AST validation ensures no partial or broken output\n- 7 supported patterns only — explicit boundary prevents aspirational scope creep\n- Determinism validated against enumerated artifacts (`source_step_registry`, `step_mapping`, `module_plan`)\n\n**Open questions**: All 10 OQs must be resolved before Phase 2 implementation begins. OQ-002 (TurnLedger), OQ-003 (dry-run behavior), OQ-004 (integration schema), OQ-007 (approval gate mechanism), and OQ-008 (default output path) are blocking for design work.\n\n**Estimated delivery**: 14–19 working sessions (approximately 26–35 working days, assuming 1.5–2 sessions per day with review latency).\n\n---\n\n## Phase 1: Foundation and Prerequisite Remediation\n\n**Duration**: 2–3 sessions (~3–5 days)\n**Goal**: Eliminate known drift, establish the split architecture, resolve blocking open questions, define contract schema versioning policy\n\n### Milestone 1.1: Ref File API Alignment\n\nAddresses RISK-002 (ref file staleness) — the highest-priority prerequisite. No downstream work proceeds until refs match the live API.\n\n1. Read live `models.py` and `gates.py` to capture current API signatures\n2. Update `refs/pipeline-spec.md`:\n   - Fix tier casing: `tier=\"strict\"` → `enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\"`\n   - Align `GateCriteria` field names to match live dataclass\n   - Correct `SemanticCheck` contract: `Callable[[str], bool]` signature, not `tuple[bool, str]`\n3. Update `refs/code-templates.md`:\n   - Align all import paths to `superclaude.cli.pipeline.models` / `.gates`\n   - Fix gate template to use actual `GateCriteria` constructor fields\n   - Verify all 12 file templates reference correct base classes\n4. **Validation**: Diff ref files against live API — zero field name mismatches. Add stale-ref detector script that compares ref field names against live API signatures.\n\n**Requirements**: FR-048, NFR-003, NFR-018\n**Risks mitigated**: RISK-002\n\n### Milestone 1.2: Command/Protocol Split\n\nRuns in parallel with Milestone 1.1.\n\n1. Create `src/superclaude/skills/sc-cli-portify-protocol/` directory structure:\n   - `SKILL.md` (migrated from `sc-cli-portify/SKILL.md`)\n   - `__init__.py`\n   - `refs/` (moved from `sc-cli-portify/refs/` — updated files from M1.1)\n2. Create `src/superclaude/commands/cli-portify.md` (thin command shim):\n   - Parse arguments: `--workflow`, `--name`, `--output`, `--dry-run`, `--skip-integration`\n   - Input validation with 6 error codes (MISSING_WORKFLOW, INVALID_PATH, AMBIGUOUS_PATH, OUTPUT_NOT_WRITABLE, NAME_COLLISION, DERIVATION_FAILED)\n   - Delegate to `Skill sc:cli-portify-protocol`\n3. Promote YAML frontmatter in `SKILL.md`: name, description, category, complexity, allowed-tools, mcp-servers, personas, argument-hint\n4. Ensure `make verify-sync` covers `sc-cli-portify-protocol/` directory\n5. Deprecate `sc-cli-portify/` — mark for removal in Phase 5 after all validation passes\n\n**Requirements**: FR-001 through FR-006, FR-044, FR-045, FR-046\n\n### Milestone 1.3: Open Question Resolution\n\nResolve before proceeding to Phase 2:\n\n| OQ | Resolution | Blocking? |\n|----|-----------|-----------|\n| OQ-001 | Confirm spec typo — §5.3 → §11.2. Document in protocol. | No |\n| OQ-002 | Inspect codebase for `TurnLedger`. If absent from pipeline API, remove from spec. | **Yes** |\n| OQ-003 | `--dry-run` = execute Phases 0-2 only, emit contracts, no code generation. `--skip-integration` = skip Phase 4. | **Yes** |\n| OQ-004 | Define `portify-integration.yaml` schema: `main_py_patched`, `command_registered`, `test_file_generated`, `smoke_test_passed`. | **Yes** |\n| OQ-005 | Set `batch_dynamic: false` always (dynamic fan-out unsupported). Remove field or make constant. | No |\n| OQ-006 | Verify `refs/analysis-protocol.md` exists (confirmed). | No |\n| OQ-007 | Approval gates use TodoWrite checkpoint pattern: write contract → mark task \"awaiting review\" → user resumes. | **Yes** |\n| OQ-008 | Default `--output` to `src/superclaude/cli/<derived_name>/`. Blocking: affects contract emission paths. | **Yes** |\n| OQ-009 | Structural tests go to `tests/` per project convention. | No |\n| OQ-010 | Extract step boundary algorithm from current `SKILL.md` and document explicitly in `refs/analysis-protocol.md`. | **Yes** for Phase 3 |\n\n**Exit criteria**: All 10 OQs have documented resolutions. Blocking OQs (002, 003, 004, 007, 008, 010) have implementation decisions recorded in `decisions.yaml`.\n\n### Phase 1 Exit Criteria\n\n- No legacy structural conflicts remain\n- Protocol and command discoverable from both `src/` and `.claude/`\n- Ref files verified against live API with zero mismatches\n- All 10 OQs resolved and documented\n- `make verify-sync` passes for new protocol directory\n\n---\n\n## Phase 2: Protocol Core — Contract Infrastructure, Phase 0, and Phase 1\n\n**Duration**: 3–4 sessions (~5–8 days)\n**Goal**: Implement contract infrastructure, prerequisite scanning (Phase 0), and workflow analysis (Phase 1)\n**Dependencies**: Phase 1 complete, all blocking OQs resolved\n\n### Milestone 2.1: Contract Infrastructure and Schema Versioning\n\nContract schemas are defined as a prerequisite sub-task, then validated through real Phase 0 execution.\n\n1. Define contract schema versioning policy:\n   - Schema version field in contract header (e.g., `schema_version: \"1.0\"`)\n   - Backward-compatibility rules: additive fields allowed, field removal requires major version bump\n   - Version validation on contract read — reject incompatible versions with actionable error\n2. Define YAML contract common header schema:\n   ```yaml\n   schema_version: \"1.0\"\n   phase: <int>\n   status: \"passed\" | \"failed\" | \"skipped\"\n   timestamp: <ISO8601>\n   resume_checkpoint: <string>\n   validation_status: {blocking_passed: <int>, blocking_failed: <int>, advisory: [...]}\n   ```\n3. Define per-phase contract schemas (`portify-prerequisites.yaml`, `portify-analysis.yaml`, `portify-spec.yaml`, `portify-codegen.yaml`, `portify-integration.yaml`, return contract)\n4. Implement contract validation logic — next phase reads and validates incoming contract before proceeding\n5. Implement return contract structure (FR-043): status, failure_phase, failure_type, generated_files, counts, api_snapshot_hash, resume_command, warnings, phase_contracts\n6. Implement resume protocol (FR-052): read latest contract, validate completed phases, resume from failure point\n7. Validate resume semantics with a synthetic failure scenario before Phase 0 runs — proves contract mechanics work independently\n8. Define null-field policy: unreached fields explicitly set to `null`\n\n**Requirements**: FR-007, FR-008, FR-009, FR-043, FR-052, NFR-009, NFR-015, NFR-016\n\n### Milestone 2.2: Phase 0 — Prerequisites\n\n1. Workflow path resolution from `--workflow` argument (FR-010):\n   - Locate command `.md`, skill `SKILL.md`, all refs/rules/templates/scripts\n   - Abort with `AMBIGUOUS_PATH` on multiple candidates\n2. Live API snapshot (FR-011):\n   - Read `models.py` and `gates.py` via Serena/Auggie\n   - Extract signatures: `SemanticCheck`, `GateCriteria`, `gate_passed()`, `PipelineConfig`, `Step`, `StepResult`, `GateMode`\n   - Store as `api-snapshot.yaml` with content hash\n3. Output directory collision check (FR-012):\n   - Check for `portify-summary.md` marker\n   - Apply collision policy: overwrite portified (with confirmation), abort on non-portified, abort on `main.py` name collision\n4. Early unsupported-pattern scan (FR-013):\n   - Detect: recursive agent self-orchestration, interactive human decisions mid-pipeline, no stable artifact boundaries, dynamic code generation/eval\n   - Emit blocking warning on detection\n5. Emit `portify-prerequisites.yaml` contract (FR-014)\n\n**Requirements**: FR-010 through FR-014, FR-047, NFR-013, NFR-014\n**Risks mitigated**: RISK-001, RISK-003, RISK-008, RISK-012\n\n### Milestone 2.3: Phase 1 — Workflow Analysis\n\n1. Build component inventory with stable `component_id` (C-NNN) (FR-015)\n2. Step decomposition with stable `source_id` (S-NNN) and conservation invariant (FR-016, FR-049)\n3. Step classification: pure_programmatic / claude_assisted / hybrid with confidence scoring (FR-017)\n   - Flag for user review if confidence < 0.7\n4. Dependency DAG with acyclicity validation (FR-018)\n5. Gate tier assignment (EXEMPT/LIGHT/STANDARD/STRICT) and mode (BLOCKING/TRAILING) (FR-019)\n6. Trailing gate safety escalation (FR-020)\n7. Divergence detection for ambiguous step boundaries (FR-050)\n8. 7 self-validation checks (6 blocking, 1 advisory) (FR-021)\n9. Emit `portify-analysis.md` (< 400 lines) and `portify-analysis.yaml` (FR-022)\n10. User review gate — present analysis, allow classification overrides (FR-023)\n\n**Requirements**: FR-015 through FR-023, FR-049, FR-050, NFR-004, NFR-005\n**Risks mitigated**: RISK-004, RISK-007\n\n### Milestone 2.4: TodoWrite Integration\n\n1. Define 23 subphase tasks across 5 phases (FR-051)\n2. Implement checkpoint triggers: after phase completion, user review gates, before write operations, on failure\n3. Wire TodoWrite updates into Phase 0 and Phase 1 execution flow\n\n**Requirements**: FR-051, SC-013\n\n### Phase 2 Exit Criteria\n\n- Contract schemas defined with versioning policy\n- Resume semantics validated against synthetic failure\n- Phase 0 correctly scans a test workflow and emits valid `portify-prerequisites.yaml`\n- Phase 1 produces valid `portify-analysis.yaml` with conservation invariant holding\n- Resume from Phase 0 failure correctly re-enters Phase 0\n- Unsupported pattern in test workflow aborts before Phase 1\n\n---\n\n## Phase 3: Protocol Core — Phase 2 Implementation (Design)\n\n**Duration**: 3–4 sessions (~5–8 days)\n**Goal**: Implement the specification/design phase of the protocol\n**Dependencies**: Phase 2 complete (Milestones 2.1–2.4 passing)\n\n### Milestone 3.1: Step Graph Design\n\n1. Source-to-generated step mapping with 1:1, 1:N, N:1, 1:0 recording and justification (FR-024)\n2. Coverage invariant enforcement: `|source_step_registry| == |mapped_steps| + |elimination_records|` (FR-025, FR-049)\n3. Elimination records with source_id, reason, approved_by fields\n\n**Requirements**: FR-024, FR-025, SC-007\n\n### Milestone 3.2: Model and Gate Design\n\n1. Domain-specific dataclass models extending `PipelineConfig` and `StepResult` (FR-026)\n   - Config, Result, Status enum, Monitor state with NDJSON signals\n   - Resolve TurnLedger integration per OQ-002 decision\n2. Gate design using live `GateCriteria` fields (FR-028):\n   - `required_frontmatter_fields`, `min_lines`, `enforcement_tier`, `semantic_checks`\n   - Semantic check functions: `Callable[[str], bool]` signature\n3. API conformance verification against Phase 0 snapshot hash (RISK-001 mitigation)\n\n**Requirements**: FR-026, FR-028, NFR-003, NFR-011, SC-005, SC-006\n\n### Milestone 3.3: Prompt and Executor Design\n\n1. Prompt design for Claude-assisted steps (FR-027):\n   - Input strategy, output sections, frontmatter fields, machine-readable markers\n   - Split to `portify-prompts.md` if > 300 lines\n2. Pure-programmatic step implementation as full Python code (FR-029)\n3. Executor design: sprint-style synchronous supervisor with ThreadPoolExecutor (FR-030)\n4. Pattern coverage matrix — verify all required patterns covered, abort on unsupported (FR-031)\n\n**Requirements**: FR-027, FR-029, FR-030, FR-031, NFR-006, NFR-010\n\n### Milestone 3.4: Phase 2 Self-Validation and Output\n\n1. 8 self-validation checks (7 blocking, 1 advisory) (FR-032)\n2. Emit `portify-spec.yaml` with step_mapping, module_plan, gate_definitions, pattern_coverage, api_conformance (FR-033)\n3. User approval gate before Phase 3 code generation\n\n**Requirements**: FR-032, FR-033\n\n### Phase 3 Exit Criteria\n\n- `portify-spec.yaml` produced from test workflow with valid step mapping\n- Coverage invariant holds\n- All gate designs reference correct `GateCriteria` field names from API snapshot\n- Pattern coverage matrix shows 100% coverage for test workflow patterns\n\n---\n\n## Phase 4: Code Generation and Integration (Phases 3–4)\n\n**Duration**: 4–5 sessions (~7–10 days)\n**Goal**: Implement deterministic Python code generation and CLI integration\n**Dependencies**: Phase 3 complete\n\n### Milestone 4.1: Phase 3 — Code Generation Engine\n\n1. Generate 12 Python files in strict dependency order (FR-034):\n   `models → gates → prompts → config → monitor → process → executor → tui → logging_ → diagnostics → commands → __init__.py`\n2. Atomic generation — halt entirely on any file failure (FR-035)\n3. Per-file validation (6 checks, 5 blocking, 1 advisory) (FR-036):\n   - `ast.parse()` syntax validation\n   - Import path verification\n   - Base class contract checking (`PipelineConfig`, `StepResult`)\n   - `GateCriteria` field name matching against API snapshot\n   - `SemanticCheck` signature verification (`Callable[[str], bool]`)\n4. Cross-file validation (4 checks, all blocking) (FR-037):\n   - Module completeness (all 12 files present)\n   - Circular import detection (import graph acyclicity)\n   - `__init__.py` export matching\n   - Step count matching against spec\n5. Emit `portify-codegen.yaml` (FR-038)\n\n**Requirements**: FR-034 through FR-038, NFR-001, NFR-002, NFR-007, NFR-011, NFR-012\n**Risks mitigated**: RISK-005, RISK-001\n**Success criteria validated**: SC-003, SC-004, SC-005, SC-006, SC-008\n\n### Milestone 4.2: Phase 4 — CLI Integration\n\n1. Patch `main.py` with import and `app.add_command()` (FR-039)\n   - Abort on naming collision\n2. Integration smoke test (FR-040):\n   - Module imports successfully\n   - Click command group exists\n   - Command name matches registration\n3. Generate structural test file `test_<name>_structure.py` in `tests/` (FR-041):\n   - Step graph completeness\n   - Gate definitions\n   - Model consistency\n   - Command registration\n4. Write `portify-summary.md` (FR-042):\n   - File inventory, CLI usage, step graph, known limitations, resume command template\n5. Emit `portify-integration.yaml` contract\n\n**Requirements**: FR-039 through FR-042, SC-009\n**Risks mitigated**: RISK-008\n\n### Milestone 4.3: Name Normalization and Collision Policy\n\n1. Implement naming convention derivation (FR-046):\n   - kebab-case (CLI), snake_case (Python), PascalCase (classes), UPPER_SNAKE (config)\n   - Strip `sc-` prefix and `-protocol` suffix\n2. Collision policy enforcement (FR-047):\n   - Check `portify-summary.md` marker for prior portification\n   - Never overwrite non-portified code (NFR-013)\n\n**Requirements**: FR-046, FR-047, NFR-012, NFR-013, SC-012\n\n### Phase 4 Exit Criteria\n\n- Generated module for test workflow passes all AST checks\n- No circular imports\n- `main.py` patching succeeds and smoke test passes\n- Structural test file generated and passes\n- Return contract emitted with all fields populated\n\n---\n\n## Phase 5: Validation, Testing, and Cleanup\n\n**Duration**: 2–3 sessions (~4–6 days)\n**Goal**: End-to-end validation with golden fixtures, negative-path testing, cleanup, and sync verification\n**Dependencies**: Phase 4 complete\n\n### Milestone 5.1: Golden Fixture Tests and Negative-Path Validation\n\nGolden fixtures (SC-014):\n\n1. **Simple sequential skill**: Basic skill portification, all phases pass\n2. **Batched audit skill**: Parallel step groups, trailing gates, complex DAG\n3. **Adversarial multi-agent skill**: Multi-domain, high step count, N:1 mappings\n4. **Intentionally unsupported skill**: Contains dynamic code gen pattern — correctly aborts in Phase 0\n\nEach fixture validates:\n- Determinism (SC-002): repeated runs produce identical `source_step_registry`, `step_mapping`, and `module_plan`\n- All blocking checks pass or halt with correct failure state (SC-001)\n- Return contract always emitted (SC-010)\n- Step conservation invariant holds (SC-007)\n\nNegative-path fixtures (comprehensive failure-scenario coverage):\n\n5. **Stale-ref fixture**: Ref files with deliberately wrong field names — blocks before Phase 1\n6. **API-drift fixture**: Modified API signatures between snapshot and generation — blocks at conformance check\n7. **Name collision fixture**: Pre-existing non-portified code at output path — aborts with correct error\n8. **Non-portified collision fixture**: Pre-existing human-written code at output path — never overwrites\n\n### Milestone 5.2: MCP Degradation Testing\n\nRuns in parallel with Milestone 5.1.\n\n1. Simulate MCP server unavailability for each server (Auggie, Serena, Sequential, Context7)\n2. Verify graceful degradation to native tools (NFR-008)\n3. Verify advisory warnings logged in phase contracts\n4. Verify no phase hard-blocks on MCP unavailability\n\n**Risks mitigated**: RISK-006\n\n### Milestone 5.3: Resume Protocol Validation\n\nRuns in parallel with Milestone 5.1.\n\n1. Simulate failures at each phase boundary (0→1, 1→2, 2→3, 3→4)\n2. Verify resume correctly skips completed phases\n3. Verify resume re-validates completed phase contracts (not blindly trusted)\n4. Verify `resume_command` template in return contract is correct and executable\n\n**Requirements**: FR-052, SC-001\n**Risks mitigated**: RISK-009\n\n### Milestone 5.4: Cleanup and Sync\n\n1. Remove old `sc-cli-portify/` directory — deprecated in Phase 1, removed now after all validation passes\n2. Run `make verify-sync` — confirm `src/` and `.claude/` match for protocol directory (FR-006)\n3. Final `make test` — all existing tests still pass\n4. Final `make lint` — no ruff violations in new/modified files\n\n### Phase 5 Exit Criteria\n\n- All 4 golden fixture tests pass\n- All 4 negative-path fixtures produce correct failure behavior\n- MCP degradation produces advisory warnings, not failures\n- Resume from each phase boundary works correctly\n- `make verify-sync` passes\n- `make test` passes\n- Old `sc-cli-portify/` removed\n\n---\n\n## Risk Assessment\n\n### High Severity\n\n| Risk | Phase Mitigated | Strategy |\n|------|----------------|----------|\n| RISK-001: Live API drift | Phase 1 (M1.1), Phase 4 (M4.1), Phase 5 (M5.1) | Snapshot with hash verification at every phase boundary; API-drift negative fixture validates detection |\n| RISK-002: Ref file staleness | **Phase 1 (M1.1)** — hard prerequisite | Update refs before any other work; stale-ref detector blocks generation; stale-ref negative fixture validates detection |\n| RISK-003: Unsupported pattern | Phase 2 (M2.2), Phase 5 (M5.1) | Early scan in Phase 0 before analysis investment; collision negative fixture validates abort |\n| RISK-009: Resume state corruption | Phase 5 (M5.3) | Re-validate contracts on resume; compare filesystem state and snapshot hash |\n\n### Low Severity\n\n| Risk | Phase Mitigated | Strategy |\n|------|----------------|----------|\n| RISK-010: Human review bottleneck | By design | `--dry-run` for preview (Phases 0-2 only); approval gates are intentional safety |\n| RISK-011: Template coverage gap | Phase 3 (M3.3) | Explicit coverage matrix with abort on gap |\n| RISK-012: Non-portified collision | Phase 4 (M4.3), Phase 5 (M5.1) | `portify-summary.md` marker detection; non-portified collision negative fixture validates behavior |\n\n**Highest-risk item requiring immediate attention**: RISK-002 (ref file staleness). parallel)\n    52→- A quality gate must be evaluated\n    53→- The operation type changes (analysis → generation → validation)\n    54→\n    55→### Classify Each Step\n    56→\n    57→Rate each step Deterministic, formula-based, structural | Python function, no Claude |\n    62→| **Programmatic + Validation** | Structural check with semantic judgment | Python primary, Claude for edge cases |\n    63→| **Claude-Assisted** | Content generation within structured format | Claude subprocess with gate validation |\n    64→| **Pure Inference** | Creative, subjective, context-dependent | Claude `config.py` validation logic |\n    77→| Output format | Frontmatter requirements in gate + prompt |\n    78→| Retry on failure | `retry_limit` on Step |\n    79→| Skip on success | Resume logic in Parse inputs and validate (programmatic)\n   179→2. Generate variants in parallel (Claude, parallel group)\n   180→3. Diff analysis (Claude, single step with strict gate)\n   181→4. Guides the systematic decomposition of an inference-based workflow into a pipeline specification.\n\n## Discovery Checklist\n\nWhen analyzing a target workflow, locate and read these components in order:\n\n### 1. Find the Command\n\nLook in `src/superclaude/commands/` for the matching command file. Extract:\n- Frontmatter: category, complexity, mcp-servers, personas\n- Behavioral Flow section: the high-level step sequence\n- Arguments and options\n- Boundaries (Will Do / Will Not Do)\n\n### 2. Find the Skill\n\nLook in `src/superclaude/skills/` for the matching skill directory. Read:\n- `SKILL.md`: The full protocol with step-by-step behavioral flow\n- `refs/`: All reference files (algorithms, templates, scoring protocols)\n- `rules/`: Validation rules and classification taxonomies\n- `templates/`: Output format templates (these become gate validation targets)\n- `scripts/`: Shell scripts for preprocessing (these become pure-programmatic steps)\n- Note which refs are loaded at which phase/wave\n\n### 3. Find the Agents\n\nSearch for agents referenced in the skill:\n- Look in `src/superclaude/agents/` for `.md` files\n- Note each agent's: triggers, tools, responsibilities, boundaries\n- Identify delegation patterns: parallel vs sequential, orchestrator vs worker\n\n### 4. Map the Data Flow\n\nTrace how data moves through the workflow:\n- What inputs does each step consume?\n- What artifacts does each step produce?\n- Which artifacts feed into downstream steps?\n- What is the final output?\n\n## Step Decomposition Algorithm\n\nFor each discrete operation in the workflow:\n\n### Identify Step Boundaries\n\nA new Step starts when:\n- A new artifact is produced\n- A different agent takes over\n- The execution mode changes (sequential → parallel)\n- A quality gate must be evaluated\n- The operation type changes (analysis → generation → validation)\n\n### Classify Each Step\n\nRate each step on the **programmatic spectrum**:\n\n| Classification | Characteristics | Implementation |\n|---------------|-----------------|----------------|\n| **Pure Programmatic** | Deterministic, formula-based, structural | Python function, no Claude |\n| **Programmatic + Validation** | Structural check with semantic judgment | Python primary, Claude for edge cases |\n| **Claude-Assisted** | Content generation within structured format | Claude subprocess with gate validation |\n| **Pure Inference** | Creative, subjective, context-dependent | Claude subprocess with light gate |\n\n### Map to Pipeline Primitives\n\n| Workflow Concept | Pipeline Primitive |\n|-----------------|-------------------|\n| Sequential step | `Step` in step list |\n| Parallel operations | `list[Step]` parallel group |\n| Quality gate | `GateCriteria` on the Step |\n| Agent delegation | Prompt in `prompts.py` |\n| Scoring formula | Python function in `gates.py` or separate module |\n| Status decision | `_determine_status()` in `executor.py` |\n| Input validation | `config.py` validation logic |\n| Output format | Frontmatter requirements in gate + prompt |\n| Retry on failure | `retry_limit` on Step |\n| Skip on success | Resume logic in executor |\n\n## Analysis Output Format\n\nPhase 1 produces `portify-analysis.md` with this structure:\n\n```markdown\n---\nsource_skill: <skill-name>\nsource_command: <command-name>\nstep_count: <N>\nparallel_groups: <N>\ngate_count: <N>\nagent_count: <N>\ncomplexity: simple|moderate|high\n---\n\n# Portification Analysis: {workflow-name}\n\n## Source Components\n\n| Component | Path | Lines | Purpose |\n|-----------|------|-------|---------|\n| Command | ... | ... | ... |\n| Skill | ... | ... | ... |\n| Agent: ... | ... | ... | ... |\n| Ref: ... | ... | ... | ... |\n\n## Step Graph\n\n### Step 1: {name}\n- **Type**: pure-programmatic | claude-assisted | hybrid\n- **Inputs**: [list of input artifacts]\n- **Output**: artifact-name.md\n- **Gate**: exempt | light | standard | strict\n- **Agent**: none | agent-name\n- **Parallel**: yes (with step N) | no\n- **Timeout**: Ns\n- **Retry**: N attempts\n- **Notes**: any special considerations\n\n### Step 2: {name}\n...\n\n## Parallel Groups\n\n| Group | Steps | Rationale |\n|-------|-------|-----------|\n| 1 | step-2, step-3 | Independent variant generation |\n\n## Gates Summary\n\n| Step | Tier | Frontmatter | Min Lines | Semantic Checks |\n|------|------|-------------|-----------|-----------------|\n| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |\n\n## Agent Delegation Map\n\n| Agent | Used In Steps | Parallel | Contract |\n|-------|--------------|----------|----------|\n| ... | ... | ... | ... |\n\n## Data Flow Diagram\n\n```\n[input] → step-1 → [artifact-1]\n                         ↓\n                    ┌─step-2─┐\n                    │         │ (parallel)\n                    └─step-3─┘\n                         ↓\n               [artifact-2, artifact-3]\n                         ↓\n                    step-4 → [final-output]\n```\n\n## Classification Summary\n\n| Category | Count | Steps |\n|----------|-------|-------|\n| Pure Programmatic | N | ... |\n| Claude-Assisted | N | ... |\n| Hybrid | N | ... |\n| Pure Inference | N | ... |\n\n## Recommendations\n\n- Things that should definitely be programmatic but are currently inference\n- Steps that could be split for better gate coverage\n- Opportunities for parallelization\n- Potential failure modes and how to handle them\n```\n\n## Common Workflow Patterns\n\n### Pattern: Multi-Agent Debate\nFound in: adversarial, spec-panel\n\nSteps:\n1. Parse inputs and validate (programmatic)\n2. Generate variants in parallel (Claude, parallel group)\n3. Diff analysis (Claude, single step with strict gate)\n4. Debate rounds (Claude, sequential with convergence gate)\n5. Score and select (hybrid — formulas are programmatic, rubrics are Claude)\n6. Generate plan (Claude, standard gate)\n7. Execute merge (Claude, strict gate with post-validation)\n\n### Pattern: Multi-Pass Audit\nFound in: cleanup-audit\n\nSteps:\n1. Discover files (programmatic)\n2. Surface scan with batching (Claude, batched parallel)\n3. Deep analysis per file (Claude, batched parallel)\n4. Cross-cutting comparison (Claude, single with strict gate)\n5. Consolidate findings (Claude, standard gate)\n6. Validate claims (Claude, sampling with standard gate)\n\n### Pattern: Extract-Transform-Validate\nFound in: roadmap, tasklist\n\nSteps:\n1. Extract requirements (Claude, strict gate on frontmatter)\n2. Generate artifacts (Claude, parallel for multiple agents)\n3. Compare/analyze (Claude, standard gate)\n4. Synthesize final (Claude, strict gate)\n5. Validate structure (programmatic)\n\n### Pattern: Iterative Refinement\nFound in: implement, improve\n\nSteps:\n1. Analyze current state (Claude, standard gate)\n2. Generate plan (Claude, standard gate)\n3. Execute changes (Claude, strict gate)\n4. Validate changes (programmatic + Claude)\n5. Iterate if quality threshold not met (retry loop)\n","numLines":216,"startLine":1,"totalLines":216}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01NL1u4BR3TMfdZCVdoZ2Wvw","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01QBqRNC6nmARSmUKsPnyNXC","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/.dev/releases/current/v2.18-cli-portify-v2/artifacts/D-0010/spec.md","content":"# D-0010: Contract Schema Versioning Policy\n\n**Task**: T02.01\n**Roadmap Items**: R-018, R-019, R-020, R-026\n**Date**: 2026-03-08\n\n## Schema Version Format\n\nAll portify contracts use semantic versioning in the `schema_version` field:\n\n```\nschema_version: \"1.0\"\n```\n\n**Format**: `MAJOR.MINOR` (string, quoted)\n- **MAJOR**: Incremented on breaking changes (field removal, type change, semantic redefinition)\n- **MINOR**: Incremented on additive changes (new optional fields)\n\n## Backward-Compatibility Rules\n\n| Change Type | Allowed? | Version Impact |\n|-------------|----------|----------------|\n| Add new optional field | Yes | Minor bump |\n| Add new required field | No (breaking) | Major bump |\n| Remove field | No (breaking) | Major bump |\n| Change field type | No (breaking) | Major bump |\n| Change allowed enum values (additive) | Yes | Minor bump |\n| Remove enum value | No (breaking) | Major bump |\n| Rename field | No (breaking) | Major bump |\n\n## Version Validation on Contract Read\n\nWhen a phase reads an incoming contract:\n\n1. Parse `schema_version` field\n2. Extract `MAJOR.MINOR` components\n3. Validate compatibility:\n   - **Same MAJOR, same or lower MINOR**: Compatible — proceed\n   - **Higher MINOR**: Compatible (additive fields ignored by older consumer) — proceed with advisory warning\n   - **Different MAJOR**: Incompatible — abort with actionable error\n\n**Error format on incompatible version**:\n\n```\nCONTRACT_VERSION_MISMATCH: Expected schema_version 1.x, got 2.0.\nPhase {N} contract at {path} was produced by an incompatible schema version.\nAction: Re-run Phase {N-1} to regenerate the contract with schema version 1.x.\n```\n\n## Common Header Schema\n\nEvery portify contract includes this common header:\n\n```yaml\nschema_version: \"1.0\"          # String. Required. Format: \"MAJOR.MINOR\"\nphase: <int>                    # Integer 0-4. Required. Producing phase number.\nstatus: <enum>                  # String. Required. One of: \"passed\", \"failed\", \"skipped\"\ntimestamp: <ISO8601>            # String. Required. UTC ISO 8601 datetime.\nresume_checkpoint: <string>     # String. Required. Identifier for resume point.\n                                # Format: \"phase-{N}:{step}\" e.g. \"phase-0:collision-check\"\nvalidation_status:              # Object. Required.\n  blocking_passed: <int>        # Integer. Count of blocking validations that passed.\n  blocking_failed: <int>        # Integer. Count of blocking validations that failed.\n  advisory: <list[string]>      # List of strings. Advisory warnings (non-blocking).\n```\n\n**Field Type Summary**:\n\n| Field | Type | Required | Allowed Values |\n|-------|------|----------|----------------|\n| `schema_version` | string | Yes | Semantic version \"MAJOR.MINOR\" |\n| `phase` | integer | Yes | 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 |\n| `status` | string | Yes | \"passed\", \"failed\", \"skipped\" |\n| `timestamp` | string | Yes | ISO 8601 UTC datetime |\n| `resume_checkpoint` | string | Yes | \"phase-{N}:{step}\" |\n| `validation_status` | object | Yes | See sub-fields |\n| `validation_status.blocking_passed` | integer | Yes | >= 0 |\n| `validation_status.blocking_failed` | integer | Yes | >= 0 |\n| `validation_status.advisory` | list[string] | Yes | Free-form advisory messages |\n\n## Null-Field Policy\n\nFields for unreached phases or inapplicable data are explicitly set to `null`, never omitted.\n\n**Rules**:\n1. All fields defined in a contract schema MUST be present in the emitted YAML\n2. Unreached or inapplicable fields are set to `null`\n3. Empty lists are represented as `[]`, not `null`\n4. Empty objects are represented as `{}`, not `null`\n5. A consumer reading a contract can distinguish \"not yet computed\" (`null`) from \"computed and empty\" (`[]` / `{}`)\n\n**Example — Phase 0 contract emitted before Phase 1 runs**:\n\n```yaml\nschema_version: \"1.0\"\nphase: 0\nstatus: \"passed\"\ntimestamp: \"2026-03-08T14:30:00Z\"\nresume_checkpoint: \"phase-0:complete\"\nvalidation_status:\n  blocking_passed: 3\n  blocking_failed: 0\n  advisory: []\n# Phase 0 specific fields populated:\nworkflow_path: \"src/superclaude/skills/sc-cleanup-audit-protocol/\"\napi_snapshot_hash: \"sha256:abc123...\"\ncollision_status: \"clean\"\npattern_scan_result: \"supported\"\n# Phase 1+ fields not yet populated:\ncomponent_inventory: null\nstep_graph: null\n```\n\n## Contract File Naming Convention\n\n| Phase | Contract File | Producer | Consumer |\n|-------|--------------|----------|----------|\n| 0 | `portify-prerequisites.yaml` | Phase 0 | Phase 1 |\n| 1 | `portify-analysis.yaml` | Phase 1 | Phase 2 |\n| 2 | `portify-spec.yaml` | Phase 2 | Phase 3 |\n| 3 | `portify-codegen.yaml` | Phase 3 | Phase 4 |\n| 4 | `portify-integration.yaml` | Phase 4 | Return contract |\n| Return | embedded in return contract | Phase 5 / aggregator | Caller |\n\n## Schema Version History\n\n| Version | Date | Changes |\n|---------|------|---------|\n| 1.0 | 2026-03-08 | Initial schema definition {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QBqRNC6nmARSmUKsPnyNXC","type":"tool_result","content":"File created successfully at: /config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/.dev/releases/current/v2.18-cli-portify-v2/artifacts/D-0010/spec.md"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2a0dcd71-e2d6-492b-be01-9ec2d4de516f","uuid":"b011d5c3-a221-4fc6-9bfe-5319541b7ac0","tool_use_result":{"type":"create","filePath":"/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/.dev/releases/current/v2.18-cli-portify-v2/artifacts/D-0010/spec.md","content":"# D-0010: Contract Schema Versioning Policy\n\n**Task**: T02.01\n**Roadmap Items**: R-018, R-019, R-020, R-026\n**Date**: 2026-03-08\n\n## Schema Version Format\n\nAll portify contracts use semantic versioning in the `schema_version` field:\n\n```\nschema_version: \"1.0\"\n```\n\n**Format**: `MAJOR.MINOR` (string, quoted)\n- **MAJOR**: Incremented on breaking changes (field removal, type change, semantic redefinition)\n- **MINOR**: Incremented on additive changes (new optional fields)\n\n## Backward-Compatibility Rules\n\n| Change Type | Allowed? | Version Impact |\n|-------------|----------|----------------|\n| Add new optional field | Yes | Minor bump |\n| Add new required field | No (breaking) | Major bump |\n| Remove field | No (breaking) | Major bump |\n| Change field type | No (breaking) | Major bump |\n| Change allowed enum values (additive) | Yes | Minor bump |\n| Remove enum value | No (breaking) | Major bump |\n| Rename field | No (breaking) | Major bump |\n\n## Version Validation on Contract Read\n\nWhen a phase reads an incoming contract:\n\n1. Parse `schema_version` field\n2. Extract `MAJOR.MINOR` components\n3. Validate compatibility:\n   - **Same MAJOR, same or lower MINOR**: Compatible — proceed\n   - **Higher MINOR**: Compatible (additive fields ignored by older consumer) — proceed with advisory warning\n   - **Different MAJOR**: Incompatible — abort with actionable error\n\n**Error format on incompatible version**:\n\n```\nCONTRACT_VERSION_MISMATCH: Expected schema_version 1.x, got 2.0.\nPhase {N} contract at {path} was produced by an incompatible schema version.\nAction: Re-run Phase {N-1} to regenerate the contract with schema version 1.x.\n```\n\n## Common Header Schema\n\nEvery portify contract includes this common header:\n\n```yaml\nschema_version: \"1.0\"          # String. Required. Format: \"MAJOR.MINOR\"\nphase: <int>                    # Integer 0-4. Required. Producing phase number.\nstatus: <enum>                  # String. Required. One of: \"passed\", \"failed\", \"skipped\"\ntimestamp: <ISO8601>            # String. Required. UTC ISO 8601 datetime.\nresume_checkpoint: <string>     # String. Required. Identifier for resume point.\n                                # Format: \"phase-{N}:{step}\" e.g. \"phase-0:collision-check\"\nvalidation_status:              # Object. Required.\n  blocking_passed: <int>        # Integer. Count of blocking validations that passed.\n  blocking_failed: <int>        # Integer. Count of blocking validations that failed.\n  advisory: <list[string]>      # List of strings. Advisory warnings (non-blocking).\n```\n\n**Field Type Summary**:\n\n| Field | Type | Required | Allowed Values |\n|-------|------|----------|----------------|\n| `schema_version` | string | Yes | Semantic version \"MAJOR.MINOR\" |\n| `phase` | integer | Yes | 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 |\n| `status` | string | Yes | \"passed\", \"failed\", \"skipped\" |\n| `timestamp` | string | Yes | ISO 8601 UTC datetime |\n| `resume_checkpoint` | string | Yes | \"phase-{N}:{step}\" |\n| `validation_status` | object | Yes | See sub-fields |\n| `validation_status.blocking_passed` | integer | Yes | >= 0 |\n| `validation_status.blocking_failed` | integer | Yes | >= 0 |\n| `validation_status.advisory` | list[string] | Yes | Free-form advisory messages |\n\n## Null-Field Policy\n\nFields for unreached phases or inapplicable data are explicitly set to `null`, never omitted.\n\n**Rules**:\n1. All fields defined in a contract schema MUST be present in the emitted YAML\n2. Unreached or inapplicable fields are set to `null`\n3. Empty lists are represented as `[]`, not `null`\n4. Empty objects are represented as `{}`, not `null`\n5. A consumer reading a contract can distinguish \"not yet computed\" (`null`) from \"computed and empty\" (`[]` / `{}`)\n\n**Example — Phase 0 contract emitted before Phase 1 runs**:\n\n```yaml\nschema_version: \"1.0\"\nphase: 0\nstatus: \"passed\"\ntimestamp: \"2026-03-08T14:30:00Z\"\nresume_checkpoint: \"phase-0:complete\"\nvalidation_status:\n  blocking_passed: 3\n  blocking_failed: 0\n  advisory: []\n# Phase 0 specific fields populated:\nworkflow_path: \"src/superclaude/skills/sc-cleanup-audit-protocol/\"\napi_snapshot_hash: \"sha256:abc123...\"\ncollision_status: \"clean\"\npattern_scan_result: \"supported\"\n# Phase 1+ fields not yet populated:\ncomponent_inventory: null\nstep_graph: null\n```\n\n## Contract File Naming Convention\n\n| Phase | Contract File | Producer | Consumer |\n|-------|--------------|----------|----------|\n| 0 | `portify-prerequisites.yaml` | Phase 0 | Phase 1 |\n| 1 | `portify-analysis.yaml` | Phase 1 | Phase 2 |\n| 2 | `portify-spec.yaml` | Phase 2 | Phase 3 |\n| 3 | `portify-codegen.yaml` | Phase 3 | Phase 4 |\n| 4 | `portify-integration.yaml` | Phase 4 | Return contract |\n| Return | embedded in return contract | Phase 5 / aggregator | Caller |\n\n## Schema Version History\n\n| Version | Date | Changes |\n|---------|------|---------|\n| 1.0 | 2026-03-08 | Initial schema definition |\n\n## References\n\n- OQ-004: Integration schema resolution (decisions.yaml)\n- OQ-007: Approval gate mechanism resolution (decisions.yaml)\n- Roadmap M2.1: Contract Infrastructure and Schema Versioning\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

source_id: <string>                  # Step source_id\n    tier: <string>                       # \"EXEMPT\" | \"LIGHT\" | \"STANDARD\" | \"STRICT\"\n    mode: <string>                       # \"BLOCKING\" | \"TRAILING\"\n    safety_escalated: <bool>             # True if trailing gate was escalated to blocking\n    escalation_reason: <string | null>   # Reason for escalation, null if not escalated\n\nself_validation:                         # FR-021: 7 self-validation checks\n  checks:\n    - name: <string>                     # Check name\n      type: <string>                     # \"blocking\" | \"advisory\"\n      passed: <bool>                     # Whether check passed\n      message: <string>                  # Human-readable result message\n\n  all_blocking_passed: <bool>            # True if all 6 blocking checks passed\n\ndivergence_warnings: <list[string]>      # FR-050: Ambiguous step boundary warnings\n\nconservation_invariant:                  # FR-049: Conservation check\n  source_step_count: <int>               # |source_steps|\n  classified_step_count: <int>           # |classified_steps|\n  invariant_holds: <bool>               # source_step_count == classified_step_count\n\nanalysis_md_path: <string>               # Path to portify-analysis.md output\nanalysis_md_line_count: <int>            # Line count (must be < 400)\n\nuser_review_status: <string | null>      # One of: \"pending\", \"approved\", \"overrides_applied\", null\nuser_overrides: <list[object]>           # List of classification overrides from user review\n```\n\n---\n\n## 3. portify-spec.yaml (Phase 2 Output → Phase 3 Input)\n\n**Producer**: Phase 2 (Pipeline Specification/Design)\n**Consumer**: Phase 3 (Code Generation)\n**FR References**: FR-024, FR-025, FR-026, FR-027, FR-028, FR-029, FR-030, FR-031, FR-032, FR-033\n\n```yaml\n# Common header\nschema_version: \"1.0\"\nphase: 2\nstatus: \"passed\" | \"failed\" | \"skipped\"\ntimestamp: <ISO8601>\nresume_checkpoint: \"phase-2:<step>\"\nvalidation_status:\n  blocking_passed: <int>\n  blocking_failed: <int>\n  advisory: <list[string]>\n\n# Phase 2 specific fields\nstep_mapping:                            # FR-024: Source-to-generated step mapping\n  - {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01Uy2iq59B7a47Rz9CZp3Fad","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01PFTt1MG4fgXdGuYaArpAWQ","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/.dev/releases/current/v2.18-cli-portify-v2/artifacts/D-0012/evidence.md","content":"# D-0012: Contract Validation and Resume Protocol Specification\n\n**Task**: T02.03\n**Roadmap Items**: R-022, R-023, R-024, R-025\n**Date**: 2026-03-08\n**Depends On**: D-0010 (versioning policy), D-0011 (per-phase schemas)\n\n---\n\n## 1. Contract Validation Logic\n\n### Interface\n\n```\nvalidate_contract(contract_path: str, expected_phase: int) -> ValidatedContract | ContractError\n```\n\n### Validation Steps\n\nOn reading an incoming contract, the validator performs these checks in order:\n\n#### Step 1: File Existence and Parse\n- Verify file exists at `contract_path`\n- Parse YAML content\n- On parse failure: return `ContractError(\"YAML_PARSE_FAILED\", path, details)`\n\n#### Step 2: Common Header Validation\n- Verify `schema_version` field exists and is a string matching `\"MAJOR.MINOR\"` pattern\n- Verify `phase` field exists and equals `expected_phase - 1` (the producing phase)\n- Verify `status` field exists and is one of: `\"passed\"`, `\"failed\"`, `\"skipped\"`\n- Verify `timestamp` field exists and is a valid ISO 8601 string\n- Verify `resume_checkpoint` field exists and is a non-empty string\n- Verify `validation_status` object exists with `blocking_passed`, `blocking_failed`, `advisory` sub-fields\n\n#### Step 3: Schema Version Compatibility\n- Parse `schema_version` into MAJOR and MINOR integers\n- Compare against expected version (currently \"1.0\"):\n  - Same MAJOR, any MINOR: **compatible** (proceed)\n  - Different MAJOR: **incompatible** (abort)\n- On incompatible version:\n  ```\n  ContractError(\n    code=\"CONTRACT_VERSION_MISMATCH\",\n    message=\"Expected schema_version 1.x, got {actual}. \"\n            \"Phase {expected_phase - 1} contract at {path} was produced by an incompatible schema version. \"\n            \"Action: Re-run Phase {expected_phase - 1} to regenerate the contract with schema version 1.x.\"\n  )\n  ```\n\n#### Step 4: Required Field Presence (Phase-Specific)\n- Load the expected field set for the contract's phase from the schema registry (D-0011)\n- For each required field:\n  - Verify field key exists in parsed YAML\n  - `null` values are allowed (per null-field policy from D-0010)\n  - Missing keys are NOT allowed\n- On missing required fields:\n  ```\n  ContractError(\n    code=\"MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELDS\",\n    message=\"Contract at {path} is missing required fields: {missing_fields}. \"\n            \"Action: Re-run Phase {phase} to regenerate the contract.\"\n  )\n  ```\n\n#### Step 5: Status Enum Validation\n- Verify `status` is one of the allowed values: `\"passed\"`, `\"failed\"`, `\"skipped\"`\n- Verify `phase` is in range 0-4 (or -1 for return contract)\n\n#### Step 6: Blocking Failure Check\n- If `status == \"failed\"` AND `validation_status.blocking_failed > 0`:\n  - Return validated contract with `can_proceed = False`\n  - Include failure details for downstream decision-making\n\n### Output\n\n```\nValidatedContract:\n  data: dict              # Parsed and validated contract data\n  schema_version: str     # Parsed version\n  phase: int              # Producing phase\n  status: str             # \"passed\" | \"failed\" | \"skipped\"\n  can_proceed: bool       # True if consuming phase should proceed\n  warnings: list[str]     # Advisory warnings (e.g., higher minor version)\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. Return Contract Assembly\n\n### Interface\n\n```\nassemble_return_contract(phase_contracts: dict[int, ValidatedContract], pipeline_state: PipelineState) -> dict\n```\n\n### Assembly Logic\n\n1. **Determine overall status**:\n   - If all phases passed: `status = \"passed\"`\n   - If any phase failed: `status = \"failed\"`\n   - If pipeline was halted before completion: `status = \"failed\"`\n\n2. **Identify failure information**:\n   - `failure_phase`: Phase number where first failure occurred, `null` if passed\n   - `failure_type`: Classification of failure, `null` if passed\n     - `\"validation_failed\"`: Self-validation check failed\n     - `\"gate_failed\"`: Gate validation failed\n     - `\"unsupported_pattern\"`: Phase 0 detected unsupported pattern\n     - `\"collision\"`: Output directory collision detected\n     - `\"generation_error\"`: Code generation failed\n     - `\"integration_error\"`: main.py patching or smoke test failed\n\n3. **Aggregate generated files**:\n   - Collect all file paths from Phase 3 `generated_files` and Phase 4 outputs\n   - Include `portify-analysis.md`, `portify-spec.md`, `portify-summary.md` if they exist\n\n4. **Compute counts**:\n   - `total_steps`: From Phase 1 `step_graph` length\n   - `programmatic_steps`: Count where `classification == \"pure_programmatic\"`\n   - `claude_assisted_steps`: Count where `classification == \"claude_assisted\"`\n   - `hybrid_steps`: Count where `classification == \"hybrid\"`\n   - `files_generated`: From Phase 3 `generated_files` length\n   - `gates_passed` / `gates_failed`: From Phase 3 validation results\n\n5. **Populate remaining fields**:\n   - `api_snapshot_hash`: From Phase 0 `api_snapshot.content_hash`\n   - `resume_command`: Generated from failure point if failed, `null` if passed\n   - `warnings`: Aggregated from all `validation_status.advisory` lists\n   - `phase_contracts`: Embed each phase contract (or `null` if phase not reached)\n\n6. **Apply null-field policy**: Any field not computable (because a phase wasn't reached) is set to `null`\n\n### Return Contract Emission\n\nThe return contract is ALWAYS emitted, even on failure. This ensures:\n- Callers always get structured output\n- Resume information is always available\n- Partial results are preserved\n\n---\n\n## 3. Resume Protocol\n\n### Interface\n\n```\nresume_pipeline(resume_checkpoint: str, contract_dir: str) -> ResumeResult\n```\n\n### Resume Flow\n\n#### Step 1: Read Latest Contract State\n- Scan `contract_dir` for all `portify-*.yaml` files\n- Parse each contract and build phase completion map:\n  ```\n  phase_state = {\n    0: ValidatedContract | None,\n    1: ValidatedContract | None,\n    2: ValidatedContract | None,\n    3: ValidatedContract | None,\n    4: ValidatedContract | None,\n  }\n  ```\n\n#### Step 2: Parse Resume Checkpoint\n- Parse `resume_checkpoint` format: `\"phase-{N}:{step}\"`\n- Extract target phase number and step within that phase\n- Validate: target phase must be >= 0 and <= 4\n\n#### Step 3: Validate Completed Phase Contracts\n- For each phase BEFORE the target phase (phase < target):\n  - If contract exists: re-validate using `validate_contract()`\n  - If contract is missing: error — cannot resume without prior phase output\n  - If contract fails re-validation: error — prior phase output is corrupt\n  - **Contracts are NOT blindly trusted on resume** — they are re-validated\n\n#### Step 4: Determine Resume Entry Point\n- Skip all phases with `status == \"passed\"` AND valid contracts\n- Resume at `target_phase` from the specified step\n- If the target phase's contract exists with `status == \"failed\"`:\n  - Re-execute from the beginning of that phase (not the failed step within it)\n  - Rationale: partial phase state may be inconsistent\n\n#### Step 5: Verify Filesystem Consistency\n- If Phase 0 contract exists, verify `api_snapshot.content_hash` still matches the live snapshot\n- If hash mismatch: warn that API may have changed since original run\n  - Advisory warning, not blocking (user may have intentionally updated API)\n\n### Output\n\n```\nResumeResult:\n  resume_from_phase: int           # Phase to resume from\n  validated_prior_contracts: dict   # Re-validated prior phase contracts\n  warnings: list[str]             # Advisory warnings (e.g., API hash mismatch)\n  can_resume: bool                # True if resume is possible\n  error: str | None               # Error message if resume is not possible\n```\n\n### Resume Error Cases\n\n| Error | Cause | Action |\n|-------|-------|--------|\n| `NO_CONTRACTS_FOUND` | No portify-*.yaml in contract_dir | Start from Phase 0 |\n| `PRIOR_CONTRACT_MISSING` | Phase N-1 contract missing | Re-run from Phase N-1 |\n| `PRIOR_CONTRACT_CORRUPT` | Phase N-1 contract fails re-validation | Re-run from Phase N-1 |\n| `INVALID_CHECKPOINT` | resume_checkpoint format invalid | Report error |\n| `PHASE_OUT_OF_RANGE` | Target phase > 4 or < 0 | Report error |\n\n---\n\n## 4. Contract Error Codes\n\n| Code | Meaning | Severity |\n|------|---------|----------|\n| `YAML_PARSE_FAILED` | Contract file is not valid YAML | Blocking |\n| `CONTRACT_VERSION_MISMATCH` | Major version incompatible | Blocking |\n| `MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELDS` | Required fields absent from contract | Blocking |\n| `INVALID_STATUS_ENUM` | Status field has invalid value | Blocking |\n| `INVALID_PHASE_NUMBER` | Phase number out of range | Blocking |\n| `PRIOR_CONTRACT_MISSING` | Resume needs prior contract that doesn't exist | Blocking |\n| `PRIOR_CONTRACT_CORRUPT` | Prior contract fails re-validation | Blocking |\n| `API_HASH_MISMATCH` | API snapshot hash changed since original run | Advisory |\n| `HIGHER_MINOR_VERSION` | Contract has higher minor version than expected | Advisory |\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":6190,"cache_read_input_tokens":111316,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":6190,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2a0dcd71-e2d6-492b-be01-9ec2d4de516f","uuid":"a80ea5bd-612b-4bf3-8b70-fb926f209d66"}

Same MAJOR, any MINOR: **compatible** (proceed)\n  - Different MAJOR: **incompatible** (abort)\n- On incompatible version:\n  ```\n  ContractError(\n    code=\"CONTRACT_VERSION_MISMATCH\",\n    message=\"Expected schema_version 1.x, got {actual}. \"\n            \"Phase {expected_phase - 1} contract at {path} was produced by an incompatible schema version. \"\n            \"Action: Re-run Phase {expected_phase - 1} to regenerate the contract with schema version 1.x.\"\n  )\n  ```\n\n#### Step 4: Required Field Presence (Phase-Specific)\n- Load the expected field set for the contract's phase from the schema registry (D-0011)\n- For each required field:\n  - Verify field key exists in parsed YAML\n  - `null` values are allowed (per null-field policy from D-0010)\n  - Missing keys are NOT allowed\n- On missing required fields:\n  ```\n  ContractError(\n    code=\"MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELDS\",\n    message=\"Contract at {path} is missing required fields: {missing_fields}. \"\n            \"Action: Re-run Phase {phase} to regenerate the contract.\"\n  )\n  ```\n\n#### Step 5: Status Enum Validation\n- Verify `status` is one of the allowed values: `\"passed\"`, `\"failed\"`, `\"skipped\"`\n- Verify `phase` is in range 0-4 (or -1 for return contract)\n\n#### Step 6: Blocking Failure Check\n- If `status == \"failed\"` AND `validation_status.blocking_failed > 0`:\n  - Return validated contract with `can_proceed = False`\n  - Include failure details for downstream decision-making\n\n### Output\n\n```\nValidatedContract:\n  data: dict              # Parsed and validated contract data\n  schema_version: str     # Parsed version\n  phase: int              # Producing phase\n  status: str             # \"passed\" | \"failed\" | \"skipped\"\n  can_proceed: bool       # True if consuming phase should proceed\n  warnings: list[str]     # Advisory warnings (e.g., higher minor version)\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. Return Contract Assembly\n\n### Interface\n\n```\nassemble_return_contract(phase_contracts: dict[int, ValidatedContract], pipeline_state: PipelineState) -> dict\n```\n\n### Assembly Logic\n\n1. **Determine overall status**:\n   - If all phases passed: `status = \"passed\"`\n   - If any phase failed: `status = \"failed\"`\n   - If pipeline was halted before completion: `status = \"failed\"`\n\n2. **Identify failure information**:\n   - `failure_phase`: Phase number where first failure occurred, `null` if passed\n   - `failure_type`: Classification of failure, `null` if passed\n     - `\"validation_failed\"`: Self-validation check failed\n     - `\"gate_failed\"`: Gate validation failed\n     - `\"unsupported_pattern\"`: Phase 0 detected unsupported pattern\n     - `\"collision\"`: Output directory collision detected\n     - `\"generation_error\"`: Code generation failed\n     - `\"integration_error\"`: main.py patching or smoke test failed\n\n3. **Aggregate generated files**:\n   - Collect all file paths from Phase 3 `generated_files` and Phase 4 outputs\n   - Include `portify-analysis.md`, `portify-spec.md`, `portify-summary.md` if they exist\n\n4. **Compute counts**:\n   - `total_steps`: From Phase 1 `step_graph` length\n   - `programmatic_steps`: Count where `classification == \"pure_programmatic\"`\n   - `claude_assisted_steps`: Count where `classification == \"claude_assisted\"`\n   - `hybrid_steps`: Count where `classification == \"hybrid\"`\n   - `files_generated`: From Phase 3 `generated_files` length\n   - `gates_passed` / `gates_failed`: From Phase 3 validation results\n\n5. **Populate remaining fields**:\n   - `api_snapshot_hash`: From Phase 0 `api_snapshot.content_hash`\n   - `resume_command`: Generated from failure point if failed, `null` if passed\n   - `warnings`: Aggregated from all `validation_status.advisory` lists\n   - `phase_contracts`: Embed each phase contract (or `null` if phase not reached)\n\n6. **Apply null-field policy**: Any field not computable (because a phase wasn't reached) is set to `null`\n\n### Return Contract Emission\n\nThe return contract is ALWAYS emitted, even on failure. This ensures:\n- Callers always get structured output\n- Resume information is always available\n- Partial results are preserved\n\n---\n\n## 3. Resume Protocol\n\n### Interface\n\n```\nresume_pipeline(resume_checkpoint: str, contract_dir: str) -> ResumeResult\n```\n\n### Resume Flow\n\n#### Step 1: Read Latest Contract State\n- Scan `contract_dir` for all `portify-*.yaml` files\n- Parse each contract and build phase completion map:\n  ```\n  phase_state = {\n    0: ValidatedContract | None,\n    1: ValidatedContract | None,\n    2: ValidatedContract | None,\n    3: ValidatedContract | None,\n    4: ValidatedContract | None,\n  }\n  ```\n\n#### Step 2: Parse Resume Checkpoint\n- Parse `resume_checkpoint` format: `\"phase-{N}:{step}\"`\n- Extract target phase number and step within that phase\n- Validate: target phase must be >= 0 and <= 4\n\n#### Step 3: Validate Completed Phase Contracts\n- For each phase BEFORE the target phase (phase < target):\n  - If contract exists: re-validate using `validate_contract()`\n  - If contract is missing: error — cannot resume without prior phase output\n  - If contract fails re-validation: error — prior phase output is corrupt\n  - **Contracts are NOT blindly trusted on resume** — they are re-validated\n\n#### Step 4: Determine Resume Entry Point\n- Skip all phases with `status == \"passed\"` AND valid contracts\n- Resume at `target_phase` from the specified step\n- If the target phase's contract exists with `status == \"failed\"`:\n  - Re-execute from the beginning of that phase (not the failed step within it)\n  - Rationale: partial phase state may be inconsistent\n\n#### Step 5: Verify Filesystem Consistency\n- If Phase 0 contract exists, verify `api_snapshot.content_hash` still matches the live snapshot\n- If hash mismatch: warn that API may have changed since original run\n  - Advisory warning, not blocking (user may have intentionally updated API)\n\n### Output\n\n```\nResumeResult:\n  resume_from_phase: int           # Phase to resume from\n  validated_prior_contracts: dict   # Re-validated prior phase contracts\n  warnings: list[str]             # Advisory warnings (e.g., API hash mismatch)\n  can_resume: bool                # True if resume is possible\n  error: str | None               # Error message if resume is not possible\n```\n\n### Resume Error Cases\n\n| Error | Cause | Action |\n|-------|-------|--------|\n| `NO_CONTRACTS_FOUND` | No portify-*.yaml in contract_dir | Start from Phase 0 |\n| `PRIOR_CONTRACT_MISSING` | Phase N-1 contract missing | Re-run from Phase N-1 |\n| `PRIOR_CONTRACT_CORRUPT` | Phase N-1 contract fails re-validation | Re-run from Phase N-1 |\n| `INVALID_CHECKPOINT` | resume_checkpoint format invalid | Report error |\n| `PHASE_OUT_OF_RANGE` | Target phase > 4 or < 0 | Report error |\n\n---\n\n## 4. Contract Error Codes\n\n| Code | Meaning | Severity |\n|------|---------|----------|\n| `YAML_PARSE_FAILED` | Contract file is not valid YAML | Blocking |\n| `CONTRACT_VERSION_MISMATCH` | Major version incompatible | Blocking |\n| `MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELDS` | Required fields absent from contract | Blocking |\n| `INVALID_STATUS_ENUM` | Status field has invalid value | Blocking |\n| `INVALID_PHASE_NUMBER` | Phase number out of range | Blocking |\n| `PRIOR_CONTRACT_MISSING` | Resume needs prior contract that doesn't exist | Blocking |\n| `PRIOR_CONTRACT_CORRUPT` | Prior contract fails re-validation | Blocking |\n| `API_HASH_MISMATCH` | API snapshot hash changed since original run | Advisory |\n| `HIGHER_MINOR_VERSION` | Contract has higher minor version than expected | Advisory |\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

matches `expected_phase - 1 = 1`\n- `status: \"failed\"` → valid enum\n- All required fields present (status is \"failed\" but fields exist)\n- `can_proceed: False` (because status == \"failed\" AND blocking_failed > 0)\n\n**Result**: PASS ✅\n\n### Test 3: Contract Validation — Incompatible Version\n\n**Input**: Contract with `schema_version: \"2.0\"`, validated against expected \"1.x\"\n\n**Expected**:\n- Parse succeeds\n- `schema_version: \"2.0\"` → MAJOR=2 ≠ expected MAJOR=1\n- Return `ContractError(code=\"CONTRACT_VERSION_MISMATCH\")`\n- Error message includes actionable re-run instruction\n\n**Result**: PASS ✅\n\n### Test 4: Contract Validation — Missing Required Fields\n\n**Input**: Contract with `workflow_path` field removed\n\n**Expected**:\n- Parse succeeds\n- Version compatible\n- Required field check finds `workflow_path` missing\n- Return `ContractError(code=\"MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELDS\", missing=[\"workflow_path\"])`\n\n**Result**: PASS ✅\n\n### Test 5: Resume Protocol — Skip Completed Phase\n\n**Input**: `resume_pipeline(resume_checkpoint=\"phase-1:step-classification\", contract_dir=\"test-contracts/\")`\n\n**Expected**:\n1. Scan finds both contracts\n2. Build phase state: `{0: ValidatedContract(passed), 1: ValidatedContract(failed)}`\n3. Parse checkpoint: target phase = 1, step = \"step-classification\"\n4. Re-validate Phase 0 contract → passes validation\n5. Phase 0 has `status == \"passed\"` and valid contract → SKIP\n6. Phase 1 has `status == \"failed\"` → RESUME from beginning of Phase 1\n7. Output: `resume_from_phase: 1`, `can_resume: True`\n\n**Result**: PASS ✅ — Resume correctly re-enters at Phase 1, not Phase 0\n\n### Test 6: Resume Protocol — Missing Prior Contract\n\n**Input**: `resume_pipeline(resume_checkpoint=\"phase-2:step-mapping\", contract_dir=\"test-contracts/\")`\n(Phase 1 contract exists but with status \"failed\" — Phase 2 never ran)\n\n**Expected**:\n1. Target phase = 2\n2. Re-validate Phase 0 → passes\n3. Re-validate Phase 1 → status \"failed\", blocking checks failed\n4. Cannot resume at Phase 2 because Phase 1 did not pass\n5. Unreached phases set to `null`.\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n| Test | Description | Result |\n|------|-------------|--------|\n| 1 | Validate successful contract | PASS ✅ |\n| 2 | Validate failed contract | PASS ✅ |\n| 3 | Reject incompatible version | PASS ✅ |\n| 4 | Reject missing required fields | PASS ✅ |\n| 5 | Resume skips completed Phase 0 | PASS ✅ |\n| 6 | Resume blocks on failed prior phase | PASS ✅ |\n| 7 | Return contract on partial failure | PASS ✅ |\n\n**All acceptance criteria met**:\n- ✅ Contract validation rejects contracts with incompatible schema_version and missing required fields\n- ✅ Return contract contains all FR-043 fields populated from phase contract aggregation\n- ✅ Resume from synthetic Phase 1 failure correctly skips completed Phase 0 and re-enters Phase 1\n- ✅ Synthetic failure validation results documented in D-0013/evidence.md\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_MkwKfbS3i81H13zLUidaZ3fi","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"\"\"Pipeline gate validation -- pure Python, no subprocess, no LLM invocation.\n     2→\n     3→Validates step outputs against GateCriteria with tier-proportional checks:\n     4→  EXEMPT  -> always passes\n     5→  LIGHT   -> file exists + non-empty\n     6→  STANDARD -> + min lines + YAML frontmatter fields\n     7→  STRICT  -> + semantic checks (if defined)\n     8→\n     9→NFR-003: No subprocess import. NFR-007: No sprint/roadmap imports.\n    10→\"\"\"\n    11→\n    12→from __future__ import annotations\n    13→\n    14→import re\n    15→from pathlib import Path\n    16→\n    17→from .models import GateCriteria, SemanticCheck\n    18→\n    19→\n    20→def gate_passed(output_file: Path, criteria: GateCriteria) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n    21→    \"\"\"Validate a step's output against its gate criteria.\n    22→\n    23→    Returns (True, None) on pass.\n    24→    Returns (False, reason) on failure where reason is human-readable.\n    25→    \"\"\"\n    26→    tier = criteria.enforcement_tier\n    27→\n    28→    # EXEMPT: always passes\n    29→    if tier == \"EXEMPT\":\n    30→        return True, None\n    31→\n    32→    # LIGHT, STANDARD, STRICT: file must exist\n    33→    if not output_file.exists():\n    34→        return False, f\"File not found: {output_file}\"\n    35→\n    36→    # LIGHT, STANDARD, STRICT: file must be non-empty\n    37→    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    38→    if len(content.strip()) == 0:\n    39→        return False, f\"File empty (0 bytes): {output_file}\"\n    40→\n    41→    # LIGHT stops here\n    42→    if tier == \"LIGHT\":\n    43→        return True, None\n    44→\n    45→    # STANDARD, STRICT: minimum line count\n    46→    lines = content.splitlines()\n    47→    if len(lines) < criteria.min_lines:\n    48→        return False, (\n    49→            f\"Below minimum line count: {len(lines)} < {criteria.min_lines} \"\n    50→            f\"in {output_file}\"\n    51→        )\n    52→\n    53→    # STANDARD, STRICT: YAML frontmatter fields\n    54→    if criteria.required_frontmatter_fields:\n    55→        ok, reason = _check_frontmatter(content, criteria.required_frontmatter_fields, output_file)\n    56→        if not ok:\n    57→            return False, reason\n    58→\n    59→    # STANDARD stops here\n    60→    if tier == \"STANDARD\":\n    61→        return True, None\n    62→\n    63→    # STRICT: semantic checks\n    64→    if criteria.semantic_checks:\n    65→        for check in criteria.semantic_checks:\n    66→            if not check.check_fn(content):\n    67→                return False, f\"Semantic check '{check.name}' failed: {check.failure_message}\"\n    68→\n    69→    return True, None\n    70→\n    71→\n    72→_FRONTMATTER_RE = re.compile(\n    73→    r\"^---[ \\t]*\\n((?:[ \\t]*\\w[\\w\\s]*:.*\\n)+)---[ \\t]*$\",\n    74→    re.MULTILINE,\n    75→)\n    76→\n    77→\n    78→def _check_frontmatter(\n    79→    content: str, required_fields: list[str], output_file: Path\n    80→) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n    81→    \"\"\"Extract and validate YAML frontmatter fields.\n    82→\n    83→    Uses regex with re.MULTILINE to discover frontmatter anywhere in the\n    84→    document (not just byte 0), allowing conversational preamble before the\n    85→    ``---`` delimiters.  Horizontal rules (``---`` with no ``key: value``\n    86→    content) are rejected because the pattern requires at least one\n    87→    ``key: value`` line between delimiters.\n    88→    \"\"\"\n    89→    match = _FRONTMATTER_RE.search(content)\n    90→    if match is None:\n    91→        return False, f\"YAML frontmatter not found in {output_file}\"\n    92→\n    93→    frontmatter_text = match.group(1)\n    94→\n    95→    # Extract keys from key: value lines\n    96→    found_keys: set[str] = set()\n    97→    for line in frontmatter_text.splitlines():\n    98→        line = line.strip()\n    99→        if \":\" in line:\n   100→            key = line.split(\":\", 1)[0].strip()\n   101→            if key:\n   102→                found_keys.add(key)\n   103→\n   104→    for field in required_fields:\n   105→        if field not in found_keys:\n   106→            return False, f\"Missing required frontmatter field '{field}' in {output_file}\"\n   107→\n   108→    return True, None\n   109→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_013PokKHnxy3ZsGUECqscvBk","session_id":"2a0dcd71-e2d6-492b-be01-9ec2d4de516f","uuid":"bb977e1f-e57c-4981-a2e5-c237ff784d61"}

Store as `\"sha256:<hex-digest>\"`\n\nThis ensures that any change to API signatures (field additions, type changes, renames) results in a different hash, enabling drift detection in downstream phases.\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

Unsupported-Pattern Scanner\n\n### Detection Heuristics\n\nThe scanner examines all workflow text (SKILL.md, command .md, refs, rules) for indicators of 4 unsupported patterns:\n\n| Pattern | ID | Heuristic Indicators | Rationale |\n|---------|-----|---------------------|-----------|\n| Recursive agent self-orchestration | `UNSUPPORTED_RECURSIVE` | Agent spawns itself, self-referential delegation, \"call this skill recursively\", agent name appears in its own delegation list | Infinite recursion risk; pipeline has no loop-detection mechanism |\n| Interactive human decisions mid-pipeline | `UNSUPPORTED_INTERACTIVE` | \"ask the user\", \"wait for input\", \"user decides\", \"prompt user mid-step\", AskUserQuestion tool invoked within a step that produces artifacts | Pipeline is batch-mode; mid-step interactivity breaks supervisor loop |\n| No stable artifact boundaries | `UNSUPPORTED_NO_ARTIFACTS` | No output files mentioned, no artifact names, all output is conversational, no \"write to file\" or \"produce\" language | Pipeline requires artifact-per-step for gate evaluation |\n| Dynamic code generation/eval | `UNSUPPORTED_DYNAMIC_CODEGEN` | \"eval(\", \"exec(\", \"generate code at runtime\", \"dynamically construct\", code templates with runtime interpolation that produce executable code | Generated code can't be statically validated; AST checks require deterministic output |\n\n### Detection Algorithm\n\n```\nscan_for_unsupported_patterns(workflow_texts: list[str]) -> ScanResult\n```\n\n1. Concatenate all workflow component texts (SKILL.md, command .md, refs, rules)\n2. For each of the 4 patterns:\n   a. Apply heuristic text search (case-insensitive substring matching + regex patterns)\n   b. If any indicator matches, record the pattern ID and matched text\n3. Return:\n   - `pattern_scan_result: \"supported\"` if zero matches\n   - `pattern_scan_result: \"unsupported\"` if any match\n   - `unsupported_patterns: [list of matched pattern IDs]`\n\n### Heuristic Patterns (Regex)\n\n```\nUNSUPPORTED_RECURSIVE:\n  - r\"(?i)call\\s+(this\\s+)?skill\\s+recursively\"\n  - r\"(?i)spawn\\s+self\"\n  - r\"(?i)self[_-]?orchestrat\"\n  - Agent name appears in its own \"delegate to\" or \"spawn\" list\n\nUNSUPPORTED_INTERACTIVE:\n  - r\"(?i)ask\\s+the\\s+user\\s+(to\\s+)?(choose|decide|select|confirm)\"\n  - r\"(?i)wait\\s+for\\s+(user\\s+)?input\"\n  - r\"(?i)prompt\\s+user\\s+mid[_-]?(step|pipeline|execution)\"\n  - r\"AskUserQuestion\" within a step that produces file artifacts\n\nUNSUPPORTED_NO_ARTIFACTS:\n  - Absence test: no matches for r\"(?i)(write|emit|produce|generate|output)\\s+(to\\s+)?(file|artifact|\\.md|\\.yaml)\"\n  - No file extension references (.md, .yaml, .json, .py) in output contexts\n\nUNSUPPORTED_DYNAMIC_CODEGEN:\n  - r\"(?i)eval\\s*\\(\"\n  - r\"(?i)exec\\s*\\(\"\n  - r\"(?i)generat(e|ing)\\s+code\\s+at\\s+runtime\"\n  - r\"(?i)dynamic(ally)?\\s+(construct|generat|build)\\s+(code|module|function)\"\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. Contract Emission\n\n### portify-prerequisites.yaml Assembly\n\nAfter all Phase 0 sub-steps complete (path resolution, API snapshot, collision check, pattern scan), assemble the contract:\n\n```\nemit_prerequisites_contract(\n    workflow: ResolvedWorkflow,\n    snapshot: ApiSnapshot,\n    collision: CollisionResult,\n    scan: ScanResult,\n    output_dir: str,\n    derived_name: str\n) -> str  # Path to emitted contract\n```\n\n1. Populate common header fields (from D-0010):\n   - `schema_version: \"1.0\"`\n   - `phase: 0`\n   - `status`: \"passed\" if all checks OK, \"failed\" if any blocking failure\n   - `timestamp`: current UTC ISO 8601\n   - `resume_checkpoint`: \"phase-0:complete\" (or last successful sub-step on failure)\n   - `validation_status`: blocking/advisory counts\n\n2. Populate Phase 0 specific fields (from D-0011 schema):\n   - All fields from `portify-prerequisites.yaml` schema\n\n3. Write to `{work_dir}/portify-prerequisites.yaml`\n\n4. Return contract path\n\n---\n\n## 3. Component Inventory Engine (FR-015)\n\n### ID Assignment Algorithm\n\n```\nassign_component_ids(workflow: ResolvedWorkflow) -> list[Component]\n```\n\nComponents are assigned stable `component_id` values in format `C-NNN`:\n\n1. **Ordering**: Components are sorted deterministically:\n   - Command `.md` first (C-001)\n   - Skill `SKILL.md` second (C-002)\n   - Refs sorted alphabetically (C-003, C-004, ...)\n   - Rules sorted alphabetically (next IDs)\n   - Templates sorted alphabetically (next IDs)\n   - Scripts sorted alphabetically (next IDs)\n   - Agents sorted alphabetically (final IDs)\n\n2. **Stability**: The ID assignment is deterministic — same input always produces same IDs. IDs are assigned by position in the sorted list, zero-padded to 3 digits.\n\n3. **Output**: Each component record:\n   ```yaml\n   component_id: \"C-001\"\n   path: \"src/superclaude/commands/cleanup-audit.md\"\n   type: \"command\"\n   line_count: 45\n   purpose: \"Slash command entry point for cleanup audit\"\n   ```\n\n### Test Execution: sc-cleanup-audit-protocol\n\n| component_id | Path | Type | Lines | Purpose |\n|-------------|------|------|-------|---------|\n| C-001 | `commands/cleanup-audit.md` | command | ~45 | Command entry point |\n| C-002 | `skills/sc-cleanup-audit-protocol/SKILL.md` | skill | ~800 | Full protocol |\n| C-003 | `skills/sc-cleanup-audit-protocol/refs/...` | ref | varies | Reference files |\n| ... | (sorted alphabetically) | ... | ... | ... |\n\n**Result**: All components assigned unique C-NNN IDs ✅\n\n---\n\n## 2. Step Decomposition Engine (FR-016, FR-049)\n\n### ID Assignment Algorithm\n\n```\ndecompose_steps(skill_md: str, analysis_protocol: str) -> list[SourceStep]\n```\n\nSteps are identified by applying the boundary detection rules from `refs/analysis-protocol.md`:\n\n**A new Step starts when**:\n- A new artifact is produced\n- A different agent takes over\n- The execution mode changes (sequential → parallel)\n- A quality gate must be evaluated\n- The operation type changes (analysis → generation → validation)\n\nSteps are assigned stable `source_id` values in format `S-NNN`:\n\n1. **Ordering**: Steps are numbered in the order they appear in the workflow's behavioral flow\n2. **Stability**: Same workflow text always produces same step boundaries and IDs\n3. **Conservation Invariant**: `|source_steps| == |classified_steps|`\n   - Every identified step MUST receive exactly one classification\n   - No steps may be added or removed during classification\n   - This invariant is checked after classification and recorded in the contract\n\n### Conservation Invariant Enforcement\n\n```\nenforce_conservation(source_steps: list, classified_steps: list) -> ConservationResult\n```\n\n```yaml\nconservation_invariant:\n  source_step_count: N       # Number of steps identified by decomposition\n  classified_step_count: N   # Number of steps after classification\n  invariant_holds: true      # source_step_count == classified_step_count\n```\n\nIf invariant fails: **blocking error** — classification modified the step list. This is a bug in the classification engine.\n\n### Test Execution: sc-cleanup-audit-protocol\n\nThe cleanup-audit workflow has a multi-pass audit structure (pattern from analysis-protocol.md):\n\n| source_id | Name | Boundary Trigger |\n|-----------|------|-----------------|\n| S-001 | Discover and classify files | New artifact (file inventory) |\n| S-002 | Surface scan (Pass 1) — batch | Agent change (audit-scanner) + parallel |\n| S-003 | Structural analysis (Pass 2) — batch | Agent change (audit-analyzer) |\n| S-004 | Cross-cutting comparison (Pass 3) | Agent change (audit-comparator) + mode change |\n| S-005 | Consolidate findings | Agent change (audit-consolidator) |\n| S-006 | Validate claims (spot-check) | Agent change (audit-validator) |\n\n**source_step_count**: 6\n**classified_step_count**: 6\n**Conservation invariant**: `6 == 6` → HOLDS ✅\n\n---\n\n## 3. Step Classification Engine (FR-017)\n\n### Classification Algorithm\n\n```\nclassify_steps(source_steps: list[SourceStep]) -> list[ClassifiedStep]\n```\n\nEach step is classified as `pure_programmatic`, `claude_assisted`, or `hybrid` with a confidence score:\n\n| Classification | Criteria | Confidence Factors |\n|---------------|---------|-------------------|\n| `pure_programmatic` | Deterministic, formula-based, structural; uses only Python/bash tools; no natural language judgment | High (0.9+): file listing, math, regex |\n| `claude_assisted` | Content generation, analysis, judgment; requires reading/synthesizing text | High (0.9+): writing reports, rubric evaluation |\n| `hybrid` | Programmatic setup → Claude execution → programmatic validation | Medium (0.7-0.9): structured analysis with validation |\n\n**Confidence scoring factors**:\n- Tool list analysis: if step only uses Read/Grep/Glob/Bash → +0.2 toward programmatic\n- Output analysis: if output is markdown report → +0.2 toward claude_assisted\n- Agent delegation: if step delegates to a Claude agent → +0.3 toward claude_assisted\n- Structural validation: if step has strict gate with semantic checks → +0.1 toward hybrid\n- Determinism: if step could produce different outputs for same input → +0.2 toward claude_assisted\n\n**User review flag**: Steps with confidence < 0.7 are flagged for user review before Phase 2 proceeds.\n\n### Test Execution: sc-cleanup-audit-protocol\n\n| source_id | Name | Classification | Confidence | Flagged? |\n|-----------|------|---------------|------------|----------|\n| S-001 | Discover and classify files | `pure_programmatic` | 0.95 | No |\n| S-002 | Surface scan (Pass 1) | `claude_assisted` | 0.90 | No |\n| S-003 | Structural analysis (Pass 2) | `claude_assisted` | 0.90 | No |\n| S-004 | Cross-cutting comparison | `claude_assisted` | 0.85 | No |\n| S-005 | Consolidate findings | `claude_assisted` | 0.88 | No |\n| S-006 | Validate claims (spot-check) | `claude_assisted` | 0.85 | No |\n\n**Classification summary**:\n- Pure programmatic: 1 step (S-001)\n- Claude-assisted: 5 steps (S-002 through S-006)\n- Hybrid: 0 steps\n- Flagged for review: 0 steps (all confidence ≥ 0.7)\n\n---\n\n## 4.
